SUMMARY  
 Today I will summarise what we have covered previously. We will start Highest Yoga Tantra in the next lesson.  
 
Since we are studying Tantra, it is extremely important to know the diff erences between Sutra 
and Tantra. This is something that you must keep in mind all the time. How do we distinguish 
Tantra from Sutra? The distinction is made by either the absence or presence of a meditation 
that accords with the aspects of the four comple te purities that exist at the time of the result. 
The four complete purities are:  
1. the Buddha’s form  
2. the Buddha’s abode  
3. the Buddha’s enjoyments  
4. the Buddha’s activities  
These four things exist on the buddha ground.  
 Tantra is the practice of the path which is the meditation on the aspects that accord with these 
four complete purities at the resultant stage. The texts that teach such a path are Tantric scriptures. The Sutras do not teach such a path.  
 How is such a meditation done? In Tantric practice,  
• one visualises oneself as the deity  
• one visualises one’s environment as the inestimable mansion of the deity  
• one purifies the offerings into emptiness and from that state of emptiness,   the offerings 
are generated as pure divine substances which one visualises oneself partaking  
• one practises the emanation and withdrawal of light rays, working for the benefit of  
sentient beings and so forth  
These are the meditations that accord with the aspects of the four purities. These are Tantric 
practices which do not exist in the Sutric vehicle. This is the reason why Tantra is sometimes 
called the resultant vehicle becaus e one can meditate on the aspects of the resultant stage (in 
 
Lesson  21          Page 135 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
the present time).   
 
Tantra has four divisions:  
1. Action Tantra  
2. Performance tantra  
3. Yoga Tantra  
4. Highest Yoga Tantra  
Why is this so?  All four classes of Tantra teach the meditations that accord wit h the aspects of 
the four complete purities of the result. They are the same in this regard.  
 One of the differences between Sutra and Tantra is that Tantra is the practice of taking the desire into the path. The four classes of Tantra are differentiated by the way in which this is done:   
• In Action Tantra, one takes into the path the desire that arises from looking at the visualised goddess.  
• In Performance Tantra, the practice becomes subtler, i.e., one takes into the path the 
desire that arises from smiling at the visualised goddess.  
• In Yoga Tantra, in addition to what is mentioned above, one takes into the path the desire 
that arises from holding hands with the visualised goddess.  
• In Highest Yoga Tantra, the practitioner has the ability to take into the path  the desire that 
arises from the union of the two sexual organs.  
 One can also see how there is a difference in the way the practice is done by looking at the names of the four classes of Tantra.  
• Action Tantra is so called because there is an emphasis on external activities versus the internal activity of meditative stabilisation.  
• In Performance Tantra, both external and internal activities are balanced.  
• In Yoga Tantra, the emphasis is on internal activity.  
• In Highest Yoga Tantra, internal activity  is most important. That is why it is called Highest 
Yoga Tantra or literally, the unsurpassed Tantra.  
So, these are the reasons for the four classes of Tantra.  
 Both Action and Performance Tantras are divided into three lineages:  
1. the Tathagata lineage  
2. the Lotus lineage  
3. the Vajra lineage  
 In general, there are five lineages but here, only three are mentioned. The other two lineages are subsumed into the three lineages.  
 There are so many deities in these two classes of Tantra. How does one identify the l ineage of a 
particular deity? One way to do this is by looking at the lord of the lineage, which is mentioned in the sadhana, the ritual text of the particular deity. For example:  
• in the text of Chenrezig’s practice, there will a line that mentions, “…are crowned by Amitabha.” This indicates that Chenrezig belongs to the Lotus lineage.  
 
Lesson  21          Page 136 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• Manjushri’s practice belongs to the Tathagata lineage because the sadhana mentions he is 
crowned by Vairochana.  
• In the sadhana of the practice of Vajrapani, it is mentioned  he is crowned by Akshobhya, 
which shows that Vajrapani  belongs to the Vajra lineage.  
 In order for one to do the practice of Action Tantra, one has to undertake the approximation (sometimes translated as retreat) of the Action Tantric deity. (The word “ approximate” means 
to get closer, i.e., to get closer to the deity and to the deity’s enlightenment). Before one can do that, one has to receive the initiation of that deity. When one receives the initiation, one has to protect the bodhisattva vows. In Action Tantra, tantric vows are not taken.  
 In order to engage in the approximation of the Action Tantric deity, there is the practice of 
meditating on the aspects of the four complete purities of the resultant stage. One generates 
oneself as the deity (self- generation) through the process known as the six deities. One then 
visualises, in the space in front, the inestimable mansion within which is the front generation deity. On this basis, one engages in the practice of repetition called the four branches of repetition.  
 The four branches of repetition are:  
1. the Self basis  
2. the Other basis  
3. the Mind basis  
4. the Sound basis  
In order to perform the repetition:  
• one generates oneself as deity (self -generation)  
• one generates the deity in front (front generation)  
• one visualises a moon disc at the heart of oneself as deity and at the heart of the front 
generation  
• one visualises the mantra garland of the deity on both these moon discs  
When these four branches are completed, one then engages in the repetition.  
 The pract ice of approximation can be divided into (1) the concentration with repetition and (2) 
the concentration without repetition.  
 The concentration without repetition has three parts:  
1. the concentration abiding in fire  
2. the concentration abiding in sound  
3. the co ncentration bestowing liberation at the end of sound  
In order to develop calm abiding, one meditates on the concentration abiding in fire and the concentration abiding in sound. One achieves calm abiding at the end of the concentration abiding in sound. On e achieves the concentration bestowing liberation at the end of sound 
when one achieves the special insight focusing on emptiness.  
 In the case of Action Tantra, the whole path is subsumed into the four yogas, all of which function to bring one closer to the deity:  
 
Lesson  21          Page 137 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
1. Deity yoga -  one meditates on oneself as the deity  
2. Empty yoga -  one meditates on emptiness  
3. Wind yoga -  one practises restraining the life -force exertion which is the practice of 
stopping the movement of the wind energy as one engages in the co ncentrations.   
4.  Repetition yoga -  one recites the mantra of the deity  
 
When these four yogas are condensed, they can be subsumed into two yogas:  
(1) the conventional deity yoga and (2) the ultimate deity yoga. There are these two yogas because the final objects of attainments are the two bodies: (1) the conventional body of the Buddha and (2) the ultimate body of the Buddha. The wind yoga can be subsumed into both  
conventional and ultimate deity yoga while repetition yoga can be subsumed into conventio nal 
deity yoga.  
 Another essential point of Tantra that one must understand is the inseparability of method and wisdom. In the context of Action Tantra, the path is divided into two:  
1. Yoga with Signs  
2. Yoga without Signs  
In Action Tantra, we talk about the non -dual yoga of profundity and clarity which basically 
refers to the Yoga without Signs. The non- dual yoga of profundity and clarity is extremely 
important in Action Tantra because according to the path of Action Tantra , this is the main 
cause of achieving enlightenment. Here, profundity refers to emptiness and clarity refers to the 
clear appearance of oneself as the deity.  They are unified and are to found in one mind. It is a 
mind that while appearing in the form of t he deity, at the same time ascertains its emptiness.   
 
What is the state of enlightenment? At the buddha ground (enlightenment), the Buddha’s body and mind are in one inseparable entity. Therefore in order to attain this state, at the time of the cause, o ne must have a cause that has both method and wisdom unified as one. This is 
another essential point that one must understand. Such a cause, i.e., a path that is the inseparability of method and wisdom unified in one mind, is only taught in Tantra, not in Sutra. This makes Tantra more profound than Sutra because in Tantra, there is one mind that unifies both method and wisdom. Tantra teaches a more profound cause for achieving the Buddha’s body. You must keep this point in mind. Tantra is superior to Sutra because it has the profound method of actualising the Buddha’s holy body.  
  
With respect to the non -dual yoga of profundity and clarity, there is (1) the non- dual yoga of 
profundity and clarity that is conceptual consciousness and (2) the non -dual yoga of profundity 
and clarity which is non -conceptual consciousness. For the non- dual yoga of profundity and 
clarity which is conceptual consciousness, the conventional deity’s body appears to that 
consciousness and that consciousness also realises emptiness. Whe n that consciousness 
transforms into a non- conceptual consciousness, i.e., it becomes a direct perceiver, that 
consciousness is still a non -dual yoga of profundity and clarity but the deity’s body does not 
appear to that consciousness. Put in another way: If it is a deity yoga, the deity’s holy body does not necessarily have to appear to that consciousness.  For example, (when we talk about the four yogas), empty deity is not form.  
 
 
Lesson  21          Page 138 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
In the Perfection vehicle (Mahayana Sutra), without any doubt, method and w isdom are very 
important. Wisdom here refers to the wisdom directly realising emptiness which is the antidote 
to the obstructions to liberation and the obstructions to omniscience. In the Perfection vehicle, what is it that makes this wisdom directly realising emptiness powerful?  
 It is bodhicitta together with the (practice of the six) perfections. In the Perfection vehicle, one engages in limitless ways of practicing these perfections for a very long time and these 
practices make the wisdom directly realising emptiness so powerful that it can overcome the 
obstructions to liberation and the obstructions to omniscience. According to Mahayana Sutra, this is how one achieves enlightenment.  
  
In the three lower classes of Tantra (Action, Performance and Yoga Tantra), it is the non -dual 
yoga of profundity and clarity that makes one achieve enlightenment.  
 One has to say that the Tantric path is quicker than the Sutric path. What makes the three lower classes of Tantra a quicker path than the Sutric path? That is where the attainments (siddhis) come in. One engages in approximation and actualises the common attainments. They come to a point when through one’s practice, one is able to perceive the Buddha and bodhisattvas directly and receive directly their blessings. This is what makes the non -dual yoga 
of profundity and clarity powerful. What makes the wisdom directly realising emptiness powerful in Highest Yoga Tantra? It is the bliss that arises from the union of the two sexual 
organs. That bliss results in one ’s mind becoming very subtle and one uses that subtle mind to 
realise emptiness. This subtle mind realising emptiness makes the Highest Yoga Tantric path 
very quick and powerful in overcoming delusions.  
 
In the three lower classes of Tantra, one newly att ains enlightenment in Akanishta. But 
according to Highest Yoga Tantra, this is not necessary as one can achieve enlightenment in the desire realm in one body and in one life - time.  
    Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme

LESSON NO: 22   DATE: 25TH JUNE 2009  
 
When we recite with our speech the Praise to Shakyamuni Buddha at the beginning of the class, 
we should visualise Guru Shakyamuni Buddha and put our palms together. We can put our 
bodies, speech and minds to (good) use during these 10 to 15 minutes of our preliminary 
prayers before class. We will be able to accumulate an immeasurable amount of merit in a very 
short time. This is something we should always remember.  
 
It is the same when one makes the mandala offering to one’s guru with whom one has a 
Dharma  connection. It is said that if one visualises one’s guru as inseparable from the Buddha 
when one makes the mandala offering, one will accumulate an immeasurable amount of merit. 
(Alternatively), one can offer the mandala by simply visualising Shakyamuni B uddha. All these 
are opportunities to accumulate merit.  
 
There are some people who do not hold Guru Shakyamuni Buddha in high regard, thinking that 
he does not have as much power as certain tantric deities such as Tara, Vajrayogini, Guhyasamaja or Yamantaka. They think tantric deities are more powerful than Shakyamuni Buddha. If one thinks in such a way, then this is a big mistake. This shows that these people don’t have any real understanding of what the state of enlightenment is.  
 
The very famous practice of Guhyasamaja, the practice that we will study soon, was actually 
manifested by Guru Shakyamuni Buddha. Deities like Guhyasamaja are the manifestations of Buddha Shakyamuni. Since that is the case, these deities are inseparable from Guru 
Shakyamuni Bud dha. Therefore, (one should) never entertain any doubts by thinking that 
praising  and making prostrations to Shakyamuni Buddha have no benefits. Never ever think like that.  
 
When we are doing these prayers in class, if we are able to do them well with sincerity, 
definitely we will rejoice and feel happy. We understand that even in a short 10, 15 minutes, we can accumulate so much merit.  
 
This happiness which comes from inside, within our minds, in our hearts, is something that only 
we can create for ours elves. It is never going to come from external  factors. It depends 
basically on our mental perspective. By adopting a certain mental outlook, we experience 
happiness in our hearts. By  adopting another kind of mental perspective, we create problems 
and un happiness for ourselves. Therefore, it is said that everything depends on our minds and 
how we think.  
 There is a Tibetan proverb which says, “A needle can make someone happy; mere words can make someone unhappy.” The proverb is telling us that the human mind is very changeable. From one point of view, the mind is very variable as the mind can be changed by  the slightest 
thing. Yet from another angle, the mind seems to be very hard and difficult to change.  
 
Lesson  22          Page 140 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Basically when applied to our situation here, whether you feel happy coming to class or not depends on your mental perspective. All of us have received,  many times, teachings on the 
Lam Rim , the stages of the path to enlightenment. When we look at the Lam Rim , in the 
beginning, there is the meditation on the human life of leisures and endowments, the perfect human rebirth. The whole point of doing this me ditation is to instil confidence and courage in 
our minds. It is to instil a sense of joy and happiness because without this confidence and joy, 
we will not be able to achieve any of our spiritual goals.  
 
We have the opportunity to receive the (tantric) i nitiations and we have the interest to study 
the tantric teachings and put them into practice. We should realise how fortunate and lucky we are to be in such a situation. The point is to think about positive things, about those things that will encourage u s to continue our studies. As much as possible, we should eliminate those  
thoughts that distract or hinder us from coming to class and (applying ourselves to) our studies.  
 We should always remember the positive side of things, the good things, the favourable conditions we have rather than thinking of the negative. We shouldn’t be putting ourselves down. Rather we should think about those positive things -  our good qualities, the good things 
about ourselves - that will uplift the mind and instil a sense o f confidence and determination in 
us. This is what we need. It is not for the sake of developing pride. If pride arises as a result of this way of thinking, then that is a mistake. That is wrong.

HIGHEST YOGA TANTRA  
 
2 The way to progress on the path of the Highest Yoga Tantra  
has two (parts):  
1 The general system of Highest (Yoga Tantra)  
2 The system of Kalachakra.  
 
2.1 THE GENERAL SYSTEM O F HIGHEST (YOGA TANT RA) 
The system of the general Highest (Yoga Tantra) has four:  
1 Becoming a vessel suitable to meditate on the path 
2 Keeping the vows and commitments pure  
3 How to practice the path  
4 The way to manifest the results of practice  
 
2.1.1  BECOMING A VESSEL SU ITABLE TO MEDITATE O N THE PATH  
One becomes a vessel suitable for meditating on the path, through entering any suitable mandala of Highest (Yoga Tantra) explained (bshad) from within the authentic divisions of 
tantra (rgyud sde) and elucidated (bkral) by a qualified (tshad Idan) maha siddha (drub chen), 
 
Lesson  22          Page 141 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
and by obtaining empowerment. Further, from the perspective of fully (rdzogs par) practicing 
of the path, one must obtain the fourth empowerment. ( Pg. 18 of the root text ) 
The system of Kalachakra is a Highest Yoga Tantra but it is treated  as a separate section 
because it has a slightly different way of positing the paths and practising the stages of the paths.  
 The text says, “ From the perspective of fully practising the path, one must obtain the fourth 
empowerment ,”  but, in general, to practise the entire path of Highest Yoga Tantra, i.e., both 
the generation and completion stages, we  need to receive four complete initiations which are:  
1. the vase empowerment  
2. the secret empowerment  
3. the wisdom knowledge empowerment and  
4. the wo rd empowerment  
 In general, there are four (types of mandalas). Regarding the mandala where one obtain the vase empowerment (bum pa’i dbang) there are three mandalas: (1) the printed, (2) the coloured particle and (3) the body mandala, and for some special disciples (gdul bya) there is 
also (4) the absorption/ concentration mandala (bsam gtan dkyil ‘khor). (Pg. 18 of the root text )  
 “For some special disciples there is also the absorption/concentration mandala”:  For disciples 
who have already attained stab le concentration, the vase initiation can be conferred using this 
absorption mandala.  
 
(Interpreter ’s comment : There seems to be a big chunk of words missing from this translation).  
 Amongst the four types of mandalas, there is a mandala known as the body  mandala.  
 
 Initiation into the 
body mandala of the 
deity  The practice of the  
the deity has the body 
mandala practice  
Practice of Heruka Chakrasamvara of 
the Ghantopada tradition  Yes Yes 
Practice of Yamantaka  No No 
Practice of Guhyasamaja  No Yes 
 I believe I have talked about the meaning of the body mandala before. The practice of the body 
mandala is not simply visualising some deities on  certain parts of the body but rather it is a practice where we  visualise certain parts of the body generated in to the entity of the deity. 
You may wonder why a particular deity has the body mandala practice while others do not and why there are some deities that has the body mandala practice but there isn’t an initiation into the body mandala of the deity. I think it is quite difficult to give you an answer. I don’t know why.  
 Geshe -la: If any one of you has an answer to this, let me know!  
 
 
Lesson  22          Page 142 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
There are four types of mandalas where we attain the vase initiation. Which mandalas are used 
to confer the three higher initia tions (the secret, wisdom knowledge and the word initiations)?  
• The secret initiation is conferred in the conventional bodhicitta mandala. One obtains the 
secret initiation by experiencing or tasting the bodhicitta of the guru’s father and mother.  
• The wisd om knowledge initiation is conferred in the bhaga mandala. The bhaga mandala 
refers to the secret place of the mother.  
• The word initiation is conferred in the ultimate bodhicitta mandala. The initiation is conferred through introducing the disciples to the meaning of the unification of no more 
learning. I guess one can say that the ultimate bodhicitta mandala refers to the unification of no more learning.  
 
Type of initiation  Type of mandala in which the initiation is conferred  
Vase  (1) Printed mandala (2) Coloured particle mandala (3) Body mandala (4) 
Absorption mandala (only for special disciples)  
Secret  Conventional bodhicitta mandala  
Wisdom 
knowledge  Bhaga mandala  
Word  Ultimate bodhicitta mandala  
 The mandala does not necessarily refer to the sand mandala or the cloth or painted mandala inside the mandala house. (As I have mentioned before) the meaning of mandala is to take the essence.  
 
In order to be a suitable vessel to meditate on the Highest Y oga Tantric path in its entirety, i.e., 
to practise the generation and completion stages, one must receive the four complete 
initiations. When one obtains the initiation of Highest Yoga Tantra, there are many vows and commitments that one takes after which  one has to keep them purely.  
 2.1.2 KEEPING THE VOWS AND  COMMITMENTS PURE  
If one meditates on the path with effort to guard (srung ba) the commitments and vows taken (kas blangs pa’i dam tshig dan sdom pa) at the time of the empowerment, one establishes the actual siddhis. Though one makes exertion (‘bad) for many aeons yet neglects the vows to be 
guarded (siting sdom) not only will one not establish the siddhis, it is stated that even a mere 
good migration will be hard to find.  
Therefore, having understoo d the advice given immediately on becoming a vessel through the 
empowerment, one needs to guard commitments and vows at the risk of one’s life (srog dang bsdos).(Pg. 18 of the root text)  
 “Immediately after becoming a vessel through the empowerment” means that immediately 
after receiving the initiation, one must know and guard all the vows and commitments that one 
has undertaken and one must know what advice was given during the initiation. In order to guard those vows and commitments, one  must first under stand and know what they are. 
Without knowing what the vows and commitments are, how is one going to keep those vows 
 
Lesson  22          Page 143 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
and commitments purely? From this point alone, one can understand the importance of study 
in order to achieve enlightenment.  
Especially, one should make strong exertion not to be affected (gos) by root downfalls and 
make exertion not to be affected by other downfalls. However, if they occur one should restore them by confession (bshags sdom) according to the rite. That is becaus e it is stated that one 
will establish the supreme (state) within seven or sixteen lives though one does not meditate on the path if one guards oneself from being affected by downfalls having obtained complete 
empowerment. It is stated that one will fall t o the bad migrations however much exertion one 
makes in other (practices) though neglecting the vows to be guarded. (Pg. 18 -19 of the root 
text)  
This is a very important point. It is not sufficient just to take the initiation but after having done 
so, one must protect all the vows and commitments. If one fails to do this but instead puts one’s effort into this or that practice, one can forget about achieving realisations or attainments because even getting a good rebirth will be difficult.  
These should be extensively known from the Explanation of the Fifty Verses of Guru (Devotion) and the explanation of the root downfalls and The Cluster of Siddhis (dngos grub snye ma). (pg. 19 of the root text )  
(Interpreter ’s correction: These should be extensively known  from the Explanation of the Fifty 
Verses of Guru (Devotion) and The Cluster of Siddhis (dngos grub snye ma) which is the explanation of the root downfalls ). 
 
It said that we should study these texts, The Explanation of the 50 Verses of Guru Devotion (and the Cluster of Siddhis ) which is the commentary on the root downfalls and commitments, both 
of which are available in English.  
 When one receives the Highest Yoga Tantric initiation, one takes the bodhisattva vows and tantric vows. The tantric vows include  the individual commitments of the five buddha lineages 
of which there are 19. (It is usually translated as the five Dhyani Buddhas but for now we stick to the five buddha lineages).  
 
Five buddha 
lineages  Commitments/ Samayas  
Vairochana  Practising the three types of morality:  
(1) the ethics of refraining from non– virtue  
(2) the ethics of gathering virtuous Dharma  
(3) the ethics of benefiting sentient beings  
Keeping the three commitments:  
Going for refuge to the (1) Buddha (2) Dharma  ( 3) Sangha  
Akshobhya  There are the four 4 commitments of the  
(1) Vajra  (2) Bell  (3) Mudra and (4) Guru  
Ratnasambhava  Practising the four types of giving:  
(1) Material (2) Dharma (3) Fearlessness (4) Love  
 
Lesson  22          Page 144 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Five buddha 
lineages  Commitments/ Samayas  
Amitabha  Upholding the entire Buddhadharma:  
(1)  the outer samaya refers to Action and Performance Tantras  
(2)  the secret samaya refers to Yoga and Highest Yoga Tantra  
(3) the three vehicles refer to the hearer, solitary realiser and  bodhisattva 
vehicles  
Amoghasiddhi  (1) To uphold all the vows, i .e., the bodhisattva and tantric vows  
(2) Make as many offerings as one can, i.e., one should strive in making 
offerings.  
 
From here, you can see that taking a Highest Yoga Tantric initiation is not an easy thing 
because after doing so, one has to learn  and train in the entire Buddhadharma: both Sutra and 
Tantra, the hearer, solitary realiser and bodhisattva vehicles and the four classes of Tantra, 
Action, Performance, Yoga and Highest Yoga Tantra.  
 These are the 19 individual commitments of the five bud dha lineages. Then there are the 
general commitments which are avoiding the 14 root downfalls and many heavy transgressions. (Interpreter ’s comment: “Bompo” is sometimes translated as secondary transgressions which 
don’t convey the actual meaning. “Bompo” means heavy. I think it should be heavy 
transgressions).  
 If one transgresses the tantric vows, what should one do? “I f they occur one should restore 
them by confession (bshags sdom) according to the rite .”  There is a specific way of confessing 
the degene ration of tantric vows.  
 
• The ordination vows which apply to monks and nuns are taken in the presence of the 
Sangha. Therefore, they are restored in the presence of the Sangha.  
• Since bodhisattva vows are taken in the presence of the buddhas and the bodhisattvas, confession of transgressions and the restoration of these vows are made in the presence of the buddhas and bodhisattvas.  
• Since tantric vows are taken from the guru and t he deity in the mandala, any confession 
and restoration the tantric vows should be done in their presence.  
 
To purify any transgressions of tantric vows, one has to perform a self - initiation but the self-
initiation can only be performed by those who have  completed correctly the approximation of 
the deity. If one has done that, then one can perform the self -initiation. Otherwise, one has to 
take the initiation again. I guess this is one of the reasons why it is good to take initiations over and over again because it helps to purify and restore any transgressions of one’s tantric vows.  
 After obtaining initiation, one has to keep the vows and commitments purely and on that basis, 
one has to practise the path. How does one practise the path and what are the paths that one 
has to meditate on?

2.1.3  HOW TO PRACTICE THE PATH  
There are two:  
1 The method of meditating on the two stages ( rim gnyis )28 
2 The practice swiftly producing (bogs ‘byin pa’i spyod pa) these two  
 
2.1.3.1   THE METHOD OF MEDITA TING ON THE TWO STAGES  
(This) has two  
1 The way to meditate on the generation stage (bskyed rim)  
2 The way to meditate on the completion stage (rdzogs rim) 
 
2.1.3.1.1  THE WAY TO MEDITATE ON THE GENERATION ST AGE  
Panchen Lozang Chokyi Gyaltsen states that the definition of the generation stage is: ‘A yoga 
that does not occur from entry, abidance and dissolution of the winds in the central channel by the strength of meditation, but which matures the continuum for the completion stage, its 
result, and which meditates on any of the three: birth, death, and the intermediate state 
through discernment newly investigating any of their compatible aspects’ (ci tigs dang rnam pa 
mthun par).  
Ngawang Palden: I think he intends the main generation stage because it is not pervasive that the generation stage meditates on any of the compatible aspects of birth, death and the 
intermediate state.  (Pg. 19 of the root text)  
 
When you look at the definition of the generation stage given by Panchen Lozang Chokyi 
Gyaltsen, what should a generation stage be? What are its defining characteristics?  
 First, it is “ a yoga that does not occur due to the entry, abidance and the dissolution of the 
winds in the central channel by the strength of meditation.”  When the winds enter, abide and 
dissolve in the ce ntral channel through the force of meditation, that practice belongs to the 
completion stage so it  doesn’t happen at the generation stage. This is the first (characteristic) you must ascertain.  
• What is the external sign indicating that the winds are entering the central channel? The exhalation, i.e., the movement of the winds from both nostrils, is balanced. Usually 
for us, there is a difference in the strength of the winds coming out from our no strils. 
One is always stronger than the other.  
• What is the sign that the winds have entered and are abiding in the central channel? 
When one stops breathing and the stomach doesn’t move. Usually our stomach is moving  
 
• When the winds dissolve in the centra l channel, there are the signs of dissolution, 
starting from the mirage up to clear light.  
28 Two stages: the stage of generation and the stage of completion.  
 
Lesson  22          Page 146 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment                                                                   
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
During the generation stage, the entry, abidance and dissolution of the winds in the central channel don’t happen through the force of meditation. When they happen through the force of 
meditation, then that is the completion stage. That is the first point.  
 Secondly, the generation stage ripens the mind for the completion stage, its result.  
 
The third point is that (the generation stage) is the meditation “ on any of  the three: birth, death 
and the intermediate state through discernment newly investigating any of their compatible 
aspects.” This meditation is done at the level of visualisation. Here it says, “ through 
discernment newly investigating the compatible aspec ts of the birth, death and the 
intermediate state .” This means that it is not (actually) happening. It is not the real birth, death 
or the intermediate state (bardo) but rather the mind meditating on the compatible aspects of those three (at the level of v isualisation). This is the main practice of the generation stage. It is 
visualising or meditating on the compatible aspects of birth, death and intermediate state.  
 The generation stage is the practice where one trains in visualising the purification of these three states: ordinary death, ordinary bardo (intermediate state) and ordinary birth.  
 There are different definitions of the generation stage. We won’t be covering the debate on this (in the text). Our purpose here is to gain an understanding of wha t is the generation stage.  
 Just to show that there are different views on or definitions of the generation stage, we continue with the text. The author says in response to the definition by Panchen Lozang Chokyi 
Gyaltsen.  
Ngawang Palden: I think he intends the main generation stage because it is not pervasive that the generation stage meditates on any of the compatible aspects of birth, death and the intermediate state. That is because (1) yogas prior to (man chad) union with a knowledge consort (rig ma ‘ du bya ba) do not meditate on any of the compatible aspects of the three: 
birth, death and intermediate state; and (2) meditation on the protection wheel (bsrung ‘khor) is not meditation on any of the compatible aspects of these three. The first (part of t he reason) 
is established because rnam gzhag rim pa’i  rnam bshad extensively states:  
Those (yogas) prior to union with a knowledge consort (rig ma ‘du bya ba) are applied (sbyar) in the context of attaining Buddhahood, but are not applied on the ordinary (srid) (generation) 
stage..etc. (Pg. 19 of the root text)  
 
Rnam gzhag rim pa’i rnam bshad is a text by Je Rinpoche, Lama Tsong Khapa which in English 
can be translated as The Explanation of the Stages of Presentation. There is a text called The 
Stages of the Presentation composed by Nagabodhi to which Lama Tsong Khapa composed this 
commentary.  
 
The text says, “ not applied on the ordinary (srid) (generation) stage..etc.”  I think it is definitely 
not referring to the generation stage. (Lama Tsong Khapa) is talking about the cycle of 
 
Lesson  22          Page 147 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
existence: birth, death and bardo. These things are not applied on the ordinary stage. So, the 
word “(generation)”  shouldn’t be there as it may give you the idea that this is related to the 
generation stage.  
The second (part of the reason) is established because that (text) states:  
The protection wheel is meditated on during (Itar) the ordinary (srid) generation stage and 
since it is just a requisite or preliminary (state) to the actual basis of the generation stage, it is not ne cessary that dharmas compatible with the ordinary (srid) (generation) stage are applied 
to them. ( Pg. 20 of the root text)   
 I am going to summarise this whole discussion in simpler words. When you read this section again at home, check what the text is saying. Basically this is a debate about the generation stage. Panchen Lozang Chokyi Gyaltsen has one definition but Ngawang Pa lden said that it is 
not necessarily so. He points out as an example the practice of the meditation on the protection wheel. In the practice of the generation stage, before the actual practice of taking the three bodies into the path, there is a meditation  on the protection wheel. He says that this 
meditation on the protection wheel is part of  the generation stage but it does not meditate on 
the compatible aspects of death, bardo and birth. That is the crux of the whole disagreement.  
 Ngawang Palden is say ing that the meditation on the protection wheel is the generation stage 
but it is not the main generation stage. For it to be a main generation stage, it has to be the 
meditation on the compatible aspects of  death, bardo and birth. But for it to be a gene ration 
stage, it doesn’t necessarily have to be a meditation on the compatible aspects of death, bardo 
and birth.   He is saying that the meditation on the protection wheel, although it is not the main generation stage, is still part  of the generation stag e. I think this discussion arises because of 
the need to divide the practice of the generation stage into different parts. When you look at a Highest Yoga Tantra sadhana,  the text is quite long so one needs to break up the text into different sections in order to describe the various stages of the practice and to understand the practice.  
 When we were studying Yoga Tantra, we talked about the three meditative stabilisations:  
1. the meditative stabilisation of the initial preparation,  
2. the meditative stabilisa tion of the supreme royal mandala and  
3. the meditative stabilisation of the supreme royal activity.  
 This whole discussion is about the generation stage: (identifying) which is the first meditative stabilisation of the initial preparation and so forth. The meditation on the protection wheel is included in the meditative stabilisation of the initial preparation. If you are interested in the details, you can refer to the middle paragraph on page 20. If you are not interested, at least you should have a rough i dea of what is a generation stage.  
(To reiterate), a generation stage:  
• is a not a yoga that occurs from the entry,  abidance and dissolution of the winds in the 
central channel through the force of meditation.

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• ripens the mind for the completion stage, it s result.  
• is a meditation on the compatible aspects of birth, death and the bardo state.

with anything. You should not be reading the text. It is all right, however, for you to write down 
your thoughts if you can't do this mentally.

absorbed. I don’t see any benefit if we are to continue like this.   (I hope) this exercise will help. 
See whether you can answer these questions:  
 
Q1. What are the four initiations/empowerments?  
Q2. How many types of mandalas can be used in bestowing the vase initiation?  
 Geshe -la: Just recall (the answers) in your mind. Some people are reading (the text). This is not 
the point. (This is to) see whether  you can remember or not. Everything had been taught. If you 
cannot remember but you really want to learn, make sure you go back and read the text and/or

Q3. What are the different  types of mandalas that are used in bestowing the three higher 
initiations?  
Q4. If the tantric path is condensed into two, what are they?  
Q5. What is the definition of the generation stage?  
 
***********************  
When the path of Highest Yoga Tantra is condensed into two, there are two stages: the generation stage and the completion stage. The generation stage is defined as:  
A yoga that does not occur from entry, abidance and dissolution of the winds in the central channel by the strength of meditation, but which matures the continuum for the completion 
stage, its result, and which meditates on any of the three: birth, death, and the intermediate state through discernment newly investigating any of their compatible aspects’ (ci tigs dang rnam pa mthun par ). (Pg. 19 of the root text)  
One should know not only the words in the definition but the essential point is to understand what they are saying. The generation stage is essentially a yoga that does not happen “ due to 
the entry, abidance and dissolution of the winds in the central channel by the strength of 
meditation.” From the perspective of being a main generation stage, it also needs to be a yoga 
that meditates on birth, death and the intermediate state “ through discernment newly 
investigating any of the ir compatible aspects.” The main practice of the generation stage is to 
take the three bodies into the path. The definition refers to the meditation on the aspects of birth, death and the intermediate state. This is something that you must understand.  
 When we talk about the three bodies, there are:  
• the three bodies of the basis  
• the three bodies of the path and  
 
Lesson  23          Page 150 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• the three bodies of the resultant stage (three resultant bodies)  
 
The three bodies of the basis  are: 
• the dharmakaya of the basis.  
• the sambhogakay a of the basis.  
• the nirmanakaya of the basis.  
 
The dharmakaya of the basis can be taken to mean the clear light of death. At the time of death, all our consciousnesses will dissolve and finally the clear light of death, the most subtle mind, will manifest.  In short, the dharmakaya of the basis refers to the most subtle mind at the 
time of death.   Next is the sambhogakaya of the basis. What happens after the clear light of death? We have to take rebirth, but before that, we  enter the intermediate state. Our body in the intermediate state is referred to as the sambhogakaya of the basis.   From the intermediate state, we then take rebirth. Rebirth is posited to be the nirmanakaya of 
the basis.  
 
These are the three bodies of the basis but here we are not talking about the actual three bodies – the dharmakaya, sambhogakaya and nirmanakaya -  of the Buddha but nevertheless 
they are given that name.   What about the three bodies of the path? These wil l come later in the text but (we can) have a 
rough understanding of them now:  
• the dharmakaya of the path is the example clear light and the meaning clear light. (As to what the example clear light and meaning clear light are, these will be explained later  when 
we look at the completion stage in detail).  
• the sambhogakaya of the path are the pure illusory body and impure illusory body. (Again, we will learn what these are when we study the completion stage).   
• the nirmanakaya of the path refers to the coarse  body that arises that is other than the 
pure and impure illusory body.  
 The three bodies of the resultant stage  are the three bodies of the Buddha.  
 
The Basis  The Path  The Result  
Dharmakaya of the basis – clear 
light at the time of death  Dharmakaya of the path – example 
clear light and meaning clear light  Buddha’s truth body  
(Dharmakaya)  
Sambhogakaya of the basis  - 
intermediate state being (bardo 
being)  Sambhogakaya of the path – pure and 
impure illusory bodies  Buddha’s enjoyment body 
(sambhogakaya)  
Nirmanakaya of the basis – 
rebirth  
 Nirmanakaya of the path – the coarse 
body that arises that is other than the 
pure and impure illusory bodies.  Buddha’s emanation body 
(nirmanakaya)  
 
 
Lesson  23          Page 151 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
These are the illustrations of the three bodies of the basis, path and the result to help you have 
a rough idea of what we are referring to. It is important to know them.  
 The human being of this southern continent is one that is born from the womb,   possessing the six constituents (elements). There are different ways of counting these six constituents.   
• One way of counting them is the four elements (earth, water, fire and wind),   the channels 
and drops.  
• Another way of positing them are the three that we receive from the father (bone, marrow 
and semen) and the three received from the mother (blood, flesh and skin).  
This is said to be the best body (best basis) for engaging in the practice of  Highest Yoga  Tantra 
and through that path to achieve enlightenment.  One cannot practise Highest Yoga Tantra without such a basis. As stated in the section on the human life of leisures and endowments in 
the Lam Rim , this is one of the reasons that makes our lives so precious.  
 
This body possesses three main channels: the central channel and the left  and right channels. In general, there are 72,000 channels in the body. What happens when a human being of the southern continent born from the womb and possessing the si x constituents, die? What 
happens during the death process?  
 When the death process starts, all the winds in the 72,000 channels of the body gradually gather into the right and the left channels. The winds in the right and left channels then gather into  the central channel. Gradually, the winds in the central channel gather and dissolve into the indestructible drop  at the centre of our chest.  
 
The coarse winds start to dissolve and become subtler. Likewise, the coarse consciousness starts to dissolve an d the subtle consciousness becomes manifest. As the consciousness 
becomes subtler and subtler, the mind gradually culminates and becomes the most subtle mind.  That is the mind at the time of death which is also known as the clear light of death.  
 That is  real death, i.e., when the most subtle level of mind (the clear light of death) manifests is 
when death actually occurs. When this most subtle level of mind is manifesting, nothing else appears to that mind. When we say  nothing appears to this clear light of death except emptiness, the emptiness referred to here is not the emptiness of true existence but rather it is mere emptiness, like the empty sky or simply nothingness.  
 It is said in the teachings that an ordinary individual can abide in the state of the clear light of 
death for up to three days. Yogis/practitioners can abide in the state for many days because they are of a different level. This clear light of death is the dharmakaya of the basis and is also referred to as the mother clear light. This  is something that you must know. This happens to 
everyone. When we die, the mother clear light manifests.  
 Whether we say the mother clear light or the clear light of death or the dharmakaya of the basis, we are referring to the same thing. Is this mothe r clear light the actual clear light? The 
answer is no.  
 
 
Lesson  23          Page 152 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
When we talk about clear light, we have to understand that we can look at clear light in terms 
of it being an object or a consciousness:  
• The objective (object) clear light refers to emptiness which is the mere negation of true 
existence.  
• The subjective (subject) clear light refers to the subtle wisdom which has emptiness as its object.  
 When a human being of the southern continent born from the womb and possessing the six constituents dies, all th e coarser winds and consciousnesses start to dissolve and become 
subtler. Eventually, when they all dissolve, the most subtle level of consciousness manifests.  This is the clear light of death.  
 
When one moves from the clear light of death, one enters the intermediate state where one 
has to take rebirth without choice. The problem lies in our inability to stop actual death, i.e., the clear light of death which means we cannot stop ourselves from e ntering the 
bardo/intermediate state which means we cannot stop rebirth. The whole point then is to stop, to purify and to overcome ordinary death. The practices for doing so come in the generation stage followed by the completion stage.  
 Let us take the generation stage practice of Guhyasamaja as an example. When we look at the sadhana, in the beginning, one visualises oneself as the principal deity and one visualises the 32 deities on different parts of the body. One then visualises the gradual dissoluti on, in stages, of 
these various deities.   
 
At the same time as one is performing these visualisations, one visualises that the death process is happening, i.e., one sees the signs of death and so forth. One trains the mind in this. 
Gradually, all the deit ies on the body and the body of oneself as the deity dissolve. Finally, 
oneself as the principal deity also dissolves into clear light. At that time, one concentrates 
single -pointedly on emptiness. This is the practice of taking death into the path of the 
dharmakaya.  
 Of course, the actual signs of the dissolution are not happening. During the generation stage, one is simply visualising.  Therefore, the generation stage is the yoga that meditates on birth, death and bardo “ through discernment newly investigating any of their compatible aspects .”  
 This is to give you a general idea. In order to know the details, you will have to look at the actual sadhana and following that, you go through the practice. Only then will things become clearer but this is not what we are going to do here.  
 The practice of meditating on the aspects of death known as taking death into the path of the 
dharmakaya in the generation stage is not  the actual thing that purifies ordinary death. What 
actually purifies ordinary dea th occurs at the completion stage. Specifically, it is the example 
clear light and the meaning clear light, which only happen during the completion stage, that 
actually purify ordinary death.   
 
Lesson  23          Page 153 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Returning back to ordinary death, the death process culminates  in the clear light of death. 
When this stops, in the next moment, one enters the bardo as an intermediate state being 
(bardo being):  
• The substantial cause of the mind of the intermediate state being is the clear light of death.  
• The substantial cause of the body of the intermediate state being is the most subtle wind 
which is one entity with the mind of the clear light of death.  
• The intermediate state being is born miraculously in various type of births   (miraculous 
births).  
• All the five senses are est ablished simultaneously.  
• The body is said to be very subtle and does not possess obstructiveness.  
 
Once one is in the intermediate state, one has to take rebirth. If one is to be reborn as a human 
being, one will enter the intermediate state of a human b eing. There are (two) schools of 
thought with regards to this:  
• According to  the Lower Abhidharmakhosa, once one has entered into the intermediate 
state of a particular rebirth that cannot be changed.  
• But, according to the Compendium of Knowledge  there is no pervasion.  
 
One arises in the intermediate state from the clear light of death. In order to overcome this state, on has to purify it. In the generation stage there is the practice called taking the intermediate state into the path of sambhogakaya. In this meditation (on the aspects of the intermediate state), one goes through the five manifest enlightenments and then arises as the primordial protector. This is very clearly stated in the sadhana of the deity.   
Taking the intermediate state into the path of sambhogakaya during the generation stage is for 
the purpose of purifying the ordinary intermediate state. It is a practice that is done on the level of visualisation alone. That is not the actual method to overcome the ordinary intermediate state which only happens with the attainment of the two illusory bodies at the completion stage.  
From the intermediate state, one has to take rebirth. This process happens as one’s consciousness and winds become coarser culminating in one taking rebirth. As the meditation on the aspects of rebirth in the generation stage is to purify that, one visualises oneself arising 
as Vajrasattva from the primordial protector.  
 
This is a rough presentation of the generation stage meditating on the  aspects of the three bodies. T here is no way you can understand them in detail now. We do not have much time.  
 At the very minimum, you should understand:  
• what are the three bodies of the basis  
• what are the three bodies of the path and  
• what are the three resultant bodies.  
• what happens during the generation stage  
 The generation stage is the meditation on the aspects of these three things –  death, 
intermediate state and rebirth and consists of :  
 
Lesson  23          Page 154 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• the meditation on the aspects of death  
• the meditation on the aspects of interme diate state and  
• the meditation on the aspects of rebirth.

LESSON NO: 24   DATE: 2ND JULY 2009  
 We are now studying the Tantric paths and grounds, in particular, (the paths and grounds of) Highest Yoga Tantra. The path o f Highest Yoga Tantra can be  condensed into two stages: the 
generation stage and the completion stage. The whole point of studying this is to have an understanding of what these two stages are.  
 We have received many Sutric teachings. You all must have received teachings on the Three Principal Aspects of the Path: renunciation, bodhicitta and right view. Even though we don’t have these actual realisations in our minds, at least intellectually, we understand what renunciation is, what bodhicitta is and wha t the correct view of emptiness is.  
 But when it comes to Tantra, in particular, the generation and the completion stages of Highest Yoga Tantra, there are people who know these words but I think it is very rare to find someone who has a good understanding of what these two stages are.  
 The purpose of receiving an initiation into Highest Yoga Tantra is to allow us to practise the generation and completion stages of Highest Yoga Tantra and in order to do that, we first have to understand what they are. Fro m the point of view of the practice to be done, an 
understanding of these two stages is indispensable.   
 When we are able to attain a correct understanding of the generation stage and the completion stage of Highest Yoga Tantra, we will definitely accumulate a great amount of merit. 
Why is that so? First, the coming of the Buddha into the world is a rare phenomenon. On top of that, the Buddha revealing the Tantric teachings is an even rarer phenomenon. On top of that, it is even rarer  to have the opportunity to meet and to practise the ultimate Tantric practice, Highest Yoga Tantra, specifically both the generation and completion stages. We should always remember how fortunate we are to have the opportunity to study and to reflect on this text that shows the practices of the generation and completion stages.  
 Even if we are unable to practise them, even if we are unable to develop these two paths in our mental continua but, at the very least, we can place imprints of them in our minds. We are not talking about placing just any kind of imprints but placing very stable and strong imprints of these two stages in our minds. In order to do that, we must engage in the threefold activity of 
listening to and reflecting and meditating on these two stages.  
Rememberi ng our good fortune to have met these Tantric teachings is not enough. We must 
strive from our own side to place stable and strong imprints of these teachings in our mental 
continua. That is a personal responsibility. Do imprints really help? Does it really matter whether we have imprints or not? Of course it does. We can tell from our (own)experience how important virtuous imprints are.  
 I started memorising the definition of the generation stage about 12 years ago. 12 years have past and now I have to te ach this text. When I read the definition in the text, (the recall) comes 
almost immediately. This is due to my having memorised the definition 12 years ago, the imprints which I had placed in my mind then without which I doubt the recall would come.  
 
Lesson  24          Page 156 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
In this life, we can see that when we place an imprint in the continuum, somehow it helps us in 
the later part of our life. We can see the usefulness of  imprints. From that, we can also infer that the imprints placed in this life will affect us and make a di fference in our future lives.  
 When I talk about placing imprints in our mental continua, I am not only  referring to imprints that are left on the mind through listening to the  teaching alone. That is not enough. We have 
to reflect on the teachings and then gain an ascertainment of th em. Only then will the imprints 
be very stable and strong. That is the kind of imprints I am talking about, the kind of imprints 
we need.  
 
*******************  
We have started to look at the definition of the generation stage. First, you must memorise the definition and then try to gain an understanding of (what it means). You must do this. If you don’t even know the words (of the definition), how are you going to understand anything, how are you even going to recall anything? Your understanding of the gene ration stage is like your 
working capital, so to speak. If you don’t even know the words, it is like trying to start a business without any money. The definition of the generation stage is there in the text and I am not going through that again. Please loo k it up on your own. In essence, (you should know) 
what happens during the generation stage practice.  
  
The generation stage involves the meditation known as taking the three bodies into the path. This is the essence of the generation stage:  
• We have the p ractice of taking death into the path of the dharmakaya. In order to really 
understand this practice, we have to examine the sadhana, the self generation rite of the deity without which, it is quite difficult to understand this subject. Essentially, this p ractice 
is a meditation on the aspects that are compatible with ordinary death, where during the death process, certain things happen. The meditation of taking death into the path of the dharmakaya is done to purify ordinary death.  
 
• The practice of taking  death into the path of the dharmakaya is followed by the practice of 
taking the intermediate state into the path of the sambhogakaya. This is a meditation on 
the aspects that are compatible with the way in which the intermediate state comes into being and  is performed in order to purify the ordinary intermediate state.  
 
• After that, there is the meditation of taking birth into the path of the nirmanakaya which is 
a meditation on the aspects that are compatible with birth. That meditation is done to 
purify ordinary birth.  
 
The meditation of …  This meditation is done to..  
taking death into the path of the dharmakaya  purify ordinary death  
taking the intermediate state into the path of the 
sambhogakaya  purify ordinary intermediate state  
taking birth into the  path of the nirmanakaya  purify ordinary birth  
 
 
Lesson  24          Page 157 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
The meditation of taking the three bodies into the path is essentially the  meditation on the 
aspects that are compatible with death, the intermediate state and birth. But this meditation is mentally fabricated. As the definition states, “… meditates on any of the three: birth, death and 
the intermediate stage through discernment newly investigating any of their compatible aspects.”  
 Does this meditation of taking the three bodies into the path actuall y purify ordinary death, the 
intermediate state and birth immediately? The answer is that it does not. What the meditation does is to ripen the mind for the completion stage. Therefore, this is one of the components of the definition of the generation stag e.  
 Although the generation stage does purify those three - ordinary death, the intermediate state 
and birth - it is not the actual purifier of those three. It is the path of the completion stage that 
directly purifies (i.e., overcomes, stops) ordinary de ath, the intermediate state and birth. But 
through the practice of the generation stage, one receives the blessings of the deity, one is 
able to purify the obscurations and negativities and accumulate vast amounts of merit which then makes it easier for th e practitioner to develop the completion stage. The generation stage 
is a meditation on the aspects that are compatible with the completion stage and therefore ripens the mental continuum for that practice.  
 When you look in detail at each and every singl e word of the definition, it (helps to) eliminate a 
lot of wrong understanding or wrong concept along the way. At the end of that process, the understanding you gain is very pure and correct. That is the reason why we have discussion sessions as part of th e Basic Program. If you don’t want to discuss, you don’t want to study, you 
don’t want to think about or reflect on it, then there is no way you can gain correct understanding. It is very important to have doubt.   The m editation on the aspects compatible with death, the intermediate state and birth  is done 
not for the purpose for establishing death, the intermediate state and birth  as even without 
any meditation, these things are going to happen to us but they are things we do not want. Death, the intermed iate state and birth are the origins of all our suffering. Therefore, we do 
this meditation to purify them.    
It is very clear that the practice of the main generation stage is taking the three bodies into the path to purify death, the intermediate state and birth. It is very clearly explained later in the 
text how the completion stage actually  purifies these three, but the question will then arise: 
How does the generation stage purify death, the intermediate state and birth?  
 During the generation stage practice, when one is meditating on the aspects that are compatible with ordinary death, ordinary intermediate state and ordinary birth, in relation to each of these states, there are the two elements of (1) ordinary appearance and (2) ordinary concept (gr asping).  These are the two objects of abandonment of the generation stage.   
 There is an appearance of death as  being ordinary. There is an appearance of the intermediate state as being ordinary. There is an appearance of birth as being ordinary. The ge neration stage 
(works to) suppress the ordinary appearance of and ordinary grasping at these three states.  
 
Lesson  24          Page 158 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
What the generation stage seeks to do is to stop the consciousness to which such (ordinary) 
appearances arise. The way to overcome ordinary appearan ce and ordinary concept is:  
• to develop divine pride of oneself as the deity (which is the antidote to ordinary concept) 
and  
• to have clear appearance of oneself as deity (which is the antidote to ordinary appearance).  
 
When one’s divine pride of oneself as the deity is strong and stable as is one’s  clear appearance of oneself as the deity, then ordinary appearance and ordinary concept will not manifest, i.e., they are suppressed. (This will be explained later in the tex t).  
 Here, we are talking about ordinary appearance and ordinary concept in relation to the mental and not sense consciousness. The practice of generation stage to eliminate ordinary 
appearance and ordinary concept is done with the mental and not sense co nsciousness, i.e., 
we are not stopping ordinary appearance and ordinary concept to the sense consciousness but 
rather to the mental consciousness. One does so by developing divine pride and the clarity of oneself as a deity. Then ordinary appearance and or dinary concept do not manifest in one’s 
mental consciousness. In this way, one suppresses the ordinary appearance of death, the intermediate state and birth and our ordinary concept of them.  
 Question: In the definition of the generation stage, is there a ny significance to the word 
“newly " as in “through discernment newly investigating any of their compatible aspects”? 
Would it affect the meaning of the definition if this word is omitted?  
Answer : I think the word “ newly ” here has the connotation of initia lly rather than being 
indicative of the notion of time. At the beginning of the generation stage, when you first 
meditate, you do it just through visualisation. (It implies that). If we take this word to mean “initially,” is there a fault?  With regards to  omitting this word, I haven’t seen any texts 
commenting on this. This is something to be further investigated.  
 Question:  Is there a difference in the usage of the term, “birth” vs. “rebirth”?  
Answer:  I don’t see anything wrong in using the two terms interchangeably. ( Interpreter ’s 
comment: For the purpose of translation, we only use the word, “birth”).  
 Question: Does the subjective clear light that Geshe -la mentioned in the last class, does that go 
on to become the omniscient mind of the Buddha?  
Answer : Yes.  
 Question: In the generation stage, the entry, abidance and dissolution of the winds in the central channel does not occur because one is using only the mental awareness. Does that mean that the s ense awareness does not occur at all, i.e., (the sense awareness occurs) only in 
the completion stage?  
Geshe -la: Generation stage is mental. Completion stage, then it becomes real.  
 The definition of the generation stage talks about meditating on the comp atible aspects of the 
death, the intermediate state and birth which is done to purify those three things but the important thing to understand is how  does this happen? The text is not explicit with regards (to 
this point). From my point of view, w e should relate it to stopping ordinary appearance and 
 
Lesson  24          Page 159 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
grasping (ordinary concept) of these three through developing divine pride and the clarity of 
oneself as a deity. This is only my idea and I could be wrong. You must think whether there is any problem with this . 
 Question:  How does one move from the generation stage to the completion stage and when 
will we know we are ready to move to the next level?  Answer:  This will come later in the text. Basically, it is the opposite of the generation stage. The 
generation stage is “ a yoga that does not occur from entry, abidance and dissolution of the 
winds in the central channel by the strength of meditation.”  When, through the strength of 
meditation, the time comes when the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central c hannel, 
then that is the beginning of the completion stage.   If you wish to study this in more detail, there is a debate in the text regarding what constitutes a generation stage. In short, the author is saying that if  it is a generation stage, it doesn’ t 
necessarily have to be the meditation on the compatible aspects of death, the intermediate state and birth. That is the conclusion. If you are not interested in (understanding this topic) more deeply, you can forget what I have just said.   If you just w ant a (basic) understanding, then just know this: The generation stage is the 
meditation on the compatible aspects of death, the intermediate state and birth.  
You must know the answer to these questions:  
• What  do you meditate on during the generation stage? The answer: You meditate on 
aspects compatible with death, intermediate state and birth.  
• Why do you want to meditate on these aspects? The answer: It cannot be to create 
death, intermediate state and birth but to overcome/purify birth, death and the intermediate state.  
• How  does meditating on these aspects purify these three things? You should think 
about your own answer through reading or discussion  
 Regarding that one says: The protection wheel and yogas prior to union with a knowledge consort (rig ma ‘du bya ba) are not the actual generation stage because it is pervasive that the 
generation stage meditates on taking any of the three bodies as the path. That is because it is not pervasive that the path in the  continuum of one on the stage of generation is the stage of 
generation.  
 Ngawang Palden: There is no pervasion (to your assertion), however (your) reason is 
established because there are many common paths of the Perfection Vehicle and the lower 
Tantras in  their continuum. (Your initial assertion) is unacceptable because it would contradict 
the explanation of the bonds (ching ba) of the four requisites of sadhanas (bsnyen sgrub), and 
the four yogas, and the six requisites, and the three concentrations in th e classification of the 
general bonds subsumed in the generation stage. These should be extensively known through the Stages of Mantra.(Pg. 20 of the root text).  
 Those of you who are really interested and wish to discuss, you should read more. If you do not understand what you are reading, you can ask me.

The actual practice of the generation stage is the meditation of taking the three bodies into the 
path. Prior to the actual practice, there are preliminaries. You should look at the sadhana of the High est Yoga Tantric deities. In the beginning, as a preliminary, there is always the practice of 
the protection wheel in order to prevent any obstacles from arising during the actual practice.

W
e have now obtained the precious human rebirth and we all have some faith in (the workings 
of) karma, the law of cause and effect. On top of that, we have the opportunity to study, reflect 
and analyse the tantric teachings which lead to the final attainment of buddhahood. All this is 
the result of the ripening of virtuous karma that was  accumulated in the past.  
W
hile we are still able to make use of this opportunity, we should try as much as possible to 
place as many imprints as we can in our minds through studying, reflecting on and analysing 
these teachings by generating  determinati on and courage.  
W
ith respect to studying the tantric teachings, especially the teachings on Highest Yoga Tantra, 
we should not procrastinate, thinking we can do this in the future. Rather, while we now have the time and opportunity, we should try our best to study as much as possible.  
W
e should remember what Guntang Rinpoche said:  
Perhaps twenty years of remembering that  
One should do a practice,  
Perhaps twenty years of being forever  
About to do a practice,  
Perhaps another ten years of saying,  
‘I never did a practice’ –  That’s the story of an empty and wasted human life
29. 
W
hen you think about this, all of us are like that. We should remember this piece of advice 
from Guntang Rinpoche so that we do not procrastinate but, instead, engage in the  Dharma 
immediately.  
Que
stion:  What is the substantial cause of the example clear light and meaning clear light?  
Answer : As we had studied (during the module) on buddha nature, the nature of the mind is 
clear light. This ultimately refers to (the extremely) subtle mind, the fundamental innate mind of clear light that we all have. There is also the (extremely) subtle wind which is of one entity with that fundamental innate mind of clear light. We have had this (extremely) subtle mind and 
wind since beginningless time.  
We can only find the explanation of this extremely subtle mind in the teachings of Highest Yoga 
Tantra. Such explanations cannot be found in the teachings of the three lower classes of Tantra 
29 Pg. 348, Liberation in the Palm of Your Hand  by Pabonka Rinpoche, Wisdom Publictions  
L
esson  25 Page 162 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment 
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
or the Paramitayana. That is why they are unable to explain such a mind.  
 
As long as the “I” or “person” exists, the extremely subtle mind together with the extremely subtle wind will exist. These are the bases for one to accomplish the two bodies of the Buddha. This is the reason why we say all sentien t beings can achieve enlightenment. Literally, we are 
“suitable” for  achieving enlightenment.  
• The extremely subtle mind (the fundamental innate mind of clear light) is the substantial 
cause for actualising the dharmakaya.  
• The extremely subtle wind (whic h is of one entity with that extremely subtle mind) is the 
substantial cause for actualising the rupakaya (the form body of the Buddha).  
 
With regards to the question:  
• this fundamental innate mind of clear light is the substantial cause for the example cle ar 
light and the meaning clear light and   
• the extremely subtle wind is the substantial cause for the illusory bodies (i.e., the impure and pure illusory bodies).  
 
Question: The meditations that we are doing on emptiness, are they the substantial cause for  
the truth body?  
Answer : Your point is that besides the mind of clear light, all the meditations that we do in 
relation to deity yoga should also be the substantial causes for dharmakaya.  
 When you investigate deeply, what exactly goes on to enlightenment? When you say the mind goes on to enlightenment, which mind goes on to enlightenment? This has to be analysed.  
 What is the real substantial cause for the dharmakaya? What is the real substantial cause for the rupakaya? This is the same as analysing which mind moves on from life to life. Do all the consciousnesses that we have go on to the next life?   The conclusio n is that the extremely subtle mind is the substantial cause for actualising the 
dharmakaya, which refers here to the wisdom truth body. The extremely subtle wind is the 
substantial cause for actualising the rupakaya, the form body of the Buddha. This is v ery clearly 
explained in Highest Yoga Tantra.  
 
Question from student : (inaudible)

substantial cause for actualising the form body of the Buddha? Using the explanati on offered 
by Highest Yoga Tantra as the benchmark, is the explanation offered by the three lower classes of Tantra correct or not?  
  When your questions are well considered, after having reflected on what you have read, such questions will benefit everyone rather than just asking questions randomly.  When you get the answers to such well considered questions, then it will really affect your mind and you get (something out of it). But random questions are quite pointless. Don’t you think this is so?
