LESSON NO: 29   DATE: 19TH JULY 2009  
 
We will spend a couple of minutes to do some reflection. Reflection is very important. 
Especially as a Buddhist, it is important to know how to keep the mind happy. Take the prayer 
we recite before class as an example. When one knows how to think, just hav ing this 
opportunity to recite this prayer alone can make our minds happy.  
 
All of us know the teachings of the Buddha but somehow we still remain unhappy. Of course, I 
think it is quite difficult to find someone who is always happy throughout his (entire ) life.  But, 
at least, from our side as a Buddhist, we should reflect on the teachings and be able to keep our minds happy. For example, we have some faith and belief in the Buddha. That in itself 
should bring some kind of happiness to our minds. This is followed by prostrations and prayers 
which come about in dependence on that faith and belief.  
 
There are many ways to develop faith in the Buddha but the best method is to study and learn 
the teachings of the Buddha. The faith that arises from understandi ng the teachings of the 
Buddha is a very special kind of faith which is very different from other (kinds of faith). It can 
become irreversible faith. That comes from studying and thinking about the Buddha’s teachings.  
When we do this, we can come to our o wn decisive conclusions about the teachings, seeing 
how they are true. Then a very special kind of faith will arise in those teachings. From that, a special faith will arise (in us) towards the Buddha (himself) because we come to understand 
that he is some one very special. We will spend five minutes in silence to think about this.  
 
********************************  
 
Before we can talk about the completion stage, we first need to understand the different levels 
of the body and mind. The body exists on three different levels:  
1.  The coarse body refers to the body that we have which we can see.   
2.  The subtle body refers to the channels, winds and the drops abiding inside our body.  
3.   The extremely subtle body refers to the extremely subtle wind.  
 
Likewis e, the mind is divided into three:  
1. The coarse mind refers to the manifest sense consciousnesses.  
2. The subtle mind refers to the 80 indicative conceptions together with the minds of appearance, increase and (near) attainment.  
3. The extremely subtle mind is the  fundamental innate mind.  
 
It is quite difficult to understand how we move from one particular completion stage to another higher completion stage without first understanding the nature of the bases, i.e., the body and mind. We should remember all these le vels.  
 
What happens at the time of death?  
• At the time of death, the coarse physical body ceases.  
• First the four different elements (earth, water, fire and wind) gradually dissolve into one 
another, i.e., the earth element dissolves into the water element and so forth.  
• The mind also becomes subtler.  
 
Lesson  29          Page 192 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• After the dissolution of the four elements, the min ds of the 80 indicative conceptions 
gradually dissolve into the mind of white appearance.  
• The mind of white appearance gradually ceases and dissolves into the mind of red increase.  
• The mind of red increase ceases and dissolves into the mind of black near attainment.  
• That mind of the black near -attainment ceases and dissolves into the extremely subtle 
mind, the mind of clear light at the time of death.  
• When this clear light mind manifests, this is when actual death is said to happen.  
 
You need to have a  rough idea of the stages of dissolution:  
o First, the coarser levels of consciousnesses dissolve.  
o Then the minds of the 80 indicative conceptions dissolve.  
o Then the mind of white appearance dissolves.  
o Then the mind of red increase dissolves.  
o Then the mind of the black near -attainment dissolves.  
o Finally, at the end of the death process, it culminates in the mind of clear light.  
 At the time of our death, (as) we go through the dissolution process, we will arrive at the stage where the mind of white appearance  manifests. At that time one has the appearance of 
whitish light. This is an internal appearance to the mind and is not something external or appearing from outside.   
 Why does this happen? We have three main channels in our body: the central channel an d the 
right and left channels. The mind of white appearance starts when the winds in the right and left channels start to enter the central channel. The winds at the upper part of the body enter 
the central channel from the top and, at the same time, the w hite bodhicitta at the crown 
(which came from our father) starts to descend together with the movement of the winds down into the central channel.  This white bodhicitta descends to the level of one’s heart 
chakra. As it descends, one will have the internal appearance of white light.  
 The mind of white appearance is followed by the manifestation of the mind of red increase. 
That happens when the winds in the right and left channels enter the central channel from beneath the navel. Due to the movement of the winds up the (central) channel, the red bodhicitta at the navel that came from our mother moves up the central channel to the level of the heart. Due to the movement of this red bodhicitta up the central channel, one has the internal reddish appearance.   After the mind of red increase, the mind of black near -attainment  will manifest. Earlier on we 
saw how the winds in the right and left channels enter the central channel from the top and bottom. These winds gradually come to the level of the heart but they have not yet dissolved at the heart.  
 
At the heart, there is th e indestructible drop which has always existed from birth. When the 
white and red bodhicittas come together at the level of the heart, there is an appearance of 
darkness to the mind. It is like fainting or blacking out. It is said that there are two parts to this 
mind of black near-  attainment: During the earlier part, it is said that one still has some 
 
Lesson  29          Page 193 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
mindfulness and  objects can appear to that mind. In the later part, that mind does not have 
mindfulness of any object. At that time, it is like one has fai nted or blacked out.  
 When one arises (wakes up) from that state, (at the end of the dissolution process) that is when the extremely subtle mind manifests. This is the clear light mind of death. What appears 
to that clear light mind is vacuity or nothingness. It is not the emptiness (of inherent existence) that we usually talk about. Together with this extremely subtle mind is the extremely subtle 
wind. It is said that one can abide in this state for 3 days. For yogis or practitioners, they are 
able to do so for a longer period of time.  
 One remains in that state of clear light mind of death till the extremely subtle wind starts to stir. That is the time when the extremely subtle wind inside the indestructible drop will leave the body with the extremely subtle mind (since these two are one entity).    
The signs of the consciousness having  left the body is the emitting of the white and red bodhicittas: The red bodhicitta at the level of the heart ascends and is emitted through the nostril. The white bodhici tta at the level of the heart descends and is emitted from the sexual 
organ. Seeing these red and white bodhicittas leave the body is taken as the signs that the consciousness has left the body.  
 The body of the intermediate state comes into being when on e enters the intermediate state.  
The extremely subtle wind is the mount of the extremely subtle mind.  
o The extremely subtle wind is the substantial cause of the body of the intermediate state being.  
o The extremely subtle mind is the substantial cause of the mind of the intermediate state being.  
 When one enters the intermediate state, the first consciousness that comes into being is the mind of black near attainment followed gradually by the mind of red increase, white appearance, the 80 indicative conceptions and so forth. Basically, the mind becomes coarser, i.e., when (the mind) is first established, it is  established as subtle and then it becomes coarser.  
You have to know the stages of the process of dying and how one comes into existence again becau se when we examine the completion stage, this whole process will come into play.  
o In essence when one dies, the coarse mind will become subtler.  
o When one enters the intermediate state, the mind moves from being subtle into a 
coarser state.  
 
Question: Can  the body of the intermediate state being also be divided into coarse and subtle 
levels?  
Answer:  It is not clear in the text but I guess one can posit different levels of subtlety because 
when the intermediate state being first comes into existence, the mi nd of black near -
attainment manifests. There is a wind that is the mount of that consciousness just as there are the winds that are the mounts of the minds of red increase and white appearance. These three levels of mind are considered subtle. We can there fore probably say that the winds which are 
the mounts of these three consciousnesses (can also) be classified as subtle bodies. When the intermediate state being comes into full existence, developing the five sense powers and so 
 
Lesson  29          Page 194 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
forth, we can then posit that to be the coarse body.  
 
In order to take rebirth, the intermediate state being has to die and does so by  going through 
the same death dissolution process. The coarser levels of mind dissolve and the subtle mind 
manifests. We have the 80 indicative conc eptions, the minds of white appearance, red increase  
and black near -attainment, finally culminating in the clear light mind of death.  
 
It is this mind of clear light of death of the intermediate state being that connects with the 
fertilised egg (at concep tion) and takes rebirth in the womb.  The mind becomes increasingly 
coarser again. The mind of black near-  attainment will manifest, followed by the mind of red 
increase and so forth. This is the reverse process. In the case of rebirth from the womb, birth  
does not refer to the time when one emerges from the mother’s womb. Birth is the first moment of conception.  
 From the perspective of a normal person, the winds do not move into the central channel until the time of death. They move into the central channel  due to the force of karma.  
 I have been asked about the mother and son clear lights and the mixing of the two. The mother clear light  refers to the clear light of death. Everyone will experience this mother clear light in 
the death process.  One can also manifest this clear light mind through the force of meditation. The clear light mind that manifests through the force of meditation is termed son clear light . If 
one is able to do that during the death process, then instead of the clear light of death arising at the time of death, through the force of meditation one manifests that son clear light. When one is a ble to do that during the death dissolution process, this is called the mixing of mother 
and son clear lights .   
 You have to know this whole process very well from how the mind of white appearance manifests and so forth and also how this process works in reverse , i.e., from the mind of black near- attainment to the mind of red increase and so forth.  
 
Question:  Does this process apply to animals?  
Answer : This particular death process pertains to those who are born from the womb, 
possessing the six constitue nts.  
 Question: If the bardo being cannot find a body in which to take rebirth, does it die every seven 
days? Answer : It is taught in the teachings that the intermediate state being can remain for a 
maximum of 49 days but within that 49 days, the intermediate state being has to take rebirth. Within that period, if he does not find a rebirth after 7 days, he will have to die and then be reborn again in the intermediate state. He has to die every week as long as he has not taken  rebirth but up to a maximum of 49 days. The intermediate state being definitely has to take rebirth within those 49 days.  
 2.1.3.1.2   THE WAY TO MEDITATE ON THE COMPLETION ST AGE  
 There are two divisions:  
 
Lesson  29          Page 195 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
1 Explaining some classifications of the completion stage of the arya system  
2 Teaching in brief the completion stage of other Highest (Yoga Tantras)  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1  EXPLAINING SOME CLAS SIFICATIONS OF THE C OMPLETION STAGE OF T HE ARYA              
SYSTEM  
 
(This) has two(types):  
1 Examining the definition  
2 The meaning of each classific ation  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.1  EXAMINING THE DEFINI TION  
The yoga in the continuum of a learner derived through making the winds enter, abide and 
dissolve in the central (channel) through the strength of meditation. ( Pg. 26 of the  root text ) 
In the definition (of the completion stage), it is a yoga  that exists in the continuum of a learner. 
There are some masters who assert that the completion stage exists at the time of 
enlightenment as well whereas there are others who say it does not exist at that time. It only exists when one is a learner. (The buddhas are non -learners). We will stick to the definition 
given here. According to this definition, does the completion stage exist in the buddhas’ continua?  
 What are the different levels of the co mpletion stage? This is something you have to memorise 
by heart. According to Arya Nagarjuna’s tradition, there are six levels of the completion stage:  
1. physical isolation (isolation of body)  
2. verbal isolation (isolation of speech)  
3. mental isolation (isolatio n of mind)  
4. illusory body  
5. clear light  
6. union  
 It would be good if there is some system that can help beginners to learn such things but the text is not arranged in that way. There are many ways of dividing the completion stage. There are the four, five and s ix divisions but they all arrive at the same conclusion. There are different 
ways of explaining the modes of progressing on the path of Highest Yoga Tantra. Here, we examine this from the perspective of Guhyasamaja, according to Arya Nagarjuna’s tradition.   
 
The completion stage has to be the yoga that is derived through making the winds enter, abide 
and dissolve in the central channel through the force of meditation. The details of the different levels of the completion stage will come later. Here, I will give a simple explanation of  these levels but it is not the definitive explanation as there are many details (to be covered later).  
 Through the force of meditation on the subtle drop at the upper opening of the central channel, the yogi experiences the signs of the dissolution that happen at the time of death. That experience lasts up to (just) before the loosening of the channel knots at the heart. That whole period of time is called the completion stage of the physical isolation . 
 
Lesson  29          Page 196 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
In general, when we talk about physical isolation, there is the physical isolation of the generation stage and the physical isolation of the completion stage. Here we are talking about the latter. During this isolation, the winds actually enter the central channel but are unable to enter the heart level of the central channel. The physical isolation of the completion stage exists from the time when the winds enter the central channel through the force of meditation and up to the time before the winds dissolve into the heart lev el of the central channel.  
 It is said that it is difficult to release the channel knots which exist at the heart charka. When (they are released) through the force of meditation, the winds at the central channel then will be able to enter the heart level  of the central channel. That is the time when one moves to the 
next level of the completion stage, i.e.,  verbal isolation .  
 Although the winds have entered the heart level of the central channel, they have yet to dissolve into the indestructible drop the re. The next completion stage level, mental isolation , 
happens when, through the force of meditation, the winds at the heart level of the central channel dissolve into the indestructible drop.  
 Question: When the white and red bodhicittas move, do the (cha nnel) knots open?  
Geshe -la: If they don’t open at the time of death, you won’t die. In order to die, must open. (This 
happens due to) force of karma.  
 Question:  Do these drops have to melt?  
Answer : The white bodhicitta that abides at our crown is the subtle part of the white bodhicitta 
from our father. For that white bodhicitta to descend to the central channel, it has to melt and is said to be in the nature of liquid.  
  Question: Is it that melting and movement of the white bodhicitta that results in what is termed ‘tantric bliss’?  
Answer:  This will be covered later. There are methods taught to induce the four joys (four 
blisses). That happens through melting the white bodhicitta at the crown. Each of these four joys is further subdivided into four, making a total of 16 joys. There is also the joy associated with the ascent of the red bodhicitta which is further divided into 12.  
 
Question: Why are they called bodhicitta?  
Geshe -la: I don’t kno w why they are labelled as bodhicitta.  
 
Question: Does verbal isolation exist at the generation stage?  
Answer : There is no verbal isolation on the generation stage. There is physical isolation on the 
generation and completion stages but verbal isolation is necessarily  on the completion stage.  
 Things to remember : 
• the six levels of the completion stage  
• a rough idea of how one moves from physical isolation to verbal isolation to mental 
isolation  
 
Lesson  29          Page 197 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• what is a completion stage. You can understand this easily by t hinking of it as the time 
when the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel through the force of 
meditation.  
 
Now there are (more) things to discuss. When you return to the definition of the generation stage, does it have to be a yoga  that d oes not occur through the winds entering, abiding and 
dissolving in the central channel through the force of meditation? On pages 26 and 27 (of the root text), there is a lot of discussion on whether the winds actually enter, abide and dissolve in the cent ral channel through the force of meditation during the generation stage. The 
conclusion that is reached with respect to the two stages is that:  
• the completion stage is the time when the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel through the fo rce of meditation.  
• during the generation stage, the winds do not enter, abide and dissolve in the central 
channel through the force of meditation.

LESSON NO: 30   DATE: 21ST JULY 2009  
 I will talk a little bit about the coming retreat that you all will be going to this October. I actually intended to talk about this after this module but some people are wondering what is going to happen during the retreat and some people have some worrie s thinking that it is going to be 
difficult.  
 It is very important to be very clear whether you want to do the retreat or not. Going for the retreat is a personal decision. Will there be any difficulties during the retreat? Probably. I can’t think of anyo ne who will not meet with any difficulties. (But) if from your own side, you really 
have a strong heartfelt desire to do the retreat, then whatever difficulties (that may arise) will become  insignificant.   Are all these people who are going for this retr eat experienced meditators?  The answer is, 
“No.” There are not even some who are good at it. We are  all going to learn and take the first step.  
 Geshe -la: All are beginners . 
 There are some people who get discouraged by thinking, “There are people who are better 
than me in their studies and therefore, in terms of meditation, I will not be able to compare 
with them.”  
 
Geshe -la : There is no pervasion.  
 This retreat is not that difficult and challenging. This retreat is mainly on the stages of the path to enlightenment, i.e., it is meditation on the Lam Rim . The retreat will be conducted in silence. 
I guess some people will experience some difficulty with this. Don’t worry. You can always (give) feedback. There is also opportunities for relaxation.  
 Of co urse, during the retreat, we will be sitting down for some time and it is possible that you 
will experience some discomfort with your legs or your back may feel a little sore. But whatever it is, you should learn to put up with it. Whenever discomfort come s and if you 
immediately need to get up and rest, then you will never ever improve. But if it is really 
unbearable, then you have the choice to stretch your legs. It is not as if no matter what 
(happens), you are forced to sit there.  
 Since (there is) now  this opportunity for you to do retreat, you should take this  opportunity to 
do so. It is mentioned in the teachings, “Worldly activities will never come to an end on its own accord. When will they end? When we put an end to them. Otherwise they will neve r end by 
themselves.” It is good to remind ourselves (in this way). It is important to remember that the opportunity to do retreat is whenever all the conditions are there. Your body and mind are fit and you are in relatively good health. You should grasp this opportunity.  
 Remember this: One day, when things go wrong and the positive conditions are no longer 
 
Lesson  30          Page 199 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
there or one falls sick and facing problems and obstacles, then at that time, even if you wanted 
to do retreat, you will never be able to do so.   
 If you really want to do the retreat, you have to make the decision now. You should not think 
that there will be another opportunity in the future and you will do it in the future. I think then that probably will not happen. Does it mean that you can do thi s retreat better in the future 
but not now? I don’t think so because you are not getting younger but older by the day. With 
age, your faculties deteriorate.  
 
This retreat is basically an exercise for all us including the teacher (Geshe -la).  All of us are  
beginners and we are going there to learn. It is good if one can attend the retreat. If we go for the retreat with a clear decision in our mind and do so by setting aside whatever work or business for a period of time, even if it is (only) for two weeks, the retreat will be useful and beneficial.  
 Definitely there is a lot of benefit in doing the retreat even if one cannot meditate at all. Whenever we recite the prayers and do the practices based on the particular deity practice, reciting mantras and keep ing silent, definitely there will be a lot of benefit and no loss.  
 It is a meditation retreat on the Lam Rim  particularly the path that is shared with persons of 
small capacity. Those who are going for retreat should prepare by learning that particular 
section of the Lam Rim . 
 
My point is that you don’t have to worry unduly by thinking of all the possible difficulties. The 
retreat is meant to be relaxing. If you really meet with some difficulties, there are channels of communication open to you and there will be  people you can talk to.  
 Geshe -la: if you have any doubts, (thinking) that the retreat will be very difficult and impossible 
to do, please don’t think that. It is possible. For myself, I am doing this for the first time. I am a beginner. So don’t  worry. Everybody is the same. Nobody is perfect.  
 
 ********************************  
 
This exercise (the impromptu quiz) is to improve the standard of learning and is meant to facilitate and help you in your studies. It has to be a collective effort betwee n the students and 
the teacher.  
 If you think that this is just another exercise by closing the texts and take out a piece of paper and doing it for the sake of doing it, then this exercise will be pointless and  a waste of time. The point is to encourage you to prepare beforehand, to lea rn to anticipate (that certain) 
questions will be asked.  
 If you are able to answer the question that I am now posing to you that is good as it shows that you are studying. You should rejoice.  
 If you don’t know the answers to the questions, you should g o home and try to find the 
 
Lesson  30          Page 200 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
answers. It is only a couple of questions, not a lot. If you are not able to answer the questions, 
then you should generate the determination to want to know the answers, thinking, “If I were to be asked these questions again, I would definitely be able to answer them.” You should have that kind of determination.  
 Impromptu quiz:  
1. What is the definition of the completion stage?  
2. Write down the six levels of the completion stage without confusing the order.  
 Some of the comments tha t I make may sound prickly to your ears but these comments are 
mainly directed at those who are interested in their studies.  These comments are not directed at those who only  come here to listen (to the teachings).   
 Geshe -la: So don’t be afraid. So for  those who want to study, I try my best.  
 
Geshe -la: If you don’t get the correct answer, go back and read. Everything is in the text. If you 
can’t remember the order (of the six levels), correct it, remember it. If you don’t know the 
definition, go back and read the text and try to remember it.   
*************************  
It is insufficient to be able to write or memorise the definition of the completion stage. You 
need to understand what it means. The completion stage is basically a realisation that occurs  
through the winds entering, abiding and dissolving in the central channel. But that alone is not 
enough. It does not mean that if it is a realisation that occurs through the winds entering, 
abiding and dissolving in the central channel is necessarily a co mpletion stage. It (also) has to 
be a yoga in the continuum of a learner and this means, in accordance with this text, the completion stage does not exist in the continua of the buddhas.  
 The six levels of the completion stage in according with Arya Nagar juna’s tradition are :  
7. physical isolation (isolation of body)  
8. verbal isolation (isolation of speech)  
9. mental isolation (isolation of mind)  
10. illusory body  
11. clear light  
12. union  
 
• Physical isolation  refers to the time when the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central 
channel from both the upper and lower openings up to (just) before entering the level of the heart.  
• Verbal isolation  happens when the winds are able to enter into the heart level o f the 
central channel through the force of meditation.  
• Although the winds in the central channel have entered (the heart level of the central channel) and dissolved at the level of the heart chakra, the winds have yet to dissolve in the indestructible dro p. When the winds in the central channel have dissolved in the 
indestructible drop, then that is the time of mental isolation . 
 
Lesson  30          Page 201 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
It is important to pay attention and try to understand what is being said because I am presenting these different levels of the completion stage in a really simple manner.  
 Although we say the winds have dissolved in the indestructible drop when one first achieves mental isolation, most of the winds (but not all of them) have done so. Initially the pervasive 
wind has not dissolved in the indestructible drop. Comes the time when all  the winds, including 
the pervasive wind,  have dissolved in the indestructible drop during the death process, one then achieves the example clear light of the final mental isolation .  
 With that example clear light of the final mental isolation acting as the cause, when one arises from the example clear light, then the mind of black near - attainment of the reverse process 
manifests. At the same time, one attains the impure illusory body  which is the real body of the 
deity. This process is similar to when one enters the intermediate state and takes on the  subtle body of the intermediate state being.  
 Do not worry if you are not getting each and every single point. It is difficult to so now. This is just an introduction. All you need to know is just a rough idea of what happens after each particular level of the completion stage. Now we are at the fourth level, the illusory body.  
 What are the substantial cause and the co -operative condition of  this impure illusory body?:  
• The operative condition of this impure illusory body is the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
• The subtle wind that is the mount of this example clear  light of the final mental isolation is 
the substantial cause of the impure illusory body.  
 
What is the entity of that impure illusory body? The impure illusory body is form but it has the 
nature of wisdom. It is not consciousness. (Although it is form), it is not atomically established, i.e., it is not made up of particles or atoms. Here we are following the assertion that form and matter are not mutually inclusive.  
 Why is this illusory body impure? This is an impure illusory body because it is not purified of the obstructions to liberation.  
 What happens after that? Through meditation, the next realisation to be achieved is the meaning clear light. (Having achieved the impure illusory body), one meditates and  there will 
come the time when one is able to realise emptiness directly. This mind of clear light directly realising emptiness is the meaning clear light.  
  
When this meaning clear light manifests, this is the uninterrupted path of the Highest Yoga 
Tantric path of seeing, which is the direct antidote to all the obstructions to liberation. Having achieved the meaning clear light, one enters the Highest Yoga Tantric path of seeing, achieving the first ground and b ecomes a superior. (Here we are talking about the five paths in 
accordance with Highest Yoga Tantra).  
• The generation stage is the Highest Yoga Tantric path of accumulation . 
• The completion stage levels of physical, verbal and mental isolations and the impure 
 
Lesson  30          Page 202 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
illusory body are the Highest Yoga Tantric path of preparation.  
• When one achieves the meaning clear light, one achieves the Highest Yoga Tantric path of 
seeing  and one becomes a superior.  
 
There are clear lights that realise emptiness directly and there  are also clear lights that realise 
emptiness inferentially through the medium of a mental image.  
• The clear light that realises emptiness inferentially through a mental image is the example 
clear light.  
• The clear light that realises emptiness directly is the meaning clear light.  
I am putting this in simple terms for ease of understanding. I am sure that most of you have heard this term many times: The non -dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness  (the inseparable 
wisdom of bliss and emptiness)? What is this (refe rring to)? It refers to the example clear light 
and the meaning clear light. Of these two clear lights, the non- dual wisdom of bliss and 
emptiness really refers to the meaning clear light. For it to be non -dual, all duality must have 
subsided which only ha ppens when emptiness is realised directly. Therefore the non -dual 
wisdom of bliss and emptiness is referring to the meaning clear light.  
 When the subtle wind that is the mount of the meaning clear light stirs, one arises from that clear light and in the n ext moment, the mind of black near-  attainment is actualised and at that 
same time, one achieves the pure illusory body:  
• The co -operative condition for this pure illusory body is the meaning clear light.  
• The substantial cause for this pure illusory body i s the subtle wind that is the mount of the 
meaning clear light.  
 
Why is it called a pure illusory body? It is called the pure illusory body because it is purified of the obstructions to liberation, the meaning clear light being the direct antidote to them.  
 
There are two kinds of union: (1) the abandonment union and (2) the realisational union.  
One achieved the pure illusory body when the obstructions to liberation are abandoned. At this 
time, one achieves the union of the meaning clear light and the pure illusory body. This union is called the abandonment union.   
 One achieves the pure illusory body when the obstructions to liberation are abandoned. They are abandoned when one achieves the path of thorough release.  
• Earlier on, the meaning clear light is the uninterrupted path of the path of seeing and it is the direct antidote to the obstructions to liberation.  
• When one achieves the path of thorough release, one has abandoned the obstructions to liberation.  
• At that moment, one becomes an arhat.  
• Of the ten grounds, one has achieved the second ground . 
• Of the five paths, one has achieved the path of meditation.  
 When the pure illusory body is obtained, one becomes an arhat, i.e., one has abandoned all the obstructions  to liberation. After that realisation, one enters into meditation again and 
manifests clear light again. When the clear light is manifested again, (on the basis of one’s pure 
 
Lesson  30          Page 203 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
illusory body) one achieves the realisational union. Amongst the two unions, the  realisational 
union is the main union whereas the abandonment union is the ordinary union.  
 Why is it a union now? It is the union of the pure illusory body and with the pure mind of meaning clear light. The clear light that one who has the pure illusory  body manifests is the 
clear light at the end of the path of the learner  which is the antidote to the obstructions to 
omniscience. Different monastic literature has different views on this.   
 The clear light at the end of the path of the learner is the uninterrupted path of the path of 
meditation and is the direct antidote to the obstructions to omniscience. In the second 
moment, when the obstructions to omniscience are abandoned, one achieves the unification of no -more learning . 
 Geshe -la: At that time, y ou become Vajradhara!   
 The emptiness of that unification of no -more learning is the dharmakaya (truth body). It is an 
uncomposed phenomenon.  
 When we talk about clear light, there is:  
1. the objective clear light which refers to the emptiness of the unification of no -more 
learning and  
2. the subjective clear light which is the consciousness that takes that emptiness as its subject and is the wisdom truth body.  
 
The person that is imputed as a non -learner is the sambhogakaya and the  substantial cause for 
the sa mbhogakaya is the pure illusory body. When we say Vajradhara has the aspect of 
sambhogakaya, this is what we are talking about. We will go into this in more detail (later). (For now), if you have some idea on how these six levels of the completion stage ac cord with the 
five paths, then we have achieved (our goal for this session).

Information on the total solar eclipse on 22 July 2009:  
 Tomorrow is the total eclipse of the sun. According to the Tibetan calendar, as per Indian local time, the total ecli pse starts at 5.45am (8.15am Singapore   time) and by 6.43am (9.15am 
Singapore time), the eclipse will reach 90%. At 7.30am (10am Singapore time),  the total eclipse will happen.  
 It is mentioned in the calendar that any virtue that is created during the t ime of a solar eclipse 
is multiplied by 100,000 x 10 million, i.e., 1000 billion times.  
Why is this so?  
There is definitely a correlation between the external sun and moon and the internal subtle body (i.e., the channels, winds and drops). Of the various channels in the body:        ●   The central channel is called the mind (or Rahul) channel.  
• The right channel is called the speech (or sun) channel.   
• The left channel is called the body (or moon) channel.   
 I am not sure but during the eclipse of the sun, the winds on the right  channel may enter the central channel on its own accord. In the earlier lesson, we said that one of the signs of the winds entering the central channel is that the movement of the bre ath in the two nostrils is 
equal. I am not sure whether this happens to everyone but a lot of people have experienced 
 
Lesson  30          Page 205 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
this based on their own checking. During the eclipse, it seems that the flow of winds in both 
nostrils is of equal strength. On normal day s, the flow of winds in one nostril is usually stronger 
than the other. His Holiness the Dalai Lama has mentioned a couple of time that he has made several observations during the eclipse and he said that it is true that the flow of winds in both nostrils is of equal strength during the eclipse.  
 I guess when the breath from the nostrils, which basically comes from both channels is equal, 
then the mind is in equilibrium and is probably more stable. Of course then, any virtue that is 
performed with a stable mind will be more powerful. Maybe this is (the merit multiplying effect) 
can be explained. So try to engage in as much virtue as possible during the eclipse time. Try to wake up a little bit earlier tomorrow and make use of that time.

LESSON NO: 31   DATE: 23RD JULY 2009  
 
As usual, we will spend about five minutes in silence to do some reflection. The main thing is to 
bring joy and delight to the mind. We should remind ourselves how we have this precious 
human life, have faith in the Buddha and have some idea of what a buddha is. We (also) have some small wish of wanting to become a buddha and we have the opportunity to learn the 
path that will lead us to our goal. We should feel happy and rejoice.  
 
We will spend the next five minutes to uplift our minds, generating this se nse of joy and 
happiness and focus our minds single -pointedly to generate a positive motivation. The point is 
to try to transform the mind into a virtuous state.  
********************* 
Do you understand how the six levels of the completion stage are related to the five paths?  
 
Geshe -la: How many people understand the last lesson on Tuesday? I don’t know. Sometimes, it 
is difficult (for me) to understand. Maybe you don’t have enough time….I am not really sure 
why you are not getting it. If I repeat (everything) again, I am not very sure whether it is 
beneficial. You want me to repeat the same (thing) or not? If I repeat the same thing, it will be never - ending.  
 
It is said that the teacher who explains Tantra needs to generate into a wrathful deity!  
 
Geshe -la: You see there are so many wrathful deities in Highest Yoga Tantra.  Why do you think 
(this is so)? Why are they not smiling all the time?  
 When we consider the teachings of the Buddha, be it the Hinayana,  Mahayana or even 
Mahayana Tantra, all the instru ctions are for subduing or disciplining one’s body, speech and 
mind. By subduing one's body, speech and mind, one progresses, becoming better and one gets closer and closer to enlightenment.  
 The teachings in the explanation of the First Noble Truth, true  suffering, talks of four aspects: 
impermanence, misery, empty and selfless. These are instructions for subduing the coarse 
mind. There are many vows (of individual liberation) when one looks at the pratimoksha vows 
contained in the Hinayana system. There are eight divisions in which there are many different 
rules of conduct. These are all methods for disciplining or subduing one's coarse body and 
speech.  
In the Mahayana, there are meditations on love, compassion and, in particular, bodhichitta is taught. Again, these methods are primarily for subduing the mind but (as they) are built upon 
the earlier methods, Mahayana meditations are more refined, subtler and more extensive in nature.  
 The bodhisattva vows are taken in the Mahayana. (They also relate) to disciplining one's body and speech and give extensive explanations on how one should practise the six perfections. In the Mahayana system, in particular there is an explanation on how to unite the two -  
 
Lesson  31          Page 207 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
bodhicitta and the wisdom realising emptiness -  and there are also elaborate explanations on 
how the two obstructions are removed.  
  
There are four classes of Tantra. In the first three classes, there is the practice of taking desire 
or bliss into the path. Then, there is the practice of keeping the vows and commitments of 
Tantra and the practice of deity yoga. All of these are means of disciplining the body and  subduing one's physical actions or behaviour. It is the same in Highest Yoga Tantra but the 
methods there are more profound. Again, there is the pra ctice of deity yoga. All these are 
methods to subdue and discipline the mind.  
 
In Highest Yoga Tantra, through the practice of striking the vital points of the body, one attempts to control one’s subtle winds. In particular, Highest Yoga Tantra teaches the methods for gaining control over one's subtle body (i.e., the subtle wind) and the subtle mind and then transforming them into the entities of clear light and the illusory bodies.  Such  methods are peculiar to Highest Yoga Tantra and are not taught else where.  
 The actual substantial causes of the two bodies -  the truth body and the form body -  of the 
Buddha are none other than these two things, clear light and the illusory body. When one analyses what are the real substantial causes of the two bodies, these are the two things that one can point to and say, “These are  what will become the Buddha's body and mind.”  
• What is the substantial cause of the wisdom truth body? It is the wisdom at the end of the 
continuum of the learner.   
• The substantial cause of the for m body of the Buddha is the pure illusory body.  
 
It is important to have this overview of the entire buddhadharma and understand how it is 
basically the method for subduing one's body, speech and mind and becoming enlightened. In 
particular, when one unde rstands the essence of Highest Yoga Tantra, one will be able to really 
understand, at the end of the day, what is it that will move to enlightenment? What kind of 
mind will become the Buddha's mind? It is none other than the wisdom at the end of the contin uum of the learner -  clear light. It is the pure illusory body that moves to enlightenment 
and becomes the Buddha’s form body. It is only in Highest Yoga Tantra that you have this final view and explanation on what kind of mind and body, when subdued, move  to enlightenment.  
 Question: It seems like at every step of the way, there is this mother and son clear lights. …There is this mind that arose from the example clear light at the end of mental isolation and the subtle wind that is the mount of this is the  substantial cause for the impure 
illusory body. But at the same time, there is also the manifestation of the mind of black near -
attainment. So it seems like there are two entities with the son and the mother. Am I correct to say that or am I getting it wrong?  
Answer : Whether it is son or mother clear light, both are clear lights. We are talking about clear 
lights. (It has) nothing to do with the black near - attainment which is simultaneous with the 
achievement of the impure illusory body
. 37 
37 The mother clear light  refers to the clear light of death. Everyone will experience this mother clear light in the 
death process.  One can also manifest this clear light mind through the force of meditation. The clear light mind 
that manifests through the force of meditation is  termed son clear light . If one is able to do that during the death

Question:  Can I rephrase my question? Because the mind of black near -attainment of the 
reverse process comes from the clear light mind of death. And the substantial cause for the impure illusory body…..  
Ven. Gyurme:  You mean the one that is simultaneous with the ac hievement of the impure 
illusory body, isn’t it?  
Answer:  Referring to the mixing of the mother and son clear lights, that is the example clear 
light of the final mental isolation. What actually happens is that the mother clear light (which is the clear lig ht of death) is not allowed to manifest but instead the example clear light manifests. 
Once the example clear light manifests, the mother clear light is finished forever.  
 Question:  Then why is it called “mixing” in such a case rather than abandoning one or the other?  
Answer:  Literally, it is called, “mixing.” But whether it is mother clear light or son clear light, it is 
the subtle  mind that we are talking about. One realises emptiness, the other doesn’t realise 
emptiness….  
 Question: But it seems like th e mind of black near- attainment is also manifesting………..  
Answer:  When one arises from the example clear light, one arises into the mind of black near-
attainment of the reverse process. When the mind of black near -attainment manifests, the 
example clear is  no longer there so it is not simultaneous (with the arising of the pure illusory 
body). When one arises into the mind of black near -attainment, simultaneous with that, one 
arises as the impure illusory body.  
 Question:  Does this impure illusory body have  its own clear light because it is from there that 
the meaning clear light is derived? It is a substantial cause for the meaning clear light. This clear light is neither the example clear light at the end of mental isolation nor is it the meaning clear light. Is there a clear light that is the same entity as the impure illusory body? Is there any 
specific name for that?  
Geshe -la: If you  didn't understand what I said to you on Tuesday, then there is no way you can 
understand the question now.  
Ven. Gyurme : In short what kind of clear light  exists from the attainment of the impure illusory 
body up to and before the actualising of the meaning clear light? In between, there is a clear light. What is it called?   
Answer : It is called the example clear light o f mental isolation or simply, the example clear light. 
Remember there are two main clear lights: the example clear light and the meaning clear light.  
 Question:  Is this example clear light different from the example clear light at final mental 
isolation?  
Answer:  The example clear light of the final mental isolation is the basis for actualising the 
impure illusory body. Once the impure illusory body is achieved, that clear light is the example clear light.  
process, then instead of the clear light of death arising at the time of death, through the force of meditation one 
manifests that son clear light. When one is able to do that during the death dissolution process, this is called the 
mixing of mother and son clear lights .  (Pg. 4 of Lesson 29 )

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
************************  
We will talk a little abo ut father Tantra and mother Tantra to have some understanding of what 
they mean since we hear these terms quite often.  Mother Tantra  is Tantra that clearly 
explains the clear light and is also referred to as wisdom Tantra. Father Tantra  is Tantra that 
clearly explains the illusory body and is also referred to as method Tantra. They are not posited 
on the basis of gender but rather their area of emphasis.  
 
On page 28 of the root text, there is the heading, “ Enumeration and the definite order of the 
five st ages”  followed by their individual explanations. This is basically a different way of 
presenting what I have already explained so far.  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.2  THE MEANING OF EACH CLASSIFICATION  
 
There are two:  
1 Enumeration and definite order of the five stages  
2 The meaning of each  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.2.1  ENUMERATION AND DEFINITE ORDER OF THE FIVE STAGES  
 
In establishing the final object to be established/ attained, one needs these common and 
uncommon paths and one does not need other paths not included here. The establ ishment of 
non-learner’s union (mi slob pa’i zung ‘jug) explained in the general Highest Yoga Tantras, must 
be preceded by learners union combining (tshogs) both the meaning clear light (don gyi ‘od 
gsal) and pure illusory body (dag pa’i sgyu ma’i sku).(Pg. 27 -28 of the root text ) 
 
“In establishing the final object to be established” is referring basically to the union of no more 
learning. (What is presented) here is the mode of assertion of most Highest Yoga Tantras but the Kalachakra Tantra gives a different  explanation. Therefore, it says here, “i n the general 
Highest Yoga Tantras….. ”  
 In order to achieve the final object of attainment:  
• the non-learner's union  must be preceded by the attainment of learner's union.  
• What is the learner's union  (or realisational union)? This is the union of two things: the 
meaning clear light and pure illusory body. The meaning clear light and pure illusory body are the substantial causes of the Buddha's mind and body respectively.  
• The achievement of this learner's union must be preceded by the attainment of the pure 
illusory body . 
The pure illusory body must precede it because if the pure illusory body does not precede it, it’s not possible (mi rung) to obtain such a union. (However) the pure illusory body does  not 
need to precede the obtainment of mere union because the pure illusory body and ordinary (phal pa) union must be obtained simultaneously. (Pg. 28 of the root text )  
“The pure illusory body must precede it ”: “It” refers to the learner’s union. If the learner's union 
is the realisational union, then the “ ordinary union” is  referring to the abandonment union.  
 
 
Lesson  31          Page 210 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
With reference to the six levels of the completion stage, starting from physical, verbal and 
mental isolations followed by the illusory body, that  (illusory body) refers to the impure 
illusory body . After that is (the level of) clear light and this refers to the meaning clear light . 
This meaning clear light is the uninterrupted path  which is the direct antidote to the 
obstructions to liberation (afflictive obstructions). When these are removed, one achieves the 
state of abandonment of the obstructions to liberation and the pure illusory body .  The 
abandonment of the obstructions to liberation and achieving the pure illusory body are 
simultaneous.  
 
Although one has removed the obstructions to liberation, one still has to remove the obstructions to omniscience (knowledge obstructions). In order to abandon these obstructions, one needs to generate the direct antidote to them.  Therefore one needs to ma nifest the 
meaning clear light again. When one generates the uninterrupted path which is the direct antidote to the obstructions to omniscience , one achieves the learner's  or the realisational 
union.  
 When the uninterrupted path that is the direct antidote to obstructions to omniscience is generated, the obstructions to omniscience will be removed. Upon removal of the obstructions to omniscience, one will achieve the state of abandonment of the obstructions to omniscience which is enlightenment and one bec omes a buddha.  
 
If you can memorise what we have discussed here and are clear about (the process), it will be 
easy for you when we approach it from the top -down manner. The reason why I have 
explained to you from the bottom -up is because I thought that when I explain it top- down, you 
will not be confused. But this doesn't seem to be the case.  
 Geshe -la: I am not including so much detail as I don’t want people to get confused. Even within 
the first isolation, physical isolation, there are so many details. F or now, just understand how 
the path moves (from level to level).  
 The obtainment of the pure illusory body must precede the meaning clear light because the pure illusory body is derived from its perpetuating/ substantial  cause (nyer len ), the very subtle 
fundamental wind (gnyug rlung) that is the mount for the (mind of) actual  clear light.  
 The obtainment of the actual  clear light must be preceded by either amassing the (two) 
accumulations for three countless aeons or the illusory body o f the third stage as its equivalent. 
That is because it is impossible (mi rung) to manifest the actual [meaning]clear light that has the potency to quickly exhaust the very subtle obscurations (sgrib pa) that other paths do not have the potency to abandon when not preceded by either of these two . (Pg. 28 of the root 
text)   
 (Interpreter ’s correction and clarification: “ The obtainment of the pure illusory body must 
precede the meaning clear light ” should read as “ The obtainment of the pure illusory body must  
be preceded by the meaning clear light.”  “Actual clear light”  refers to the meaning clear light.)  
 Our earlier explanation of the six levels of the completion stage is from the perspective of 
 
Lesson  31          Page 211 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
someone who is definitely of the Highest Yoga Tantric lineage,  i.e., a practitioner who enters 
the Highest Yoga Tantric path right from the beginning. Specifically, (the explanation) is from 
the perspective of the practitioner of Guhyasamaja in accordance with the tradition of Arya Nagarjuna.  
 “The obtainment of the  meaning clear light must be preceded by either amassing the 
accumulations for three countless aeons or the illusory body of the third stage as its equivalent”: 
This means that the obtainment of the meaning clear light is not necessarily preceded by the 
impure illusory body.  
 
For example, Shakyamuni Buddha practised all the way up to the tenth ground but he was not on the path of Highest Yoga Tantra. He entered the path of Highest Yoga Tantra when he was on the tenth ground. He entered from the level of me aning clear light which means that 
realisation was not  preceded by the achievement of the impure illusory body.   The point is how does one achieve the meaning clear light? What must precede it? In order to achieve the meaning clear light, one must either have amassed the collection of method or 
wisdom (the two accumulations) over a period of three great countless eons or one must already have achieved the impure illusory body which can act as the substitute for those two collections.  
 
The reason is given  next: “That is because it is impossible (mi rung) to manifest the actual clear 
light that has the potency to quickly exhaust the very subtle obscurations (sgrib pa) that other 
paths do not have the potency to abandon when not preceded by either of these t wo.”  “These 
two”  means either the two collections or the impure illusory body.  
 
The obtainment of the illusory body of the third stage must be preceded by actual mental isolation (sems dben dngos) or an equivalent (dod thub) completion stage (practice) bec ause if 
it is not preceded by the dissolution of all karmic winds in the indestructible (drop) at the heart by the strength of meditation in accordance with the stages of death, obtainment of the illusory body exactly in accordance with (ji ma ji bzhin) the intermediate state is not possible (mi rung). (pg. 28 of the root text)  
 Is the obtainment of the impure illusory body necessarily preceded by the actual mental isolation? “ The obtainment of the illusory body of the third stage must be preceded by actual 
mental isolation  or an equivalent completion stage practice”  means that the obtainment of the 
illusory body of the third stage need not necessarily  be preceded by actual mental isolation. It 
can be preceded by some other equivalent completion stage practice.   
 For example, in the mother Tantras, different terminologies are used. In place of verbal isolation which is essentially the practice of vajra recitation, in the mother Tantras, there is the 
(practice of) tumo yoga. Based on this yoga , there are different ways to induce the four joys 
and so forth, causing the winds to enter into the heart level of the central channel. Those practices in the mothe r Tantras are methods to actualise the impure illusory body.  
 The obtainment of actual mental isolation or an equivalent (dod thub) completion stage

This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
(practice) must be preceded by either the branch of life -force exertion imputed by the name 
Vajra recitatio n verbal isolation’ (ngag dben rdo rje bzlas pa) or an equivalent (dod thub) 
completion stage (practice) because it is impossible (mi rung) to generate actual mental 
isolation or an equivalent (dod thub) completion stage (practice) without completely releasing 
(grol) the channel knots (rtsa mdud) of the heart by such a completion stage (practice).  
 To completely loosen ( grol) the channel knots at the heart in easy stages (bla rim) through such 
a yoga, it must be preceded by actual physical isolation or a co mpletion stage (practice) similar 
in type because it is more difficult to loosen the channel knot at the heart than the other channel knots and it is easier to gather (sdud) the winds in other places than to gather them at 
the heart.  
 To fully generate suc h a completion stage (practice) in one’s continuum, it must be preceded 
by meditation in four sessions on the gross and subtle generation stage which definitely matures the continuum for the generation of the full comprehension of the completion stage. And one must obtain the complete pure empowerment and properly protect the commitments 
(samaya) and vows.  
To fulfil the definition of being a vessel protecting the commitments and vows one must train in the stages of the common path of the practices (spyod pa’i lam) of the two bodhichittas and (their) branches. Since practices (spyod) which extract the benefit (bogs ldon) are represented 
(mtshon nas ston) by external life exertion (srog rtsol), such (practices) are definite in number 
for all the paths and they are also definite in (their) order. That exhausts their presentation (go 
bar zad). (Pgs. 28 –  29 of the root text)  
(Interpreter ’s correction: “ Since practices which extract the benefit…” sh ould read as  “Since the 
enhancing conducts ... ………”)  
 You must know (the following):  
• What are the five completion stages  
• The illusory bodies: The impure illusory body and the pure illusory body.  
o Why is one called impure and the other pure illusory body? The impure illusory 
body is so called because it is not purified of the afflictive obstructions and the pure illusory body is so called from the perspective of being purified of those obstructions.   
• What kind of clear lights are there and what differentiates them? There are mainly two:  
o The example clear light is a conceptual consciousness. It realises emptiness conceptually, but it doesn't realise emptiness directly.  
o The meaning clear light is a direct perceiver which directly perceives emptiness.  
• The learner's union is also divided into two:    
o The abandonment union which is a union of abandonment and the pure illusory body  
o The realisational union which is a union of realisation and the pure illuso ry body  
• The difference between mother and father Tantras:  
o Mother Tantras are those Tantras that clearly emphasise the clear light  
o Father Tantras are those Tantra that clearly emphasise the illusory body

LESSON NO: 32   DATE: 28TH JULY 2009   
 We will spend five minutes in silence to reflect on what we have studied so far particularly on the six levels of the completion stage: physical , verbal and  mental isolations, illusory body, 
clear light and union. In the next five minute, see whether you can come out with the reasons as to:  
• why the clear light must precede the attainment of union,  
• why the illusory body must precede the attainment  of clear light,  
• why the mental isolation must precede the attainment of illusory body and so forth, 
working backwards to physical isolation.  
 
*****************************  
We have been talking about the six levels of the completion stage. We have also ta lked a lot 
about the five completion stages. The meaning is the same. When we talk about the five 
completion stages, then instead of starting with physical isolation, we start with verbal isolation which will include physical isolation. When we condense th is further into four 
completion stages, then we start with mental isolation which subsumes physical and verbal isolations. These are the different ways of classifying the completion stage.   When we posit the five profound paths from the perspective of Guhyasamaja, we have five stages:  
1. the stage of mantra generation,  
2. the stage of mental isolation,  
3. the stage of illusory body,  
4. the stage of clear light and  
5. the stage of union.  
These stages are posited from the perspective of the entire path including the generation stage. (It does not mean that each stage) is directly related to one of the five paths.   
• The stage of mantra generation is the generation stage.  
• On page 28 of the ro ot text, “ the illusory body of the third stage ” is referring to the  third 
stage of illusory body amongst the five stages. When we refer to the illusory body, it does 
not necessarily mean the impure illusory body as it can also refer to the pure illusory bo dy. 
But when we say the illusory body  of the third stage, then that necessarily means the 
impure illusory body.  
• Likewise, the reference to the “meaning clear light of the fourth stage ,” refers to the fourth 
stage of clear light of these five stages. It do es not refer to all clear lights but to the 
meaning clear light that is the direct antidote to the obstructions to liberation.   
 There are six preparatory branches:  
1. Individual withdrawal  
2. Meditative stabilisation  
3. Lengthening vitality  
4. Retention  
5. Subsequent m indfulness  
 
Lesson  32          Page 215 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
6. Concentration  
 
• Individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation are included in  physical isolation.  
• Lengthening vitality is included in verbal isolation.  
• Retention is included in clear light  
• Subsequent mindfulness and concentration are included in union.  
 
Try to memorise these various classifications in order to get some rough idea of them.  
 Regarding this one says: It follows that it is pervasive that the learner’s union combining both the clear light and the illusory body is obtained before the obtainment of non -learner’s union, 
because just that is pervasive for obtaining the non -learner’s union explained in the general 
highest (yoga tantras).  
 Ngawang Palden: There is no pervasion but the  reason is easily (established). (Also your

38) is unacceptable because it is said that obtainment of the non- learner’s union of the 
Kalachakra system must be preceded by the branch of concentration combining both the body 
of empty form (stong gzugs kyi sku) and the immutable great bliss (mi ‘gyur ba!i bde chen) but 
it is not said that it must be preceded by the union combining both the clear light and the illusory body.  
 However, this is formulated as a basis for examination for those with analytical (wisdom), it is not to be taken as a final conclusion (mtha’ gcig tu).(Pg. 29 of the root text)   According to the Guhyasamaja system, there is a union of no -more learning that must be 
preceded by the union of learning, which is basically the union of the meaning clear light and the pure illusory body. But if one was asked, “In general, is the achievement of the union of no -
more learning necessarily preceded by the union of the meaning clear light and the pure 
illusory body?” The answer is, “No.”  
 Gener ally speaking, the achievement of the union of no -more learning is not necessarily  
preceded by the union of the meaning clear light and the pure illusory body. For example, according to the Kalachakra Tantra, in order to achieve the union of no -more learni ng one does 
not have to achieve the union of the meaning clear light and the pure illusory body. According to that  system, the union of no -more learning is preceded by the achievement of the 
concentration of the empty body and immutable bliss.

of the ultimate clear light (don gyi ‘od gsal) must be preceded by the obtainment of the impure illusory body because the Illuminating Lamp states:

Ngawang Palden: There is no pervasion because that is explained as the mode of progression 
on the paths of one of definite lineage of highest (yoga tantra). (Also your assertion) is unacc eptable because that being (possessing) the same continuum as the Teacher who had 
just obtained (thob ma thag) the meaning clear light, did not previously establish the impure illusory body because the Illuminating Lamp states:  
 
Bestowing the last two empo werments
39 without having previously bestowed the vase 
and the secret empowerment, and generating the last two stages without having 
previously generated (the stages) prior to and including the third stage illusory body, 
should be understood as special case s (dmigs bsal)...(Pgs. 29 –  30 of the root text)  
 
From the perspective of the Guhyasamaja Tantra, when we are talking about a person who 
enters the Tantric path from the beginning, this practitioner (does so) on the basis of having 
obtained a pure initiation and training in the coarse and subtle generation stages. After that, he 
progresses on the completion stages from the physical, verbal and mental isolations, illusory 
body, clear light up to union. This is a step -by-step process.  
 
But, in general, must the achievement of the meaning clear light necessarily  be preceded by 
the attainment of the illusory body of the third level? The answer is, “No” because Guru 
Shakyamuni Buddha, through practising the Paramitayana path, progressed all the way up to  
the tenth ground. On the tenth ground, he entered the Tantric path at the level of the meaning 
clear light. He did not achieve the illusory body of the third level prior to entering that level.  
 
In general, one must receive the four complete empowerments: vase, secret, knowledge and word initiations. But Buddha was a special case. He did not have to receive the vase and secret initiations and started off with receiving the knowledge and word initiations.  
 Again, one says: It follows that it is pervasive that in order to attain the impure illusory body, one must have previously established mental isolation (sems dben) because the Illuminating 
Lamp states:  
 
The wind and mind which are the basis for  establishing any such illusory body...  
 And:  
 
Since that generating the three (types) of primordial awareness of mental isolation are the mind and the wind as its mount, mental isolation must proceed the illusory body.  
 
Ngawang Palden: There is no pervasion because stating that mental isolation must precede the illusory body, and that vajra recitation verbal isolation must precede mental isolation is from the perspective of the mode of progress on the path of a disciple for whom the Guhyasamaja (tantra) was given. But that is not certain for the mode of progress of disciples of mother tantra because the (Illuminating Lamp) states:  
39 The last two empowerment: refers to wisdom and word empowerments.

The substitute for inducing mental isolation by the wind of vajra recitation is the yoga of 
candali (gtum mo). The substitute for mental isolation are the four joys (bga’ba bzhi), including great bliss (bde ba) derived from the winds entering and dissolving in the central channel dependent on meditating on tummo. Those two are derived from the 
tantra of the yogini. (Pg. 30 of the root text)   
When we talk about the achievement of the illusory body in general, must that necessarily be 
preceded by the verbal and mental isolations? It is not necessarily so. The practitioner of mother Tantra achieves the illusory body (through the prac tice of) tumo yoga. The yoga of 
inducing the four joys follows that. The tumo yoga is a substitute for verbal isolation. The four joys induced by the tumo yoga is the substitute for mental isolation.  
  
Therefore in the presentation of the grounds and paths of Yangchen Galo (dbyangs can dga’ blo) he states that other paths of tantra are subsumed (‘dus) in the five stages of Guhyasamaja 
(‘dus) by their nature, but they are not subsumed by means of actually being (those stages). 
They must be subsumed by being compatible in function (byed las mthun pa) . 
 2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2   THE MEANING OF EACH  
When we classify the completion stage of the superior sys tem (of Guhyasamaja), there are said 
to be) six (levels): (1) physical isolation, (2) verbal isolation, (3) mental isolation, (4) illusory 
body, (5) clear light, and (6) union. There are also said to be five and four levels and four 
branches of preparation  (sbyor ba’i yan lag). It is said that there is no contradiction since these 
(differences in number) are due to (different ways of) classification (dbye bsdu).  
In explaining a little of the meaning of each, there are two:  
1 The concentration of three isola tions  
2 The concentration of two isolations ( Pg. 30 of the root text )  
 (Interpreter ’s correction: “ The concentration of two isolations” should be amended to “ The 
concentration of the two truths .”) 
 The “two truths” are the conventional truth and the ultimate truth. In this context, the conventional truth refers to the illusory body and the ultimate truth refers to the clear light.  
 2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.1  THE CONCENTRATIONS OF THREE ISOLATIONS  
 has three  (subdivisions):  
1 The concentration of the vajra body: (ordinary) physical isolation (lus dben sku rdo rje’i ting nge ‘dzin)  
2 The concentration of the vajra speech: (ordinary) verbal isolation (ngag dbengsung rdo rje’i ting nge ‘dzin)  
3 The concentration  of the vajra mind: (ordinary) mental isolation (sems dben thugs rdo 
rje’i ting nge ‘dzin)

2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.1.1  THE CONCENTRATION OF  THE VAJRA BODY: (ORD INARY) PHYSICAL 
ISOLATION  
 
The completion stage (practice) generated by a yogi who has consummated the  subtle and 
gross generation stages of this system, from (that yogi) generating the primordial awareness of the empties (ston pa’i ye she) derived from causing the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in 
the central channel by meditating on the subtle (drop)  in the lower opening (of the central 
channel), up to but not including the generation of the primordial awareness that arises through dissolving of the upper and lower winds in the central channel at the heart is the nature of completion stage physical is olation. ( Pg. 31 of the root text)  
 If one were to ask, “What is physical isolation of the completion stage?” that is the definition. This paragraph talks about a yogi who meditates on the subtle in the lower opening of the central channel. Depending on the tantric system (that is being practised), there are different places where one can focus on to cause the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel. In Tibetan, it literally means “to strike at the different vital points of the body.”  
 
• According to the Arya tradition of Guhyasamaja, one meditates on the subtle in the lower 
opening of the central channel to cause the winds to enter into the central channel.  
• According to the Luipa tradition of Heruka Chakrasamvara, one strikes at the vital point of 
the navel to cause the winds to initially enter into the central channel.  
• According to the Ganapada tradition of Heruka Chakrasamvara, one strikes at the vital 
point of the heart to cause the winds to initially enter into the central channel.  
• In Kalachakra, one strikes at the vital points at the upper and lower openings of the central 
channel to cause the winds to gather and then enter into the central channel.  
 
You need to know that according to the Arya tradition of Guhyasamaja, one strikes at the vital 
point at the lower opening of the central channel to cause the winds to initially enter into the central channel. Geshe -la thinks what he is saying now may contradict what he had said earlier 
when he gave the rough overview of the completion sta ge.  
 
This yogi by meditating on the subtle in the lower opening of the central channel generates the 
wisdom (primordial awareness) of the empties derived from causing the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel. (In the Tibetan text, there is no mention of drop. It just says 
“subtle .”)  
 There are four such wisdoms:  
1. appearance (or empty)  
2. increase (or very or extremely empty)  
3. near- attainment (or great empty)  
4. clear light (or all empty)  
 When does physical isolation start? It starts whe n the wisdom of the empties are generated 
from the winds entering, abiding and dissolving in the central channel up to but not  including 
the generation of the wisdom that arises through dissolving of the upper and lower winds in 
the central channel at the heart.
