Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Question: When the four empties are generated, is this clear light (i.e. the fourth empty) the same as the clear light of death?  
Answer:  Although it is clear light, it is similar to but not the clear light of death.  
 There are eight signs of disso lution when the different elements of the body dissolve one after 
another. These eight signs start from the vision of the mirage up to the clear light (of death). 
There are the eight actual signs of dissolution and there are eight signs which are similar t o the 
eight actual signs of dissolution. These similar signs of dissolution appear on different occasions, for example, during the time of sleep when the coarser levels of consciousnesses dissolve. They also arise during sexual union between a man and woman and when one faints.  
 At the time of the basis, the eight actual signs of dissolution only occur at the time of death. At the time of the path, these eight actual signs of dissolution occur at the level of mental isolation.
4 
 During the completion stage of physical isolation, the wisdoms of the four empties arise but they are not the actual empties but something similar to the actual ones. Something that is similar to the example clear light also occurs at the completion stages  of physical, verbal and 
mental isolations.  
 
The wisdoms of the four empties do not occur naturally during physical, verbal isolation and 
mental isolations but they occur through the force of meditation. It is through familiarity that one comes after the other. (This is) unlike the four empties that arise naturally during sleep and not through the force of meditation.  
 Question:  Are the four empties of the basis and the four empties of the path the same?  
Answer : They are the same.  
 Question: How can it be  called “mixing of mother and son clear lights” when the mother clear 
light no longer manifests when the son clear light manifests?  
Answer: When we say the mother and son clear lights mix, this means that instead of allowing 
the most subtle mind of clear light to arise as it is in its natural state, one transforms that mind into the entity of the path.  
 
Geshe -la: The Swee Kim (name of student) who is a teenager and Swee Kim who is an uncle are 
mixed together. Swee Kim has many different parts; young, old, uncle, teenager…They are 
mixed with one another, isn’t it? When the teenager Swee Kim is no longer there any more, only 
uncle Swee Kim remains. The mother clear light and son clear light are something like that. It is a continuum of the mind but we don’t r eally label the changes but the continuum is the same.  
 Question: Is the emptiness of the mother clear light the basis for the truth body ? 
Answer : The most subtle wind and mind continue up to enlightenment. When you look at them 
from the perspective of a c ontinuum, the most subtle wind and mind are the cause of the two 
bodies. But the substantial cause is the meaning clear light and the pure illusory body. It is the

This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
same as meditating on bodhicitta or impermanence, doing prayers and prostrations. They are 
the causes for enlightenment. Here, we are looking at the direct cause.  
 
• You have to remember the stages of the profound paths. In the text, when you see the 
words, “ the illusory body of the third level”  and the “ meaning clear light of the fourth level,”  
you need to understand what they are referring to. That is why I talked about the stages of 
the profound paths.  
• You should be able to list the six preparatory branches.  
• You should know what physical isolation is and understand its definition and entity.

LESSON NO: 33   DATE: 30TH JULY 2009   
 We will spend the next five minutes to adjust our motivation for participating  in this session.  
 All of us have received teachings from the Lam Rim , the stages of the path to enlightenment. 
We are advised there that whenever we listen to the teachings of the Buddha, we should to do 
so by abandoning the three faults of the vessel and cultivating the six recognitions or attitudes. 
We know what these ar e but the point is to see whether we can actually put them into practice 
whenever we listen to the teachings.  
 Do we actually listen by abandoning the three faults of the vessel? Do we actually cultivate those six attitudes? This is something we should check up for ourselves.  
 
When, prior to listening to the teachings, one has abandoned the three faults of the vessel and 
cultivated the six attitudes, it is definite that imprints will be placed on one’s mental continuum. (With this preparation), one’s mind will become closer to the Dharma and one also develops 
the affinity with the instructor. One will develop the mind that seeks the Dharma and be able to generate the thought, “The Dharma is really a wonderful thing.” It is extremely important to always list en to the teachings by abandoning the three faults of the vessel and cultivating the 
six attitudes, making sure these are always done. Why? Because when one listens to the 
teachings correctly:  
• firstly, one will place many imprints that arise from hearing i n one’s  mental continuum;  
• secondly, one will develop interest and desire in seeking the Dharma and  
• thirdly, one will develop the mind of delight and generate affinity with the instructor.  
 
When we don’t listen to the teachings with these attitudes, I think it is difficult for these three things to happen. In the end, who profits from listening to the teachings with these helpful attitudes and through abandoning those faults? None other than ourselves, no one else. We usually consider the I or self to be very important, therefore, it is important that the I  profits. In order for that to happen, this is how we must think when we listen to the teachings.  
 Impromptu Quiz:  
1) What is the definition of physical isolation in the completion stage? If possible, you should write the definition as it is found in the text.  
2) What are the six preparatory branches?  
 If you know the answers, that is very good. But if you don’t, when you go home, make sure you know the answers so that if you were asked the same questions again the next time, you should have the confidence to be able to produce the correct answers.  
 
**************************************  
 There are six preparatory branches:  
7. Individual withdrawal  
 
Lesson  33          Page 222 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
8. Meditative stabilisation  
9. Life force exertion (In this context,  it is translated as lengthening vitality)  
10. Retention  
11. Subsequent mindfulness  
12. Concentration  
• Individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation are included in physical isolation.  
• Lengthening vitality is included in verbal isolation.  
• Retention is included i n clear light  
• Subsequent mindfulness and concentration are included in union.  
 
Memorise this and if you can, think about why they are related to (these specific levels of the completion stage). The reasons will come later.  
 If classified there are two:  
1 Individual withdrawal (sor sdud) in post equipoise (rjes thob) where all phenomena 
such as objects, (sense) powers, etc., appear as the manifestation (rnam ‘gyur) of bliss and emptiness ( bdestong gyi rtam ‘rgyud)  
2 Absorption where the deities appear as the manifestation (rnam ‘gyur) of bliss and emptiness. (Pg. 31 of the root text)  
 
In brief, the complete stage of physical isolation is divided into two:  
1) Individual withdrawal  
2) Meditative stabilisation  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.1.1.1  INDIVIDUAL WITHDRAWA L IN POST EQUIPOISE WHERE ALL 
PHENOMENA APPEAR AS THE MANIFESTATION OF  BLISS AND EMPTINESS  
 The reason why all appearance is sealed (rgyas btab) by the bliss and emptiness of equipoise is because when one has completed the analysis of view, one knows that emptiness is the essence of that subject which is the empty basis (stong gzhi) and the subject is the manifestation (rnam ‘gyur) of that emptines s. This is common in sutra and tantra.  
 In this context, just as subjects are (posited as) the manifestation (rnam ‘gyur) of emptiness through the point of being resolute (mos) in them being the single taste of bliss and emptiness, 
they are posited also as  the manifestation (rnam (gyur) of bliss by the power of that resolution 
(mos). That is because in taking them as the path of great bliss, it is indispensable they function 
to assist (grogs) to ignite (‘ bar) as bliss all that appears, through the power of familiarity with 
that. (Pg. 31 of the root text) 
 (Interpreter ’s clarification: “ The empty basis ” can be understood as the “basis of emptiness.”)  
 Individual withdrawal  is posited in the context of post- meditative equipoise.  
 
• Meditating on the subtle at the lower opening of the central channel causes one of the 
winds called the downward voiding wind (also translated as “downward dispelling wind”) to 
 
Lesson  33          Page 223 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
reverse, i.e., change direction and move upwards.  
• The reversal of the downward voiding wind causes the tumo  at the navel to ignite.  
• The blazing of the tumo causes the bodhicitta at the crown to melt and drip and gradually 
the four joys are generated.  
• The melting and flowing down of the white bodhicitta in the central channel act as the conditions for one’s mind to be transformed into the entity of bliss. One then uses that bliss to ascertain emptiness.  
• One enters into meditative equipoise on this non- dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness. At 
this time, it is not a fully qualified non -dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness but nevertheless 
one enters into meditative equipoise on this wisdom.  
This is the main practice of the completion stage. When we say, “To take desire into the path”, 
from one point of view, this is what it means, i.e., first one generates the bliss arising from the 
melting of the bodhicitta and one uses that bliss to realise emptiness.  
 
When one arises from that meditative equipoise on the non-dual wisdom of bliss and 
emptiness, the sense powers start to manifest followed by the manifestation of the sense consciousnesses. When the sense consciousnesses manifest, one perceives the objects of those 
sense consciousnesses -  form, sound, smell, t aste and touch.  
 
In post -meditative equipoise, one remembers that non- dual bliss and emptiness that was 
experienced during meditative equipoise . Then, one trains to see all those sense objects as the 
manifestation of emptiness. That emptiness is taken as the object of the mind of bliss. In short, 
in post -meditative equipoise, one sees everything in the nature of emptiness and trains to see 
that emptiness as the display (or sport) of bliss.  
  
During meditative equipoise, one generates the wisdom of bliss and emptiness. In the post-meditative equipoise, one trains to see everything that appears to one’s sense consciousnesses as the manifestation of bliss and emptiness such that whatever one sees assist in the generation of the wisdom of great bliss. This practice in post -meditative equipoise of seeing 
everything that appears as the manifestation of bliss and emptiness is something that ignites the wisdom of great bliss. That is called individual withdrawal.  
 Question:  Where does the downward dispelling wind e nter the central channel? Does it enter 
from the bottom or does it enter from the navel chakra?  
Answer : It enters from the bottom (i.e., lower opening).  
 What do we mean when we say individual withdrawal of the physical isolation? You must not forget what comes before (individual withdrawal):  
• Through meditating on the subtle at the lower opening of the central channel, that causes 
the downward dispelling w ind to reverse (i.e., go up) and causes the tumo to blaze.  
• The blazing of the tumo melts the bodhicitta at the crown and through melting and 
dripping of that bodhichitta one develops the wisdom of great bliss.  
• One then uses the wisdom of great bliss to a scertain emptiness in meditative equipoise.  
• When one arises from meditative equipoise, all the sense consciousnesses will start to 
function again. Objects will appear to those consciousnesses again and whatever appear -  form, 
 
Lesson  33          Page 224 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
sound, smell, taste and touch  - one trains by remembering one’s experience in meditative 
equipoise.  
• By remembering that experience, in post -meditative equipoise, whatever appears, one 
trains to see it as the manifestation of bliss and emptiness. By training in such a way, it doesn’t 
matter what appears to your sense consciousnesses. Everything that appea rs assist in the 
blazing of the wisdom of great bliss. That is the individual withdrawal.  
 Question: What is the meaning of “withdrawal” in “individual withdrawal”? Does it mean withdrawing the sense consciousnesses from ordinary appearance?  
Answer : Indiv idual withdrawal is a practice of seeing everything in post - meditative equipoise 
as the manifestation of emptiness and bliss. Although one ascertains the emptiness of form, sound, smell, taste and touch, what essentially appears to oneself is still the or dinary form (of 
these objects).     
 Question: The uncommon objects of abandonment of the generation stage are ordinary appearance and ordinary grasping. So if one is already on the completion stage, one has already consummated the generation stage. That b eing the case, won’t one have already 
overcome those two abandonments?  
Answer : The ordinary appearance and ordinary grasping are also the objects of abandonment 
of the completion stage. In Tantra, ordinary appearance is the knowledge obstruction and ordina ry concept is the afflictive obstruction. That being the case, obviously one has not yet 
abandoned ordinary appearance.  
 In post -meditative equipoise, one trains in whatever appearing to oneself as the manifestation 
of emptiness. That emptiness is taken as  the object by the wisdom of great bliss. In short, 
individual withdrawal trains one to take everything that appears to oneself as an assisting factor in the generation of the wisdom of great bliss.  
 The reason why all appearance is sealed (rgyas btab) by the bliss and emptiness of equipoise is 
because when one has completed the analysis of view, one knows that emptiness is the essence of that subject which is the empty basis (stong gzhi) and the subject is the 
manifestation (rnam ‘gyur) of that emptiness. This is common in sutra and tantra. (Pg. 31 of the 
root text)  
 
(Interpreter ’s correction: “The reason why all appearance is sealed (rgyas btab) by the bliss and 
emptiness of equipoise ” should read as “ The reason why all appearance is sealed (rgyas btab) 
by the bliss and emptiness of equipoise through remembering that is because….”   The words, 
“through remembering that ,” which appears in the Tibetan text are missing from the 
translation in the root text. The “subject” here can be taken to mean “object” and is not referring to the consciousness).  
 When one has completed the analysis of view, simply thinking about  dependent origination, one realises everything is empty. Just by thinking about emptiness, one immediately understands dependent origination. The analysis of view is completed when both the understanding of dependent origination and emptiness complement one another.  
 
 
Lesson  33          Page 225 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
In this context, just as subjects are (posited as) the manifestation (rnam ‘gyur ) of emptiness 
through the point of being resolute (mos) in them being the single taste of bliss and emptiness, 
they are posited also as the manifestation (rnam (gyur) of bliss by the power of that resolution 
(mos). That is because in taking them as the pa th of great bliss, it is indispensable they function 
to assist (grogs) to ignite (‘ bar) as bliss all that appears, through the power of familiarity with 
that. (Pg. 31 of the root text) 
 
(Interpreter ’s clarification: “Resolute” or “resolution” can be unders tood as “believed” or 
“belief.”)   
 
Here, it is just a belief, not a reality that emptiness and bliss have become of a single taste. That bliss arises from the melting of the bodhicitta. This wisdom of great bliss takes emptiness as its object and realises  emptiness. This wisdom of great bliss and emptiness are not a single taste 
yet. It is only a belief that these two are of a single taste because we are still on the path of preparation.  
 When we say bliss and emptiness are of a single taste, it is like po uring  water into water which 
is completely of one taste. As I have mentioned earlier, it is only when one has achieved the meaning clear light that one achieves a fully qualified non -dual bliss and emptiness which is of 
a single taste.  Here, at (the leve l of) physical isolation on the completion stage, one is still on 
the Highest Yoga Tantra’s path of preparation. So that bliss and emptiness being of a single 
taste is only at the level of belief.  
 
In the Perfection vehicle’s path of preparation, when one enters into meditative equipoise on 
emptiness, one realises emptiness. On that path of preparation, the duality of subject and object has not subsided. That duality of subject and object only subsides when one realises emptiness directly.  
 In meditative equipoise, although the wisdom of great bliss and emptiness has not yet become a single taste, we believe that to be so. When one is in post -meditative equipoise, one trains to 
see everything that appears as the manifestation of that wisdom of great bliss and emptiness. That is individual withdrawal.   Meditative stabilisation  refers to the training to see this wisdom of great bliss and emptiness 
arising as deities.  
 During individual withdrawal at post -meditative equipoise, one sees whatever that appears to 
be the manifestation of bliss and emptiness. But, during  meditative stabilisation, one trains by causing that bliss and emptiness to arise as deities. These deities that are generated are the manifestation of the wisdom of great bliss and emptiness.  
 In summary:  
• Individual withdrawal is the yoga that utilises whatever appears to the sense consciousnesses as the means to ignite the wisdom of great bliss. Individual withdrawal is the practice during the post -meditative session.  
• Meditative stabilisation is a  meditation that visualises whatever that appears in the 
 
Lesson  33          Page 226 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
aspects of the five buddha lineages. This practice happens while one is in meditative 
equipoise.  
 For our purposes, it is sufficient for you to have a rough idea of what individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation are. For now, we do not need to know the details of these two. With 
this (commentary) as the basis, go back and reread the text and see what I have explained is what the text is saying.  
 
Question:  Can I say that the meditative stabilisation at the generation stage where one 
develops divine pride and clear appearance of sentient beings as deities is done at the level of 
the mental consciousness whereas this meditative stabilisation at the completion  stage is done 
at the level of the sense consciousness?   
Ven. Gyurme : They are both done in meditative equipoise  
Question: If that is the case, what is the difference between the two?  
Answer:  The meditative stabilisation where all deities appear as the manifestation of bliss and 
emptiness is done with the mental consciousness. It is not (the same) meditation (as) when you see things appearing to your sense consciousnesses as deities.  
 Question:  Seeing sentient beings and the environment as deities - if thi s practice is common to 
both the generation and completion stages, what is the difference between these two?  
Answer:  The meditation of eliciting a clear appearance and then generating divine pride is 
common to both generation and completion stages. There i s a difference (between the two), 
because the exalted wisdom generated on the completion stage arises due to the winds entering, abiding and dissolving in the central channel whereas the visualisation done on the 
generation stage is just at the level of vi sualisation which did not happen due to the winds 
entering, abiding and dissolving in the central channel.  
 There is physical isolation in the generation stage and there is physical isolation in the completion stage. But what we are talking here is the phy sical isolation of the completion stage 
which is divided into two divisions.  
1) Individual withdrawal in the post -meditative equipoise where all phenomena such as 
objects, sense powers, etc., appear as the manifestations  of bliss and emptiness.  
2) Meditative stabilisation where the deities appear as the manifestations of bliss and 
emptiness.  
Question:  What is the physical isolation on the generation stage?  
Answer:  It is the meditation of oneself as the deity during the generation stage.  
 We will try to complete the physical isolation in the completion stage in the next class.  
 2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.1.1.2  ABSORPTION WHERE THE DEITIES APPEAR AS TH E MANIFESTATION  OF 
BLISS AND EMPTINESS  
 has five (types):  
1 The investigation (sgom pa’i rtog) meditating without detailed examination on 
the bodies of deities, the object aspects (gzungs rnam) of undifferentiable bliss and emptiness.

2 The analysis meditating with detailed examination.  
3 The mental bliss (yid bde) concordant with the primordial awareness of 
undifferentiable bliss and emptiness

From among those, the three: individual withdrawal, investigation and analysis are post

equipoise physical isolation meditating on the ultimate deity. (Pgs. 31- 32 of the root text)  
 
From today’s class, you should understand that:  
• Physical isolation has two divisions: individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation.  
• Meditative stabilisation has five divisions: investigation, analysis, joy, bliss and 
concentration  
 
Whatever we study here, all the definitions are according to the Arya Na garjuna’s tradition of 
Guhyasamaja.  
 The subject matter is not easy but we have to try without giving up. Even when you have no teeth, when you are hungry, you still have to chew with your gums!

LESSON NO: 34   DATE: 4TH AUGUST 2009  
 
(Impromptu Quiz)  
1) Write down the six preparatory branches in the correct order.  
2) What do individual withdrawal and mental stabilisation refer to?  
3) How are the six completion stages condensed into the six preparatory branches?  
If you are able to answer these questions, that is good. But if you don’t know the answers, you should try to find out the answers when you go home. It is important you remember these 
points because they are the bases for discussion.  
 
In general, in whate ver one does, if one gives up or gets discouraged, then nothing can be 
accomplished. Likewise it is not beneficial to (allow oneself) to be discouraged with one’s 
studies. It is good if one’s interest in the Dharma (can be) like the desire of a thirsty per son for 
water. When one’s interest in the Dharma is that strong, it will benefit one’s studies and 
practice. When  one fervently seeks the teachings, one will definitely experience the taste of 
the Dharma and one will see the teaching and the teacher in a very positive light.  
 
Having such a fervent desire (for the Dharma) is important. The reason one is stuck is because of the lack of this real desire for the Dharma. Because one’s desire for the Dharma is not as strong as that of a thirsty person thirsting  for water,  one doesn’t really value the teachings. If 
one doesn’t value the teachings, then of course one doesn’t value the teacher. One doesn’t see the teachings and the teacher as something very special.  
 When one doesn’t have strong desire for the Dha rma, I think it is very difficult to have real faith 
in and respect towards the guru, the teacher. Real faith in the teacher first comes from valuing 
the Dharma and seeing its benefits and preciousness. Then naturally one will see the preciousness of the teacher. When one values the teacher, one’s practice of guru devotion will be good and one’s reliance on the virtuous friend will turn out well. Otherwise,  without this interest in the Dharma, one’s guru devotion or reliance on the spiritual master will no t turn out 
well.  
 
Faith in the guru does not refer to the kind of faith that arises in the mind because one likes the 
way the guru looks at one or the guru’s external appearance and behaviour. That kind of faith 
is like the mindset of a child. When one’s faith in the teacher is so shallow, then as soon as that 
teacher does something one doesn’t like –  showing one a black face or speaking harshly to one 
-  immediately, the faith is gone. Why? Because the faith originates from just that - a certain 
facial ex pression, a certain manner of  speaking. As soon as that changes, one’s faith changes 
and is lost. This is something one must really understand and one should not make such a   
mistake.  
 
In the first place, do you need the Dharma? If you need the Dharma, then you have to seek it 
out. If you really want the Dharma, then you need to rely on the teacher. When the teacher is 
able to guide and teach you, you will benefit and o f course, faith will arise in that (teacher) .  
 It is important to first have the mind  that seeks the teachings. (Once you have that mindset), 
 
Lesson  34          Page 229 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
then you should pursue your studies continuously without discouragement.  
 
Even when I look at and read this text, there are many things that I still don’t understand.  Are there things that I do understand? Yes, of course there are things that I understand. But there are also many things that I don’t understand. But, slowly, understanding will arise. It takes time.  
 
****************************  
 In the physical isolation of the completion stage:  
• One is able to cause the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel by 
meditating on the subtle at the lower opening of the central channel.  
• As the winds enter the central channel, the tumo at the navel is ignited.  
• Through the blazing of the t umo, the white bodhicitta at the crown starts to melt and drip.  
• The mind is transformed into the entity of bliss and one uses that bliss to ascertain emptiness by entering into meditative equipoise and focussing on that. This wisdom of great bliss ascertaining emptiness is the actual realisation  of the completion stage.  
• When one arises from that session, the sense consciousnesses manifest again and the 
objects of the sense consciousnesses appear:  form, sound, smell, taste and touch.  
• During the post -meditative session, one trains to view everything that appears as  the 
manifestation of emptiness. Whatever appears is empty (“empty” literally means great 
bliss). That emptiness is taken as the object by the wisdom of great bliss. The (purpose of doing so) is to generate the non -dual wisdom of great bliss and emptiness.   
• When that is done well, one trains in causing that non- dual wisdom of great bliss and 
emptiness to appear as deities.  
 In short, this practice of seeing everything that appears as the manifestation of emptiness during the post- meditative equipoise is called individual withdrawal. The practice of causing 
that wisdom of great bliss and emptiness to arise as deities is called meditative stabilisation.  
 
When we look at the etymology of individual withdrawal, “individual” refers to those objects 
that appear to the individual sense consciousnesses: form, sound, smell, taste and touch. “Withdrawal” is the training of withdrawing all that appears and condensing them into the 
entity of bliss and emptiness.  
 
Meditative stabilisation is the meditation on the five tathagatas.  
 Individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation are the two divisions of physical isolation of the completion stage and are condensed into the completion stage level of physical isolation.  
 ABSORPTION WHERE THE DEITIES APPEAR AS THE MANIFE STATION OF BLISS AND EMPTINESS  
has five (types):  
1 The investigation (sgom pa’i rtog) meditating without detailed examination on 
the bodies of deities, the object aspects (gzungs rnam) of undifferentiable bliss and emptiness.  
2 The analysis meditating with  detailed examination.  
 
Lesson  34          Page 230 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
3 The mental bliss (yid bde) concordant with the primordial awareness of 
undifferentiable bliss and emptiness  
4 The bliss of pliancy (shin sbyangs) concordant with that  
5 The concentration of single pointed mind (sems rtse gcig pa’i ting nge ‘dzin)  
From among those, the three: individual withdrawal, investigation and analysis are post 
equipoise physical isolation and the (following) three: joy41, bliss and concentration are 
equipoise physical isolation meditating on the ultimate deity.  (Pgs. 31 –  32 of the root text )  
(Interpreter ’s suggestion: “ the object aspects (gzungs rnam) of undifferentiable  
bliss and emptiness” can be read as “ the apprehended aspects of  non-dual bliss and 
emptiness.” “C oncordant ” to be replaced by “concomitant.”)  
 
The classification of physical isolation is the physical isolation of:  
The 100 immaculate lineages  
The 5 lineages of those  
The 3 secret lineages  
The 1 extremely secret lineage  
 
The 100 immaculate lineages: One meditates on the twenty gross (phenomena): the five 
aggregates, the four elements, the six bases (of perception) such as the eye and so on and the 
five such as forms, sounds and so on, through classifying them in five components, and one 
meditates on the twenty deities: the five Tathagatas, the four consorts, the six Bodhisattvas 
such as Kshitagarba and so on, and the five such as Rupa Devi and so through classifying them 
in five lineages. ( Pg. 32 of the root text)   
 Between investigation and analysis, this physical isolation of the100 immaculate lineages is 
primarily analysis  which is a detailed meditation on the deities.  
 There are 20 bases of isolation. One classifies them into five components and meditates on 
them by classifying them into five lineages.  
 Investigation  does not meditate on the deities in such a detailed way. Investigation meditates 
by condensing everything into either five lineages or three secret lineages or one extremely secret lineage.  
• Analysis is the detailed examination of the deities and  
• Investigation is a less detailed examination of the deities.  
Therefore, the physical isolation of the 100 immaculate lineages is analysis while the remaining 
four lineages come under investigation.  
 When we lo ok at the physical isolation of the100 immaculate lineages, how does one 
meditate? The basis of the isolation is the 20 gross phenomena. Of those, there are five 
aggregates. Of those aggregates, for example, let’s take the aggregate of form (Vairochana). 
We can relate the aggregate of form to the five tathagata lineages:  
41 Joy here refers to the mental bliss mentioned above.  
 
Lesson  34          Page 231 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment                                                                   
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
1. Vairochana of Vairochana 
2. Akshobhya of Vairochana,  
3. Ratnasambhava of Vairochana,  
4. Amitabha of Vairochana and  
5. Amoghasiddhi of Vairochana  
 
This particular way of classification is according to Guhyasamaja but when  we look at the 
Arya’s tradition of Guhyasamaja, there are 32 deities in the mandala. When we multiply the 32 
deities by the five tathagata lineages, there should be 160 deities, bu t here there are only 100 
immaculate lineages. Why is that so? The reason is given in the following paragraph.  
 From among the thirty -two deities, the ten Wrathful Ones, Maitreya and Samantabhadra are 
not explained since they are not explicitly and clearly stated in the Root Tantra. In general there are thirty -two deities of physical isolation because the complete thirty -two deities are 
stated in the context of physical isolation explained secretly in the index [prologue of the root tantra] (gleng gzhi). T his should be understood from the commentary in the annotations 
(mchan).  (Pg. 32 of the root text )  
 (Ven Gyurme:  The Tibetan text reads, “ This way of explanation is from the perspective of not 
including the ten Wrathful Ones, Maitreya and Samantabhadra because they are not explicitly and clearly stated in the Root Tantra.)  
 According to the Arya tradition of Guhyasamaja, the 32 deities of Guhyasamaja are as follows:  
• Five Tathagatas: Akshobhya, Vairochana, Ratnasambhava, Amitabha and Amoghasiddhi  
• Four mot hers: Lochana, Mamaki, Pandaravasin and Tara  
• Eight bodhisattvas: Ksitigarbha, Vajrapani, Khagarbha, Lokeshvara, Manjushri, Sarvanirvana 
Viskambini, Samantabhadra and Maitreya  
• Five vajra goddesses: Rupa Vajra, Shabda Vajra, Gandhe Vajra, Rasa Vajra and Sparsha Vajra  
• Ten wrathful ones: Yamantakrit, Prajnatakrit, Hayagriva , Vighnantakrit,  Achala, Takkiraja, Nilandanda, Mahabala, Ushnishachakravartin and Sumbharaja  
 
When we classify each of these 32 deities into the five tathagata lineages, there should be 16 0 
deities instead of 100. Why is it that only 100 immaculate lineages are mentioned here? This is because in the Root Tantra of Guhyasamaja, only 20 of the 32 deities are explicitly mentioned and the remainder 12 deities are not clearly stated. The 12 deities that are not explicitly mentioned are the ten w rathful ones, Maitreya and Samantabhadra. Since only 20 deities are 
explicitly mentioned in the Root Tantra of Guhyasamaja, therefore, there is only the classification of the 100 immaculate lineages.  
 The explanation is given in the text. What I have said is another way of presenting that 
explanation. (The main thing is) you (need to) know how to arrive at the 100 immaculate 
lineages instead of the actual 160 lineages and why only the 100 immaculate lineag es are 
mentioned.  
 
 
Lesson  34          Page 232 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
I think the 100 immaculate lineages are mainly a classification for analysis. Individual 
withdrawal is not meditation on deities possessing faces and hands and the three: joy, bliss and concentration do possess the ultimate aspect (of the deities). ( Pg. 32 of the root text )  
 Analysis here refers to the analysis from the classification of analysis and investigation and not to the general term, analysis.  
 
Investigation is resolution (mos) in the aspects of the five lineages or Vajradhara through 
condensing the bases of purification in five without applying the twenty gross components to 
each of the twenty deities. That is because Gyaltsab Je’s Notes on the Five Stages states:  
 
Investigation  is resolution in being the mere aspect of Vajradhara and detailed 
contemplation applying individual deities to each object and power is analysis. ( Pg. 32 
of the root text)  
 Meditative stabilisation of physical isolation has five divisions. Of these five,   we have analysis which is the detailed examination of the deities by classifying each of the 20 deities (that are explicitly mentioned in the Root Tantra of Guhyasamaja) into the five tathagata lineages 
resulting in 100 deities.  
 Investigation is (a les s) detailed examination of the deities. It basically condenses the 100 
immaculate lineages into five tathagata lineages and meditates on that. To substantiate this point, the author quotes from Gyaltsab Je’s Notes on the Five Stages . 
 
• The five tathagata li neages are essentially the purified aggregates.  
• When they are condensed (further), we arrive at the three secret lineages which are 
essentially the three deities that are the purified factors of body, speech and mind  
• When these three secret lineages are f urther condensed, we have one  extremely secret 
lineage which is basically Vajradhara, the purified factor of the mind.   
In short, all these lineages can be condensed into Vajradhara. One expands on the classification 
from Vajradhara into the three secret  lineages, to the five lineages and then up to the 100 
lineages.  
 When one condenses the 100 lineages into the five lineages, one meditates on whatever appears as the entity of the five tathagata lineages and this meditation serves to increase the non-dual  wisdom of bliss and emptiness.  
 By condensing (those five) into the three secret lineages, the meditation is on seeing whatever appears as the three secret lineages and this also serves to increase the non -dual wisdom of 
bliss and emptiness.  
 (When) one  sees whatever appears as having the entity of Vajradhara,  through this 
meditation, it also assists in the generation and increase of the non -dual wisdom of bliss and 
emptiness  
 In short, the physical isolation of the completion stage is divided into two:  
 
Lesson  34          Page 233 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• During individual withdrawal , one trains to see everything that appears in post -equipoise 
as the manifestation of emptiness.  
• In meditative stabilisation, one causes the non -dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness to arise 
as deities in the elaborate way and in  the condensed way.  
 
The etymology of (the term) ‘physical isolation’ exists because the physical basis of isolation is the twenty gross (objects) etc. The cause of isolation is generating the appearance and conception of oneself as ordinary (tha rnal pa’ i snang zhen). The meaning of isolation is 
meditating on those as being the aspect (rnam ‘gyur) of bliss and emptiness or as being deities. Thus, to be physical isolation it does not need (to conform to) that full etymology and if it (does conform) to the complete etymology of physical isolation it does not need to be physical 
isolation because from the verbal isolation onwards one meditates on everything that appears 
as being sealed by bliss and emptiness. (Pg. 34 of the root text)  
 
(Interpreter ’s clarification: “ The cause of isolation is generating the appearance and conception 
of oneself as ordinary (tha rnal pa’i snang zhen)” can be read as “ The cause of isolation is 
generating the ordinary appearance and ordinary conception of oneself as ordinary (tha rnal 
pa’i snang zhen).”  “The cause of isolation ” refers to what is gotten rid of (during isolation).)  
 The (purpose of isolation) is to isolate from ordinary appearance and ordinary grasping. Whatever appears in post -meditative equipoise is empty and that  emptiness is the 
manifestation of great bliss. One causes the non- dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness to arise as 
deities. When one sees everything as deities, that assists in the igniting of great bliss. Through the blazing of that great bliss, that cause s the sense consciousnesses to withdraw, which 
further increases the bliss.  
 
Essentially, in both the meditation and the post -meditation sessions, we are trying to increase 
the non- dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness which is the actualising cause of the dharmakaya.  
 
As long you understand that the physical isolation of the completion stage is actually a practice to increase the great bliss in both the meditation and the post- meditation session that is good.  
 In general, the state of our minds is influenced  by how the mind looks at things. When 
everything appears to the mind as very pleasant, the mind will be always in a blissful state. That is why the meditation of the completion stage of physical isolation is the meditation on seeing (whatever) appears as empty and (seeing) that emptiness as the manifestation of great bliss.  
There is also the meditation of seeing everything as deities. (When) whatever appears to the mind is either the non- dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness or  deities, the mind will always  be 
happy.  
 This is one of the most profound essential points of Highest Yoga Tantra. Whatever appears is empty and that empty is in the nature of great bliss. When one develops the very subtle mind of non- dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness, the coarse le vel of consciousnesses gradually 
withdraws and dissolves. What remains is a very powerful mind that gradually develops on the completion stage, acting to counteract the afflictions.  
 
Lesson  34          Page 234 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
This meditation does not rest on whether the person who appears to you is  a deity in reality. 
When we are able to have that kind of pure appearance, basically we have fulfilled the purpose of doing the meditation which is to achieve happiness.  
 Please read the entire section on verbal isolation. I plan to finish the verbal iso lation in the next 
three lessons. It is very important for you to read before coming to class. If you don’t read 
before coming to class and I have to go through each and every single line, it will take up too 
much time and we will never finish. If you don’ t read before coming to class, when I summarise  
and highlight the different points, you will not understand. It is not important (at this stage) whether you understand or not after reading. Just try to think of what you have read.

LESSON NO: 35   DATE: 6TH AUGUST 2009  
 
It is very important to set a proper and virtuous motivation for participating  in this session. In 
particular, when we listen to the teachings, we should do so by abandoning the three faults of 
the vessel and generating the six recognitions or attitudes.  
 
Cultivating the six recognitions is something that you already know from studying the Lam Rim . 
This is just a refresher for you. The first attitude is recognising ourselves as patients who have a 
chronic illness. This first recognition is extremely important. All of us are tormented by the 
three poisons. This is very obvious. At the slightest instigation, we get angry. We can also generate attachment and desire very easily. When we do not strive to weaken these mental 
poisons now, then, at the time of dea th, due to being overwhelmed by them, we will have to 
go to the lower realms.  
 
We are all Buddhists and, in particular, we are followers of the Great Vehicle, the Mahayana.  
We also believe that we are in the class of Vajrayana practitioners. That being t he case, it is 
very important to remember the importance of weakening our mental poisons. Whenever we 
attend teachings, it is very important to listen to them with the motivation of wanting to reduce our mental afflictions. Then those teachings will defini tely benefit us. This is something 
that all of you already know and should put into practice.  
 So whenever we attend teachings, we should adjust our motivation before they begin. It is also important that when we listen to the teachings, we should do so w ith enthusiasm.  Even when 
one is unable to understand (this) particular subject in detail, one should feel happy for having the opportunity to learn something that is so deep and profound. One should feel happy even 
if one only gains a rough understanding  of this profound subject. It is important, at all times, to 
have the understanding of how fortunate we are to have some faith in such a profound subject and to have all the available conditions and opportunity to hear, read and study it. We should always remember this.  
 The majority of you are studying such a subject for the very first time. Since this is so, of course 
it is difficult. Whatever we do for the first time, usually, (in the beginning we will face 
difficulties). As I had said (many times befor e), it is important not to get discouraged and to 
lose heart. You will not benefit once that happens. What you have to do is to pray to be able to 
learn the subject and then put in the effort to do so.  
Manjushri told Lama Tsong Khapa to always do these thr ee things together in combination:  
1. Make fervent requests to one’s deity, seeing it as inseparable from one’s guru  
2. Strive in the accumulation of merit and purification of obscurations  
3. Work hard at the texts and to study the teachings  
When all these three are done, then definitely one will develop. Such advice is really beneficial 
for the mind. I think there are some people who may feel disheartened or discouraged, saying they don’t understand anything. Such discouragement benefits no one.

Lesson  35          Page 236

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
With respect to the etymology of the words, “physical isolation,” the basis of isolation is the 
coarse body appearing to us as ordinary and our ordinary conception of it. In order to isolate that ordinary appearance and c onception, one trains by seeing everything that appears to be 
the display of emptiness and bliss. Then, one trains to cause that non -dual bliss and emptiness 
to arise as the deity.  
 The completion stage of physical isolation has two divisions: (1) Individ ual withdrawal and (2) 
meditative stabilisation. There are five types of meditative stabilisation:  
1) Investigation  
2) Analysis  
3) Joy 
4) Bliss  
5) Concentration  
 We had looked at the difference between investigation and analysis in the last class. Briefly, analysis  is essentially the physical isolation which is the meditation on the 100 immaculate 
lineages and investigation is the meditation on the five lineages, the three secret lineages or 
the one extremely secret lineage.  
 How do we arrive at the classification of th e 100 immaculate lineages? Following the Arya 
tradition of Guhyasamaja, there are 32 deities. When we exclude 12 deities out of these 32, we 
are left with 20 deities. When we relate each of these 20 deities with the five lineages, we will 
arrive at the tot al of 100 immaculate lineages.  
 
When the 100 immaculate lineages are condensed, they become the five Tathagata lineages. When those are condensed, we have the three secret lineages -  the lineages of the exalted 
body, speech and mind. When the three secret lineages are further condensed, we arrive at 
the one extremely secret lineage, which is basically Vajradhara.   
  
Question: Investigation and analysis are meditative stabilisations to generate deities in the mental continuum. Why are these meditative stab ilisations performed in post -meditative 
equipoise?  
Answer :  Meditative equipoise is “ yamba shapa”  in Tibetan, which refers to meditative 
equipoise on emptiness or bliss and emptiness. In this context, it refers to the meditative equipoise on emptiness after the subsidence of coarse duality.  
 
What about meditative stabilisation? Meditative stabilisation is one of the (divisions of) physical isolation and is “ samten” in Tibetan. (This is sometimes translated as “concentration,” 
but for our purposes here, we  will translate “ samten ” as meditative stabilisation).  
 In general, meditative stabilisation is a mind that takes a chosen object and places the mind single -pointedly on it. There is a meditative stabilisation on the conventional deity and a 
meditative st abilisation on the ultimate deity.   
 The meditative stabilisation on the conventional deity is related to the post- meditative 
equipoise. When the mind focussing on the conventional (i.e. conventional deity) is stronger, 
 
Lesson  35          Page 237 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
then there is investigation and analysis, both of which deal with the conventional.  The other 
three meditative stabilisations -  joy, bliss and single -pointed concentration -   are (practised in) 
meditative equipoise. The conventional aspects of these three  minds are not as strong.  
 It is easy to explain this when we distinguish between meditative equipoise and meditative stabilisation. If we posit them to be the same, then that will be a problem.  
 
Question: During meditative equipoise, the winds have ente red the central channel. Do they 
remain there during post -meditative equipoise?  
Answer : A physical isolation of the completion stage must entail the winds entering the central 
channel. That being the case, I guess one can posit that if it is physical isolation in post-
meditative equipoise, the winds have entered into and are in the central channel.  
 Question: Do the preliminaries of meditating on the subtle drop at the lower opening of the central channel, igniting the tumo and so forth –  do they fall unde r the definition of individual 
withdrawal and absorption? In essence, where is the boundary of the beginning of physical isolation?   
Answer : The definition says, “…… a yogi who has consummated the subtle and gross generation 
stages of this system, from (tha t yogi) generating the primordial awareness of the empties …..” 
So when one generates the first empty of white appearance, i.e. the wisdom of the empty, this marks the start of physical isolation. The whole meditation preceding that is not physical 
isolation because that is the way to arrive at physical isolation. That is why the definition says, 
“…derived from causing the winds …”. Anything that is derived cannot be the effect, isn’t it?  
 
Question:  Are the four joys the four wisdoms?  
Answer : Through medita ting on the subtle at the lower opening of the central channel, the 
winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel.  The blazing of the tumo at the navel melts the white bodhicitta at the crown which starts to drip and descends down the central channel:    
• As the bodhicitta descends to the throat, one joy is generated.  
• When the bodhicitta moves from the throat to the heart, another joy is generated.  
• When the bodhicitta moves from heart to the navel, another joy is generated.  
• When the bodhicitta m oves from the navel to the tip of the secret place,   then the last joy 
is generated.  
These are the four descending joys .  
 When the bodhicitta moves upwards from the secret place to the navel, from the navel to the heart and from the heart to (the throat  and from the throat to the) crown,  the four ascending 
joys are also generated.  
 It is said that the four ascending joys are stronger than the four descending joys.   After the bodhicitta descends from the crown to the secret place, it must  not be lost and one has to prevent the emission of the bodhicitta. Instead of letting the bodhicitta emit from the body, one causes that bodhicitta to ascend the central channel. Such a practice is necessary 
during the completion stage of the physical isolation.  
 
Lesson  35          Page 238 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Ques tion: If the bodhicitta can go up and down the central channel, shouldn’t the winds be 
able to do likewise? Why are they stuck at the level of the heart?  
Answer : I also have this qualm but this is not clear in the text.  
 Thus, to be physical isolation it  does not need (to conform to) that full etymology and if it (does 
conform) to the complete etymology of physical isolation it does not need to be physical 
isolation because from the verbal isolation onwards one meditates on everything that appears 
as bein g sealed by bliss and emptiness. ( Pg. 34 of the root text ) 
 How do you posit a physical isolation that does not conform to the etymology but is still a physical isolation?  This is something you have to think about. See if you can come out with an illustra tion.  
 2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.1.2  THE CONCENTRATION OF  THE VAJRA SPEECH: (O RDINARY) VERBAL 
ISOLATION  
 The nature of the second stage in the six -fold (division) of the completion stage of the Arya 
system is ‘the completion stage (practice) of a yogi from the generation of the primordial awareness of appearance derived from the winds entering, abiding and dissolving into the central channel at the heart by meditating on the mantra drop (sgnagthig) at the top (sna rtse) 
of the heart, up to (but not including) the g eneration of the primordial awareness of 
appearance derived from causing the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the indestructible (drop) at the heart through completely loosening the channel knot (rtsa mdud) at the heart by 
the strength of meditation. ( Pg. 34 of the root text ) 
 During physical isolation, the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel but they are unable to do so at the level of the heart. Verbal isolation  is posited at the time when the 
winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel at the heart.  
 
During the time of verbal isolation, in order to cause the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in 
the central channel at the heart, one meditates on the   mantra drop at the heart inside the 
central channel and through this meditation, due to the winds entering, abiding and dissolving in the central channel at the heart, the first wisdom, the wisdom of appearance, is generated and that marks the start of ve rbal isolation.  
 Although the winds have entered, abide and dissolved in the central channel at the heart, they have yet to dissolve in the indestructible drop. That being the case, I think I will say that during verbal isolation, the channel knots at the  heart are loosened but are not completely loosened.  
  
When the channel knots are completely  loosened at the heart, only then can the winds dissolve 
in the indestructible drop. When this happens and the wisdom of appearance is generated, that is when menta l isolation starts .  
 Therefore, the boundary of verbal isolation is “ up to but not including the generation of the 
primordial awareness of appearance derived from causing the winds to enter, abide and 
 
Lesson  35          Page 239 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
dissolve in the indestructible (drop) at the heart thr ough completely loosening the channel knot 
(rtsa mdud) at the heart by the strength of meditation.”  
 
I have talked a little about this before. If you compare what I had said to this definition, see whether they have the same meaning. It is very important f or you to understand the meaning 
of the definition. Do you have any qualms or questions pertaining to this definition?  
 
Question: When are the channel knots at the heart initially loosened? I thought the loosening 
of the channel knots only occurs when the winds enter the indestructible drop and not before 
that.  
Answer : The channel knots are initially loosened during verbal isolation through the meditation 
of the mantra drop at the heart but are not completely loosened.  
 If you had read the pages pertaining to verbal isolation, you would know that there are three types of life -force exertions. They are:  
1) Meditating on the mantra drop at the heart  
2) Meditating on the light drop at the tip of the nose  
3) Meditating on the substance drop at the tip of the s ecret place  
 These are the stages of meditation:  
• First, one meditates on the mantra drop at the heart in the central channel  
• Then, one meditates on the light drop at the tip of the nose.  
• Then, one meditates on the substance drop at the tip of the secret place.  
 
Although these three yogas are designated as verbal isolations but the meditations on the mantra drop at the heart and the substance drop at the tip of the secret place are not fully 
qualified verbal isolations. That means that they are not  the actu al verbal isolation. The actual 
verbal isolation  has to be done with the meditation on the light drop at the tip of the nose.  
 This stage is called ‘verbal isolation’ (gnag dben) or Vajra recitation’ (rdo rje bzlas pa), but this is a one -sided designation (phyogs cig gi ming gis btags) life -force exertion and the two yogas 
of life -force exertion meditating on the mantra drop
42 and the substance drop (rdzas thig) are 
neither verbal isolation nor vajra recitation. ( Pg. 34 -35 of the root text)  
 If you remember the six preparatory branches, the third is life -force exertion. It is ‘ tsog chou’ in 
Tibetan and is sometimes translated as wind yoga, pramana yoga or in this context, 
lengthening vitality and exertion.  
 
What you have to know is that the meditati on on the light drop at the tip of the nose is the 
actual verbal isolation. Although the meditations on the mantra drop at the heart and the substance drop at the tip of the secret place are fully qualified life -force exertions, they are not 
42 Life- force exertion meditating on the mantra drop: is the yoga in which practitioners meditate on a mantra 
circle at the heart, visualising the syllable HUM at the heart channel. The syllable should be very bright and by 
focusing on the nada of the syllable, the gathering and dissolution of the winds into the central channel at the 
heart is facilitated.  
 
 
Lesson  35          Page 240 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment                                                                   
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
the actual verbal isolation. These three can be posited as the levels of verbal isolation but it 
doesn’t mean that whatever is posited as a level of verbal isolation is necessarily verbal isolation. Keep this in your mind. Go back home and read the text and see whether this is what 
the text is actually saying.   
 The three -fold process of the winds arising, entering and abiding  is called natural repetition (or 
recitation). These three -  the winds arising, entering and abiding -  is (also) called speech which 
is the basis of isolation . Speech here refers to this natural repetition which is taken to be the 
winds arising, entering and abiding . 
 The object of negation (that which is to be negated) is the ordinary conception which views 
these three -  the tones of the winds aris ing, entering and abiding  - to be different from the 
tones of the three syllables OM, AH, HUM. This ordinary concept which sees these two as separate is the object of negation.  
 “Isolation”  means bringing these two into union by seeing the tones of the winds arising, 
entering and abiding  as being indifferentiable from the tones of the three syllables OM, AH, 
HUM.  
 
• In the practice of vajra recitation, one sees the tones of the winds arising i.e., the winds 
exiting or exhalation. This exhalation is indifferentiable  from the tone of the syllable 
HUM.  
• With inhalation, one sees the tone of the winds entering as indifferentiated from the tone of the syllable OM.  
• When the tones of the winds are neither entering nor arising, i.e., the tone of the winds 
are abiding, one sees that as indifferentiated from the tone of the syllable AH.  
 I think for us, abiding doesn’t exist unless you are accustomed to it.  
 What does speech refer to? It is essentially the resonance or natural vibration of the winds. (In the text, this is described as the “ tone .”)  
 Here, isolation means one does not conceive that speech which is the tone of the winds as ordinary but rather one causes those tones to arise as the tones of the syllables OM, AH, HUM. 
Speech here primarily refers to the tone of the winds. The root of speech is wind.  
 
There are stages of meditation in the three life -force exertions because (1) in the context of 
physical isolation (there is meditation) to gat her the winds at the central channel at the heart 
when unable to do so, though one is able to gather the winds in the central channel, (2) 
meditation on the mantra drop at the top of the heart and (3) through long term familiarity with that one first gener ates primordial awareness of the four empties and transfers to the 
stage of life -force exertion designated as vajra recitation verbal isolation . (Pg. 36 of the root 
text)   
 This is the rough description of the way of meditating on these life -force exertion s.   
 
 
Lesson  35          Page 241 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Things that you have to know and need to understand from this class are:  
• the definition of verbal isolation  
• its boundaries  
• when does one move from physical isolation to verbal isolation.  
• what is the basis of verbal isolation  
• what is the object of ne gation  
• what is the meaning of isolation.  
As long as you have a rough understanding of what is the basis of isolation, what that speech 
refers to and what is the object of negation to be isolated and what is the meaning of verbal 
isolation, then you know how one’s speech can be transformed into the speech of the Buddha in the future.   The main thing is to understand how the completion stage of physical isolation is the cause for the achievement of the Buddha’s body and  how  verbal isolation contributes as the cause for the achievement of the Buddha’s speech. Later, we will see how mental isolation contributes towards the achievement of the Buddha’s mind. If one understands in detail how the Buddha’s body, speech and mind are achieved, then one will really understand that the attainment of enlightenment is something that is very amazing and very powerful.
