 LESSON NO: 36    DATE: 11
TH AUGUST 2009  
 As usual, we will spend five minutes in silence to do some reflection. We can refle ct on the 
meaning of the refuge and generating bodhicitta prayer which we always recite before class, checking to see whether what is described in that prayer manifests in our minds. If not, we 
should try to generate that motivation.  
 
We should check to s ee whether there is real refuge in our minds. After generating refuge, if 
we find that the bodhicitta thought is not in our minds, at least within the next five minutes, we should try to generate it.  
 When we manage to generate real refuge in our hearts, we are protected from the sufferings of the lower realms in future lives. When we are able to develop some bodhicitta within this short period of time, that helps to   purify negativities accumulated si nce beginningless time. 
There is nothing more powerful than bodhicitta for purification of negativities. We can relate this exercise to people around us. If there is someone whom we have difficulties getting along with, check whether we can develop forbear ance,  patience, positive thoughts and the good 
heart towards him within this short period of time. This is more practical and beneficial.  
 
This is a bit of a refresher (for all of you).  
• When we recite, “I go for refuge until I am enlightened to the Buddha, Dharma and the 
Supreme Assembly,” this is the practice of causal refuge. At the time when we recite “I”, we should have some understanding of emptiness and investigate who is this “I” who is going for refuge and try to bring to mind that this “I” is me rely designated by thought.  
• “By the practice of giving and other perfections,/ May I become a buddha to benefit all 
living beings”: This is the practice of resultant refuge.  
We had covered this when we studied The Tathagata Essence . This is just a reminde r. Actually 
there are many things that we can reflect on when we recite this verse of refuge and bodhicitta.  
 
*****************************  
 
Verbal isolation is essentially bringing the tones of the winds arising, entering and abiding into an indivisible union with the tones of the three syllables OM AH HUM.  
 The object of negation (i.e., what we are trying to stop) is the conception in the continuum of an ordinary individual believing that the tones of the winds arising, entering and abiding to be differ ent from the tones of these three syllables.  
 
There are three levels (or stages) of verbal isolation bringing the tones of the winds arising, 
entering and abiding into an indivisible union with the tones of the three syllables.  These are essentially the three yogas of life -force exertions:  
4) Meditating on the mantra drop at the top of the heart  
5) Meditating on the light drop at the tip of the nose  
6) Meditating on the substance drop at the tip of the secret place  
 
Lesson  36          Page 243 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Of these three yogas, only the meditation on the  light drop at the tip of the nose is the actual 
verbal isolation . The other two are designated as verbal isolation but they are not  the fully 
qualified verbal isolation.  
• “At the top of the heart ” refers to the middle of the heart chakra.  
• “At the tip of the nose ” refers to the upper opening of the central channel, i.e., the point 
between the two eyebrows.  
• “At the tip of the secret place ” refers to the lower opening of the central channel.  
 
There are stages of meditation in the three life -force exertions because (1) in the context of 
physical isolation (there is meditation) to gather the winds at the central channel at the heart when unable to do so, though one is able to gather the winds in the central channel, (2) meditation on the mantra drop at the top of the heart and (3) through long term familiarity 
with that one first generates primordial awareness of the four empties and transfers to the stage of life -force exertion designated as vajra recitation verbal isolation. ( Pg. 36 of the root 
text)  
 There a re three stages of meditation in the three life -force exertions:  
• During physical isolation, although the winds gather in the central channel, they do not 
gather at the heart level of the central channel.  
• In order to gather the winds at the heart level of the central channel, one meditates on the 
mantra drop at the heart. Through meditating on that, the winds enter, abide and dissolve 
in the central channel at the level of the heart.  
• Due to this three -fold process of the winds entering, abiding and dissolv ing in the heart 
level of the central channel, when the wisdom of the first empty (the wisdom of appearance) arises, that is the time one moves from physical isolation to verbal isolation.  
 Since this is a state compatible with (rjes mthun) mental isolation it is suitable to be designated as mental isolation but it is not the actual state (mtshan nyid pa). The Illuminating Lamp states:  
 Having well understood the point of meditating on the indestructible (drop) at the heart, and through long familiarity with that, the primordial awareness of the four empties is first generated. Since this is compatible with mental isolation it is suitable to be designated as that, but it is not actual (mental isolation).  
 
By meditating on the mantra drop as the method of ga thering (sdud) the winds at the heart in 
that way, the winds gather and dissolve in the central channel. This is dissolution (of the winds) in the central channel near to the indestructible drop at the heart, but not dissolution of the winds in the actual indestructible drop. To dissolve the winds in the actual indestructible drop its channel knot must be loosened. For that purpose, life -force exertion meditating on the light 
drop at the tip of the nose (sna rtse) meditates on vajra repetition of the indivisible union (sbyor) of the individual tones of the three: the wind (1) rising, (2) entering and (3) abiding and 
the tones of the three letters. ( Pg. 36 of the root text).  
 
 
Lesson  36          Page 244 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
When one meditates on the mantra drop at the heart, although the  winds enter, abide and 
dissolve in the heart level of the central channel of the heart, they do not  dissolve in the 
indestructible drop at the heart. In order for the winds to dissolve in the indestructible drop at 
the heart, the channel knots at the heart must first be loosened. In order to do this, one 
meditates on the light drop at the tip of the nose.  
 I myself have a qualm about this. When one is meditating on the mantra drop at the heart, do 
the channel knots at the heart loosen a little or not at al l? 
 
What the text is saying is in order for the winds to dissolve in the indestructible drop, the channel knots at the heart must be loosened. In order to do this, one meditates on the light drop at the tip of the nose. But prior to this meditation on t
he light drop at the tip of the nose, 
one is meditating  on  the mantra drop at the centre of the heart chakra at the central channel. 
One will have to say when one is meditating on the mantra drop at the heart that the channel knots at the heart have yet to be loosened. But through that meditation (on the mantra drop),  the winds are said to enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel at the level of the heart. If 
the channel knots at the heart are not loosened, how is that accomplished? That is my qualm.   
 
Question: Is there only one set of channel knots? It sounds like there is a channel knot at the heart level of the central channel and another channel knot around the indestructible drop?   
Answer : It is not mentioned in the text that there is a knot that obstructs the indestructible 
drop but it does mention the channel knots at the heart.  
 By meditating on the mantra drop as the method of gathering ( sdud) the winds at the heart in 
that way, the w inds gather and dissolve in the central channel.  (Pg. 36 of the root text ) 
 One meditates on the mantra drop at the heart in order to gather the winds that are yet to be gathered at the heart. In  order to loosen the channel knots at the heart, one meditates on the 
light drop at the tip of the nose. Here, “light” refers to the winds so the meditation on the light drop actually refers to meditation on the winds.  
 That is because just as one opens ( ‘byer) a blocked hollow bamboo stem by inserting a long 
stick into it, one opens the channel knots by inserting winds into them . (Pg. 36 of the root text ) 
 When the wind moves in the channel, one recognizes the resonance (sgrog bzhin) to be like the 
tone of  the three letters and directing the mind to that without distraction is the method of 
vajra recitation in this context. (Pg. 36 of the root text)  
 
Question: Do all the winds gather in the central channel during (1) post- equipoise physical 
isolation and (2 ) during speech isolation?  
Answer : During post -equipoise physical isolation, the winds are not gathered in the central  
channel as one has arisen from meditative equipoise. The purpose of gathering the winds in the central channel (and having them enter), abide and dissolve there is to generate the non -
dual wisdom of bliss and emptiness. When that wisdom is generated, one places one’s mind on 
that wisdom. When one arises from that meditative equipoise, the sense consciousnesses 
 
Lesson  36          Page 245 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
manifest again and the winds are not gathered in the central channel. That is clear for post -
equipoise physical isolation.  
 
Question:  In order for i t to be a physical isolation, it has to be a yoga derived from the winds 
entering, abiding and dissolving in the central channel. If that is the case, what about individual withdrawal which is post -equipoise physical isolation?  Since one is not in meditat ive equipoise, 
the winds are not gathered in the central channel. It follows then that individual withdrawal is 
not physical isolation.  
Answer: Physical isolation starts “ from (that yogi) generating the primordial awareness of the 
empties (stone pa’i ye sh e) derived from causing the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the 
central channel by meditating on the subtle (drop) in the lower opening (of the central channel), 
up to but not including the generation of the primordial awareness that arises through dissolving of the upper and lower winds in the central channel at the heart .” (Pg. 31 of the root 
text). Individual withdrawal falls in between the generation of the wisdom of the empties in 
the meditative equipoise up to but not including verbal isolation.   
 Question: So the phrase, “the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel” does not mean they stay put there?  
Answer : The boundary of the definition covers both meditative and post- meditative equipoise. 
What is derived from the winds enter ing, abiding and dissolving in the central channel? The 
wisdoms of the empties are derived from causing the winds to enter, abide and dissolve in the 
central channel but those wisdoms are generated up to but not including verbal isolation.  
 
Question: Is th at the reason why in the definition of the completion stage, it is said to be a yoga 
in the continuum of a learner derived through making the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central (channel) but “ in general it is explained that if it is a completio n stage (practice) it is 
not pervasive that it is derived from making the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel. That is because both individual (withdrawal) and absorption (sor bsam) of the Kalachakra system are preliminary (practices) fo r making the winds enter the central 
channel ”(Pg. 26 of the root text)  ? 
Answer : Generally speaking, for it to be a completion stage, it must be “ the  yoga in the 
continuum of a  learner derived through making the winds enter, abide and dissolve in the central channel though the strength of meditation” .  (Pg. 26 of the root text). This is from the 
perspective of Highest Yoga Tantra as a whole but this does not mean that this is necessarily so for all Highest Yoga Tantras. For example, from the perspective o f the Kalachakra Tantra, 
individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation are considered  preliminaries to that of the completion stage of Kalachakra. It is not the actual completion stage.  
If one is using this as a support for the earlier discussion, t hen it is not like that as the earlier 
discussion pertains to a different point.   
****************  
I will now give a brief description of the visualisation of vajra recitation:  
• Visualise a light drop at the heart travelling up to the central channel to the upper opening of the central channel, seeing that the tone (also referred to as resonance or vibration) of the winds to be inseparable from the tone of the syllable HUM. That i s the process of the 
winds arising.  
 
Lesson  36          Page 246 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• Now, visualise the light drop from the upper opening of the central channel going down the 
central channel towards the level of the heart. That is the process of the winds entering and 
one sees the tone of the winds entering to be inseparable from the tone of the syllable OM.  
• Next, the winds abide at the heart. One meditates on the tone of those winds  to be 
inseparable from the tone of the syllable AH.  
The meditation is to see the two things –  the tones of the winds ar ising, entering and abiding 
and the tones of the syllables OM AH HUM - as inseparable and as one. This meditation is not 
done as if one is reciting with one’s speech or one is doing mental recitation but as if one is 
hearing it with one’s ears.    
 Initially, one has to put effort into seeing these two (the tone of the winds and the tones of the three syllables) as inseparable. Due to repeated meditation and familiarity over time, it is said 
that experience becomes spontaneous and one will naturally view the tones of the winds 
resonating with the tones of the three syllables.  
 As an ordinary individual, our winds arise and enter but the phase of abiding is not there. But through effort in meditation, one can generate the phase of abiding. Through thi s meditation, 
the arising, entering and abiding of the winds are brought slowly into equilibrium. It is also said that through the force of meditation, after some time, the phases of the winds arising and entering are shortened and the phase of abiding lengthens. It is possible to lengthen the phase of abiding to the level where the winds, for a while, do not arise and enter but simply abide.  
 After gaining familiarity with this visualisation, there are additional practices such as sending the winds from the tip of the nose (i.e., the upper opening of the central channel)  out of the 
body to a distance of four -finger -widths and gradually extending that to the length of an elbow 
and so forth.   
 The purpose of doing this vajra recitation or meditation of the  light drop at the tip of the nose 
is to loosen the channel knots at the heart. The example mentioned in the text is this: “ That is 
because just as one opens  a blocked hollow bamboo stem by inserting a long stick into it, one 
opens the channel knots by ins erting winds into them.”  
 Regarding vajra recitation, if the object of recitation is classified by means of the winds there are two (types): main and secondary vajra recitation and also these are classified respectively in four and five (types). If we clas sify them from the point of view of the recited mantra there 
are two: vajra recitation of the two letters: HUM HO and vajra recitation of the three letters (OM, AH, HUM.) (Pg. 36 of the root text)  
 Regarding vajra recitation, there is the vajra recitation of the root winds and the vajra 
recitation of the secondary winds. There are five root and five secondary winds. In the context 
of vajra recitation, only four of the five root winds are practised, excluding the pervasive wind 
as one is unable to do vajra recitation with that wind at this point in time.  
 In terms of the mantra that is recited, there is the vajra recitation of the two letters HUM HO and the vajra recitation of the three letters OM, AH, HUM.   
 
 
Lesson  36          Page 247 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
There are three occasions when completion stage p ractitioners perform vajra repetition 
because it is stated that (1) when they perform vajra repetition to establish the activities of 
pacification, increase, subjugation and ferocity, they do it through the four main winds. (2) When they perform vajra repe tition to establish the five higher perceptions of the eyes, ears, 
nose, tongue and body, they do it serially through the five secondary winds such as the moving one etc. (3) When they perform vajra repetition to invoke (‘dren pa) the empties of mental isolation having previously established physical isolation, they do it first through the four main 
winds, then having attained mastery (nus pa) in that, they do it through the five secondary winds. ( Pg. 37 of the root text)  
 Of the three occasions when comple tion stage practitioners perform vajra repetition, it is the 
third one that we are talking about in this context of verbal isolation.  
 What is verbal isolation?  It is basically seeing the tone of the natural winds that are arising, 
entering and abiding to be inseparable from the tones of the three syllables OM AH HUM. This is something you have to remember and understand.  
 When does one transfer from physical is olation to verbal isolation?  Due to the winds entering, 
abiding and dissolving in the central channel of the heart, when the wisdoms of the four empties arise, that is the time when one is transfers to verbal isolation  
• In order to gather the winds that are not gathered in the central channel of the heart, one 
first meditates on the mantra drop at the heart.  
• In order to loosen the channel knots at the heart, one performs the vajra recitation.  
• Of the three life -force exer tions, only the meditation on the light drop at the tip of the nose 
is the actual verbal isolation . That particular life -force exertion is to be given the name 
verbal isolation. As I mentioned earlier, although this meditation is on the light drop, the 
light drop is actually a wind drop and it is a wind yoga.   
 We need to have a rough understanding of what verbal isolation is. I find it difficult to explain the actual meditation on vajra recitation as that is very complicated.  
 When we talk about the inde structible drop, there are actually two drops:  
1. There is the indestructible drop at the heart that exists during one’s lifetime which is 
made up of the two bodhicittas: the white and red drops. It remains at the heart as long as one is alive, only separatin g at the time of death.  
2. There is the indestructible drop that has existed forever which is basically the most 
subtle wind and mind which can never be separated.  
    Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme

LESSON NO: 37   DATE: 13TH AUGUST 2009  
 As before, we will spend the next five minutes reflecting on the refuge prayer we recite before class. We had reflected on the meaning of causal and resultant refuge and the nature of the I who is going for refuge that is merely designated by the force of conceptuality.  
 Then there is the generation of bodhicitta or the altruistic intention thinking; “ I will lead all 
sentient beings to enlightenment ” or as said in some other prayers, “ I will place all sentient 
beings in the sorrowless state, ” which  refers  to the non -abiding nirvana, full enlightenment. 
We should see if we are able to transform our minds into the meaning of that prayer, to check whether our minds match what we recite verbally. If not, then spend the next couple of minutes to do so.   
*******************  
Verbal isolation is essentially the meditation on the inseparability of the tones of the winds arising, entering and abiding from the tones of the three letters OM AH HUM.   There are three life -force exertions during the stage of the verbal isolation:  
• First, one meditates on the mantra drop at the heart and that is done to cause the winds to 
enter into the central channel at the heart. When the winds do this, the wisdoms of white 
appearance, red increase and black near attainment are generate d. When the wisdom of 
white appearance arises, that is the time marking one’s transference from the physical isolation to verbal isolation.  
• Next is the meditation on the light drop at the tip of the nose. This meditation is done to 
loosen the channel knot s at the heart.  
• This is followed by the meditation on the substance drop at the tip of the secret place.  
Meditating on the substance drop at the tip of the secret (organ) has two (types):  
(1) Retention of the (drop of) bodhichitta (produced) from the unio n of oneself and an action 
seal (las rgya)
43 at the meeting point of the two (lower) openings of the central channels, 
without releasing it.  
(2) Meditation focusing on the drop at the meeting point of the two lower openings of the central channels of onesel f and a wisdom seal (yi rgya).
44(Pg. 37 of the root text)  
Basically this is a meditation focussing on the meeting point of the lower openings of the two central channels of oneself in union with either the action seal or the wisdom seal. Why is 
there a nee d for this meditation?  
There is a need to meditate in this way because to gather the winds at the heart when the two 
previous (types of) life -force exertion are unable to gather them there, this meditation on the 
43 Action seal (las rgya): is an actual consort.  
44 Wisdom seal (yi rgya): is an imagined consort.  
 
Lesson  37          Page 249 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment                                                                   
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
substance drop is advantageous (bogs ‘don du ‘gro ba) in gathering the winds at the heart.  
The life -force exertion uniting (sbyor) both oneself and the seal (phyag rgya) exists both (1) 
before and (2) after the consummation of vajra recitation and one must understand the 
method of how this acts as  the means for gathering the winds in the central channel through 
the Illuminating Lamp. (Pg. 37 of the root text)  
There are three life -force exertions during verbal isolation. It is sufficient for you to know the 
reasons for meditating on the mantra drop at the heart, for the light drop at the tip of the nose 
and the substance drop at the tip of the secret place.  
 Question : Is there a generation of the fourth wisdom of the empties (all - empty) in physical and 
verbal isolations?  
Answer:  The four empties do arise during the three stages in the isolations of body, speech and 
mind. But when one looks at the terminology th at is used in the context of the three isolations, 
only the wisdoms of the three empties appear. When one looks at the definitions of the three isolations, they talk about the arising of the wisdom of the four empties. But based  on what we have covered so  far, we have only been referring to the three and not four empties.   
 The fourth empty exists but there is no explicit reference to it. Why? Because all the four empties are similar in the sense of possessing duality and the fourth empty (all -empty) is 
subsumed by the third empty.  
 
On the fourth stage of the meaning clear light, however, clear light is specifically mentioned 
separately because the fourth stage of the meaning clear light does not possess any duality 
(whereas the wisdoms of the four emptie s do). In order to separate them on the fourth stage 
of the meaning clear light, we then talk about the wisdoms of the four (as opposed to the three) empties.  
 The clear lights that arise during the physical, speech and mental isolations possess duality. The 
fourth empty (all -empty) is a conceptual consciousness. When one reaches the fourth stage of 
the meaning clear light, that meaning clear light is a direct perceiver.  
 Question: With reference to the vajra recitation of the two letters, how do they cor respond 
with the movement of the winds?  
Answer : When one does vajra recitation of the two letters, HUM and HO, HUM will be related 
to the arising of the winds and HO will be relating to the entering of the winds. When we talk 
about the vajra recitation of the two letters, there is no reference to the stage of abiding. I 
haven’t seen this  clearly explained in the texts.  
 
Anyway, pertaining to even the vajra recitation of the OM AH HUM, depending on the text one 
refers to, for example, Khedrub Je and Gyaltsab Je have different ways of explaining this. There 
is an explanation of seeing the tone of the winds arising to be inse parable from the tone of 
HUM, the tone of the winds entering to be inseparable from the tone of OM and the tone of the winds abiding to be inseparable from the tone AH.   
 
 
Lesson  37          Page 250 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Question: With reference to the bottom of page 37 of the root text, there is a state ment that 
“dissolution of the winds at the heart exists without dissolution of the winds in the central 
channel at the heart. That is because to dissolve the winds internally, there is no pervasion they dissolve in the central channel.” It’s a bit confusing. I don’t understand this….  
Answer: During verbal isolation, the winds have to enter the central channel at the heart but 
they do not dissolve into the indestructible drop at the heart.    
 
There is much debate on this issue but I think it may be better to say that during verbal 
isolation, the winds dissolve into the central channel at the heart. The issue here is whether the winds actually dissolve in the central channel at the heart during verbal isolation.  
 (Following the extract mentioned in the question) there is a quotation from Lama Tsongkhapa’s Illuminating Lamp which seems to be saying this is not the case and this is the qualm that is being brought up. ‘ Tog jue ’ or eliminating the qualm in Tibetan is a very important part of the 
learning process.  But in order to understand the qualm, it is only useful if one reads and thinks 
about it beforehand. If not, there is no point in (eliminating the qualm now).  
 I think we have to say that the winds do dissolve into the central channel at the heart during  
verbal isolation because the definition says so. It is not helpful at all just to say this without knowing the reason or without any source. But when one says the source is the definition, then that makes sense.  
 There are many qualms in this particular section from pages 37 to 39 but I am not sure whether it will be beneficial to look at them because even if I do, most of you do not understand (the 
qualms).  
 Let’s say we are positing the view that the winds do dissolve in the central channel at the heart  
during verbal isolation. When we meditate on the first life -force exertion, the meditation on 
the mantra drop at the (top of the) heart causes the winds to dissolve at the heart but (the text) seems to be saying that although the winds dissolve at the hea rt, they do not dissolve at the 
heart in the central channel.  
 When do the winds dissolve in the heart at the central channel? It is only when we do the second life -force exertion, the meditation on the light drop at the tip of the nose. I think we 
just leave it as that. If not, it becomes too confusing.  
Student : So the winds can dissolve in the heart outside  the central channel…  
 Question:  Are there winds that are not gathered by the two previous life -force exertions and 
that is why we need the third li fe-force exertion?  
Answer: The text says that when you meditate on the light drop at the tip of the nose, you are 
able to gather the winds at the heart. Even the meditation on the mantra drop at the heart also 
gathers the winds at the heart.  
 There is a n eed to meditate in this way because to gather the winds at the heart when the two 
previous (types of) life -force exertion are unable to gather them there, this meditation on the 
 
Lesson  37          Page 251 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
substance drop is advantageous (bogs ‘don du ‘gro ba) in that gathering the wi nds at the heart.  
(Pg. 37 of the root text).  
 
The purpose of the meditation on the substance drop at the tip of the secret place is to gather the winds that were not gathered by the two previous life -force exertions. Are there such 
winds? If one says yes, then we have to posit what those winds are. Ma ybe we can say this 
refers to the pervasive wind. Even if we were to say that there aren’t any winds not previously 
gathered, that is not a problem. In such a case, the meditation on the substance drop at the tip 
of the secret place is the support  for the two previous life -force exertions to gather the winds 
at the heart. Here, “ don du”  can be taken to mean “enhance,” i.e., it enhances what was 
already achieved by the two previous life -force exertions.  
 The meditation on the light drop at the tip of the no se causes the channel knots at the heart to 
loosen but not completely. It is the meditation on the substance drop at the tip of the secret place that causes the channel knots at the heart to be completely loosened. When this 
happens, all the root and secon dary winds, except the pervasive wind, dissolve into the heart 
in the central channel. I am not sure whether it is all right to say this as there are many different views and explanations. During the verbal isolation all the winds, except the pervasive wind dissolve in the heart in the central channel.  
 There are many qualms starting from the bottom of page 37 all the way to the top of page 39. 
In short, do the winds dissolve at the heart during verbal isolation? If the winds do dissolve at 
the heart during verbal isolation, do they necessarily dissolve in the central channel at the heart? These are the qualms.  
 The Grounds and Paths (of Guhyasamaja According to Arya Nagarjuna) of Yangchen Galo 
states:  
 
In dependence on performing vajra recitation in which the individual tones of the three: (the winds) arising, entering and abiding dawns as the tone of the three letters, the channel knots above and below the heart are loosened and the upper and lower winds dissolve into the central channel at the heart, one generates the primordial awareness of appearance (snang ba’i ye shes) and one transfers from physical to verbal isolation. (Pg. 38 of the root text ) 
 
According to Yangchen Galo, during vajra recitation, the channel knots above and below the 
heart are loosened and the upper and lower winds dissolve into the central channel at the 
heart. Through that, one generates the wisdom of appearance and transfers from the physi cal 
to verbal isolation. His presentation differs from what we have been discussing.   To be able to eliminate this qualm, one has to read the text thoroughly. When this is done and if we find time in the future, we may look at this again.  
 In any case, o ur position is that the winds do dissolve in the central channel at the heart during 
verbal isolation, which involves meditating on the three life -force exertions. You have to know 
what these three are and why they are done.  
 
Lesson  37          Page 252

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
********************* 
 
The com pletion stage (practice occurring) (1) from the generation of the primordial awareness 
of appearance derived from dissolving the winds in the indestructible drop at the heart through completely loosening the channel knots at the heart in dependence on the internal condition: vajra repetition and absorption as the two stages of gathering winds (sdud) and the external 
condition: the life -force exertion of a mudra / seal(consort) (2) up to (but not including) the 
attainment of the impure illusory body’ is the nature of the stage of mental isolation. ( Pg. 39 of 
the root text ) 
 By depending on the two conditions –  (1) the internal condition and (2) the external condition - 
the winds dissolve into the indestructible drop at the heart. Due to that dissolution, the wisdom of appearance arises and that is the time when one transfers from verbal isolation to 
mental isolation .    
 One of the internal conditions  for developing the mental isolation is the practice of vajra 
recitation where, as before, one meditates on the inseparability of the tones of the winds arising, entering and abid ing from the tones of three syllables.  
 The (other internal condition) is absorption. There are many ways to translate the word 
‘samten’  in Tibetan. We shall use ‘meditative stabilisation’ instead of absorption, of which 
there are two: (1) apprehending th e entirety and (2) subsequent destruction.  
 
Let’s talk about these two (meditative stabilisations) in general. For example, if one is 
meditating on oneself as Guhyasamaja:  
• One emanates light from the heart of oneself as the deity.  
• That light strikes the environment and its  inhabitants, transforming everything into light.  
• The environment dissolves into the inhabitants and the inhabitants dissolve into oneself as the deity father and mother.  
• Then one’s consort dissolves into oneself as the principal.  
• One dissolves from top to bottom into the syllable HUM at the heart.  
• The syllable HUM gradually dissolves from bottom up until the nada (the squiggle at the top of the syllable HUM) and that dissolves into emptiness.   
Because this process of dissolving onese lf is subsequent to the dissolution of the  external 
world and its inhabitants, this process is called subsequent destruction.  
The process of dissolving oneself as the deity father and mother from top to bottom and then 
finally into emptiness that does no t involve the dissolution of the external world and its 
inhabitants is called apprehending the entirety.  
 In general, these two stages of dissolution occur during the generation stage when one dissolves the mandala into oneself. In this context here of be ing an internal condition for 
actualising mental isolation, it is related to the winds. The process is similar.   
 
Lesson  37          Page 253 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
In this context, one emanates light from the heart of oneself as the deity which strikes the 
environment and its inhabitants. The environment dissolves into the inhabitants and the 
inhabitants dissolve into oneself as the deity. Then oneself as the deity dissolves. One tries to gather all the winds at the heart. As one goes through the stages of dissolution, all the signs of dissolution associat ed with the vision of mirage and so forth (appear). This stage of dissolution 
is called subsequent destruction.  
 
But if one does not dissolve the environment and its inhabitants but one  simply gathers the 
winds into the heart without that dissolution, that dissolution is called apprehending the entirety.  
 You just need to have a rough idea of what these two meditative stabilisations (absorptions) which are posited as the internal condition are.  
 The external condition  is reliance on the consort or “ the life-force exertion of a seal ” (or 
mudra) (of which there are two): (1) The action seal (or action mudra) refers to an actual woman born from karma and (2) The wisdom seal (or wisdom mudra) who arises through the force of meditation.  
 There are three types  of action seals:  
1. The simultaneously born action seals refer to those who have achieved the meaning clear 
light.  
2. The field born action seals are those who are abiding on the stages of physical, verbal (and 
mental) isolations and who have achieved the illus ory body.  
3. The mantra born action seals refer to those who are abiding in the generation stage.  
 By depending on the method, the internal and external conditions, the channel knots at the heart are completely loosened and the winds are able to dissolve in t he indestructible drop at 
the heart.  
 Due to the dissolution of the winds into the indestructible drop, when the wisdom of appearance initially arises, that is the beginning of mental isolation and the time when one moves from verbal isolation to mental i solation.  From that point of time “ up to (but not 
including)  the attainment of the impure illusory body”  is posited as mental isolation.  
 
During the stage of mental isolation, the winds actually dissolve into the indestructible drop at 
the heart but the two conditions –  the internal and the external - must be present for that to 
happen.  
 From today’s (class), one should have a rough understanding of:  
• the two types of meditative stabilisations, the subsequent destruction and apprehending the entirety.  
• the two types of seals: the action seal and the wisdom seal.  
• the three types of action seal:  
1. The simultaneously born action seals are those who have actualised the meaning clear light.  
 
Lesson  37          Page 254 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
2. The field born action seals are those abiding on the three isolations (including 
the impure illusory body).  
3. The mantra born action seals are those abiding on the generation stage.  
 Because the simultaneously born action seal is posited as having the realisation of meaning clear light, one can see that one can achieve enlighte nment on the basis of a female body.  
 
In the Hinayana and Paramitayana, do they actually say it is possible to achieve enlightenment 
on the basis of a woman’s body? I think when one  looks at the Hinayana teachings, it is very 
clear that one cannot achiev e enlightenment as a woman. It is the same in the Paramitayana.  
 
But here in the context of Tantra, the simultaneously born action seal has achieved meaning 
clear light in which case enlightenment is a certainty. From that perspective, there is no 
difference whether one is male or female with regards to achieving enligh tenment. Doesn’t it 
become clear that a man cannot achieve enlightenment without a woman?  
 
There are also many qualms as well in this section of mental isolation. Please read up as I will 
try to finish the section on mental isolation in the next class.   
 
  
 
 Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme

FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
LESSON NO: 38   DATE:18THAUGUST 2009  
 
No matter how loud the sound of the thunder,  
It is all empty.  
No matter how beautiful the rainbow,  At the end it disappears.  
 
Milarepa said that when one has to bear the consequences of one’s own negative actions and 
life is impermanent, one should not postpone one’s Dharma practice. All of us are aware of 
these facts but we should analyse and see whether this understanding arise  in and is mixed 
with our minds.  
 
We should reflect on the empty nature of all phenomena, in particular, impermanent 
phenomena. Our mental and physical aggregates are impermanent, disintegrating and changing in each and every single moment. In particular,  we should look at the basis of 
designation of the I or self, our aggregates and how by nature they are empty of inherent 
existence but we grasp at our bodies to be inherently existent and we apprehend our bodies 
and minds to be permanent.   
 Due to these two forms of grasping, afflictions like anger and attachment are generated. Due 
to these afflictions, we engage in many negative actions and in the end, we have to suffer the 
consequences of those actions. What I have said so far is not something that we do n’t know 
but it is something for us to analyse and bring to mind continuously for it is through that that we will be able to modify our actions and behaviours of body, speech and mind. We should reflect on this advice and, as much as possible, put it into practice without procrastinating.  
 For five minutes, we will reflect on how all phenomena are empty of inherent existence. In particular, (we focus on the fact that) our bodies and minds, the bases of designation of the I, are in the nature of emptiness a nd impermanence, disintegrating and changing in each and 
every single moment. By reflecting in this way, we should be motivated not to procrastinate (any further) but to (immediately) put the teachings into practice.  
***************************  
Let us loo k again at the definition of mental isolation:  
The completion stage (practice occurring) (1) from the generation of the primordial awareness 
of appearance derived from dissolving the winds in the indestructible drop at the heart through 
completely loosening the channel knots at the heart in dependence on the internal condition: vajra repetition and absorption as the two stages of gathering winds (sdud) and the external 
condition: the life -force exertion of a seal (consort) (2) up to (but not including) the  attainment 
of the impure illusory body’ is the nature of the stage of mental isolation. ( Pg. 39 of the root 
text). 
The points we must remember are that:    
1. there are two conditions –  the internal and external conditions - that must be present 
before menta l isolation can be achieved.  
 
Lesson  38          Page 256 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
2. The internal conditions are the vajra repetition and meditative stabilisations 
(absorptions) which are the two stages of gathering the winds.  
3. There are two types of meditative stabilisations:  (1) apprehending the entirety and (2) 
subsequent destruction.  
4.  The external condition is the life -force exertion of the seal, which means relying on a 
consort. There are two types of seals: (1) the action seal and (2) the wisdom seal.  
 
When the internal and external conditions are presen t, the channel knots at the heart are 
completely loosened. Due to this, the four root winds and the five secondary winds (are then 
able to) dissolve in the indestructible drop. Through this  dissolution, the wisdom of 
appearance arises. That is the time wh en one generates the realisation of mental isolation. 
From that time, “ up to (but not including) the attainment of the impure illusory body” is posited 
as mental isolation.  
 From this, we can say that during the stage of verbal isolation, the channel knots at the heart are not completely loosened. During verbal isolation, the four root and five secondary winds do 
dissolve at the central channel at the heart. What is the difference then between verbal and mental isolations? During mental isolation , the four root and five secondary winds not only 
dissolve in the central channel at the heart but they dissolve in the indestructible drop at the heart.  
 
Question: The complete loosening of the channel knots at the heart, the dissolution of the 
winds in the indestructible drop at the heart and the arising of the wisdom of appearance, are these three simultaneous?  
Answer : I am not sure whether these three occur sim ultaneously because the channel knots at 
the heart have to be completely loosened before  the winds can dissolve in the indestructible 
drop. The wisdom of appearance can only arise after  the dissolution of the winds in the 
indestructible drop. Think whether  there is any fault in positing these three stages in the 
following order:  
• first the channel knots at the heart have to be completely loosened  
• due to the complete loosening of the channel knots at the heart, the winds dissolve in the 
indestructible drop at the heart  
• due to this dissolution, the wisdom of appearance arises  
 
Is there any fault in saying these three are simultaneous? That means the  winds dissolve in the 
indestructible drop at the heart when the channel knots at the heart are completely loosene d 
and at the same time the wisdom of appearance arises. But for the winds to dissolve into the 
indestructible drop at the heart, the channel knots at the heart have to be completely loosened first.  
 Student : If we follow the first scenario, it follows the n that the first two stages fall under verbal 
isolation due to the boundaries of the definition of mental isolation which start from the generation of the wisdom of appearance  
Response : I am not sure whether there is a big time lapse between one stage and the next, i.e., 
when  the channel knots at the heart are completely loosened, after a while the winds dissolve 
in the indestructible drop at the heart and after a while, the wisdom of appearance arises.  
 
Lesson  38          Page 257 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
I guess there is no big time gap between these three  stages. We are talking about very small 
moments of time such as a finger snap. If one posits that there is a time lag between these three events, then the qualm that was brought up will be valid.  
 Question: The definition refers to the reliance on a conso rt. What if the deity (one is practising) 
does not have a consort?  
Answer : That will come later.  
 You have to understand what mental isolation is and what are the two conditions. In order for mental isolation to happen, the channel knots at the heart must be completely loosened. The 
external condition is reliance on the consort.  
 By this definition, one can see that in order to develop mental isolation, one must rely on a seal. Is that clear from the definition? Does the definition of the mental isolation  say that in 
order to actualise the mental isolation, you need to rely on a seal? If the answer is yes, then is the opposite true, i.e., one cannot actualise mental isolation if one does not rely on a seal? One’s understanding of the definition has to be firm and one needs to clearly understand what the definition is saying.  
 There is some debate in this text as to whether one needs to rely on an action seal in order to 
actualise mental isolation but one can conclude that it is definite that to actualise m ental 
isolation, one must rely on a seal (mudra).  
 
Question: Since the definition refers to the life -force exertion of a seal as an an external  
condition, will this exclude the reliance on a wisdom (visualised) seal?  
Answer:  If it is an external seal, is that necessarily an action seal? This definition seems to be 
saying that the wisdom seal (visualised consort) can  also be considered as an external 
condition. What is the reason for it being an external seal when it is a visualised consort? This is someth ing for you to discuss.  
 1. There are some scholars who are of the view that one must rely on an action seal to develop mental isolation.  
2. There are other scholars who are of the view that in order to develop mere mental isolation, one does not need to rely on an action seal. But one definitely needs to rely on an action seal if 
one wants to develop the example clear light of the final men tal isolation in this life.  
3. Another group of scholars asserts that sharp- facultied trainees need not necessarily rely on 
the action seal either in generating (mere) mental isolation or to generate the example clear 
light of the final mental isolation. These are the views held by three different groups of scholars. It is extremely difficult for us to judge which view is correct and which view is incorrect.  
 Of these three views, there are scholars who think that Lama Tsongkhapa subscribed to the second v iew, i.e., in order to develop mere mental isolation one does not need to rely on an 
action seal. But in order to develop the example clear light of the final mental isolation in this life, then one must  rely on an action seal.  
 
Lesson  38          Page 258 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
What I have just discussed is basically a summary of two to three pages in the Tibetan text. The 
third view is not stated in this text. For our purposes here, one needs to understand that with regards to these three views, many scholars indicated that they think Lama Tsongkhapa subscribed to the second view.  
 If our system posits that one must rely on the action seal in order to develop the example clear 
light of the final mental isolation in this life, the question arises as to how Lama Tsongkhapa 
achieved enlightenment himself. L ooking at his life story, how did he display the act of 
achieving enlightenment?   He displayed the act of achieving enlightenment in the intermediate state. During the time when he was passing away, in place of the clear light of death, Lama Tsongkhapa manifested the example clear light and instead of arising in the ordinary intermediate state, Lama Tsongkhapa actualised the illusory body and on the basis of that body, he achieved enlightenment. It is clear he did not rely on an action seal during his  lif etime. Why? Because he 
wanted to show that the basis for practising his tradition is the practice of celibacy.  
 So by looking at his life story and at our assertion (which is in order to develop the example clear light of the final mental isolation in this life, one must rely on an action seal), one has to answer how Lama Tsongkhapa actualised the mental isolation in that lifetime. In that lifetime, 
he did actualise the example clear light of the final mental isolation without depending on an 
action seal.  
 
There are four states of existence:  
1. the birth existence which refers to the very moment the consciousness connects to the next life from the intermediate state  
2. “Ngon tho kyi sidpa” (Tib.) which refers to the second moment of connecting to the next life u p to and before the manifesting the mind at the time of death of that life. This can be a 
very long time.  
3. the death existence refers to when the mind at the time of death manifests  
4. the intermediate state existence comes into existence when the mind at the  time of death 
ceases.  
The reason for discussing these four existences is related to the question of Lama Tsongkhapa achieving the example clear light of the final mental isolation in that life time without 
depending on an action seal. Of these four existences, to develop the example clear light of the 
final mental isolation during “Ngon tho kyi sidpa”, one must depend on an action seal.  
 
• First, one must understand the definition of mental isolation and conclude in one’s own 
mind, “Yes, it is this and it is not that.” It is very clear in the definition. In order to develop mental isolation, one must  rely on a seal. There are two types of se als: the action seal (or 
actual consort) and the wisdom seal (visualised consort).  
• With respect to the need for relying on an actual consort, there are three views from 
different groups of scholars:  
o One view says that one needs an action seal to develop mental isolation.   
o Another view says that one needs an action seal to develop the example clear light 
 
Lesson  38          Page 259 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
of the final mental isolation and  
o The final view says the action seal is not necessary.  
• All scholars agree that one must rely on a seal to develop mental isolation but there are 
differing views as to whether this seal has to be an actual consort or not.  
• The third view indicates that in order to develop the example clear light of the final mental 
isolation, one only needs to rely on a visualised consort.  
 
According to our system, we assert that one must rely on an action seal in order to develop the 
example clear light of the final mental isolation in this life which amongst the four states of 
existen ce refers to the “Ngon tho kyi sidpa.” This particular assertion is not without a source. It 
can be found in Lama Tsongkhapa’s  Illuminating Lamp.   
 
I (the author) wonder whether this is Je Rinpoche’s intention, but since unanimous agreement 
would be diffi cult I put this (view) forward (bkod) merely to stimulate analysis. (Page 41 of the 
root text ) 
 Most people see the common side of Lama Tsongkhapa from his ordinary biography but there is also an uncommon side of him as in reality, he is the manifestation of Manjushri, Chenrezig 
and Vajrapani, the three lineages combined into one. To the common view of disciples, Lama Tsongkhapa achieved enlightenment in the intermediate state (as described above).  
 The need for (a seal) is to cultivate ( bskyangs pa ) bliss and emptiness of the generation stage 
and through that to act as the special maturer of the roots of virtue generating comprehension of the completion stage. Though there are many needs (for a seal) such as gathering the winds 
with extreme ease etc., through meditating on the methods for piercing the vital points of the body on the generation stage, it is stated that a fully qualified basis / practitioner (yul rten ) is 
extremely rare. And if one does not practice properly it is stated that there is the grea t 
disadvantage of falling to evil migrations. Therefore, it is extremely good to consummate the gross and subtle stages of the generation stage through a wisdom consort ( ye rgya ) and then 
meditate on the means for piercing ( bsnun) the points in a body of the completion stage. That 
is because such a path is capable of generating mental isolation and such disadvantages do not occur if one relies on an action seal having already obtained mental isolation. ( Page 41 of the 
root text)  
 This is an extremely import ant point: Both the practitioner and the consort one relies on must 
be fully qualified. That is extremely rare. When one is not qualified or when one’s seal is not qualified, instead of achieving enlightenment one will definitely go to the lower realms. Th is is 
something to keep in mind.  
 When one looks at the Six Session Guru Yoga, there is a section that lists out the heavy 
transgressions which include, “ Relying upon an unqualified consort;  /Sitting in union without 
the three recognitions.”  Relying upon  an unqualified consort is a very heavy tantric 
transgression and even when one’s consort is qualified, if one sits in union without the three recognitions, one still commits a very heavy transgression. When one’s consort is qualified, one has to sit in union with the three recognitions, i.e., seeing (1) one’s body as the deity’s body (2) one’s speech as mantra and (3) one’s mind as Dharma. A very heavy tantric transgression is 
 
Lesson  38          Page 260 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
said to be  even more serious than committing a root downfall of the individual liberation vows.  
 
A qualified consort (seal) refers to one of the three types of action seal -  the field born, mantra 
born and simultaneously born. When one is not qualified to do this practice, it will not lead one closer to enlightenment but only to the  lower realms so one should be very careful.  
 When is the time for one to engage in the practice of using a consort? One has to consummate 
the coarse and subtle generation stages on the basis of a wisdom consort. Then through 
striking the vital points of t he body, one develops all the realisations of the completion stage 
up to mental isolation. Having done that, when one relies on the action seal at that time, it will only be beneficial and there will be no fault. Through the practice of seeing everything a s the 
manifestation of emptiness, which is in the nature of great bliss and causing the non -dual 
wisdom of bliss and emptiness to arise in the form of deity, with those realisations and attainments, that is the time when it will be perfect for one to enter  into union with a consort.  
 There is a correction to the translation on pg. 39 of the statement from Nagarjuna’s Five Stages : 
 Regarding that, one says: To generate the three (types of) primordial awareness of mental isolation one must depend on an action seal. The intention of the statement in the Five Stages is just that. It states:  
 
From among all the illusory (bodies) the female illusory (body) is superior. The classification of the three (types of) primordial awareness, is clearly illustrated through that.  
 
“From among all the illusory (bodies) the female illusory (body) is superior ” should read as 
“From among all the illusory , the illusory female is superior .”  
 Question: Since the field born and the mantra born action seals are themselves not qualified to 
engage in consort practice, why are we allowed to take them as consorts?   
Answer : The ideal time for relying on an action seal is during mental isolation but the use of an 
actual consort exists during the generation stage. It is mentioned in the texts that by depending on an actual consort on the generation stage, the winds enter the central channel. In the earlier section on  the definition of the completion stage, it was mentioned that on the 
generation stage, there is entry, abidance and dissolution of the winds in the central channel 
but those things happen due to relying on an actual consort. Mental isolation is the ideal time 
for relying on the actual consort as there will be no danger in doing this. Relying on an action seal before  mental isolation does carry risks. Does consort practice exist prior to mental 
isolation? Yes, it exists at the generation stage.  
 Question: O n the Sutric paths and grounds, at the path of preparation, the bodhisattva will no 
longer abandon bodhicitta. In Tantra, due to the bodhisattva’s improper reliance on an action seal and going to the lower realms, will he abandon bodhicitta then ?  
Answer : The bodhisattva on the Sutric path of accumulation can still go to lower realms but 
once he reaches the path of preparation, he will not go to the lower realms. The bodhisattva 
 
Lesson  38          Page 261 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
who makes a mistake in his practice of relying on the consort will go to the lo wer realms but is 
he still a bodhisattva? Yes, he is.

Lesson  38          Page 262

FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
LESSON NO: 39   DATE: 20TH AUGUST 2009  
 
When you recite the prayer of going for refuge and generating bodhicitta and when you recite 
the word, “Buddha,” what comes to mind?  Are you able to have some idea of what a buddha is? 
Similarly, when you recite the words,  “Dharma” and “ Sangha”, what comes to mind?  
 
Focus on that and see whether you have any faith towards the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha . If 
there is some faith, then wh at kind of faith is it? How did that faith arise? The essence is that 
when you recite the words Buddha, Dharma and Sangha, think about them and reflect on them. 
At the end of that, see whether you are able to feel from your heart that the Three Jewels are 
something really inconceivable and amazing. On that basis, see whether you have any interest 
in actualising them?  
 
It is really important to meditate on the meaning of refuge because when one doesn’t have a good understanding of refuge, then one will not have any aspiration seeking the Three Jewels. 
When one doesn’t have such a  aspiration, then saying that one is a Buddhist is merely (at the 
level of) words.  
 
In order to be a real Buddhist, one needs a very strong aspiration to seek out the Three Jewels and become like them. When one has that, then one is allowed to identify oneself as a 
Buddhist. One of the benefits of going for refuge to the Three Jewels is that one won’t go to 
the lower realms. The reason for this benefit is this strong mind seeking ou t the Three Jewels 
and wanting to become like them. When one has such a strong mind, then of course one will not go to the lower realms.  
 
In order for one to be a real Buddhist, therefore, one must have meditated on these points on refuge. Before one can m editate on these points, one must have reflected on them. Before one 
can do any reflection, one must have listened to and studied the explanation of the teachings. So the three - fold activities of studying, reflection and meditation are necessary in order to for 
one to become a real Buddhist.  
 
An ideal meditation on refuge must be preceded by excellent reflection and  excellent 
reflection must be preceded by excellent studying. The more that one learns and studies, the 
greater one’s capacity for analysis and reflection will be. Then obviously the greater will be the 
extent and scope of one’s meditation. At the end of such a three -fold process i.e., after an 
excellent meditation, the result will be real faith in the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha and one 
will hav e a very strong interest in seeking them out. At the end of this process, one will become 
a real Buddhist of the highest quality. In order to achieve this, the entire process that I have 
mentioned above becomes indispensable.  
 When one thinks about it, what is the reason for doing these (Basic Program) studies, studying so many different modules? One of the reasons is to recognise and understand what the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha  really are. In particular, when we study the paths and grounds, 
they help us  understand what a buddha really is. When we have a good understanding of the 
different topics like paths and grounds and the achievement of buddhahood, of what it takes to 
 
Lesson  39          Page 263 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
become a buddha, only then will the thought arise, “Buddhahood is not really a simp le thing. It 
is something inconceivable and amazing.” Then you will really  appreciate what a buddha is. 
This comes after having done these studies. When one does not understand the paths and grounds well and one thinks of the Buddha, then nothing much com es to the mind.  
 Sometimes, it is possible that students who engage in these studies may  think; “What is the point of doing this? It is pointless and useless. What does it have to do with me?”  We should 
understand that one of the benefits for studying these topics is to understand in whom we are 
taking refuge? Who is this Buddha? In order to understand what a buddha is, then these studies are indispensable.   Through such studies, we come to recognise our objects of refuge – the Buddha, Dharma and 
Sangha.  Through our analysis, we come to develop faith in the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha . 
Through that faith, we come to develop a strong interest in seeking them out and to actualise them. When one has that kind of strong aspiration, naturally one’s actions of bo dy, speech and 
mind will be modified and change for the better. One becomes subdued, disciplined and also good -hearted. Otherwise, it is very difficult to become a good- hearted person just on the basis 
of being a Buddhist on the nominal level.  
 Just by sa ying “I am a Buddhist” and then trying to become a good person, I think this is really 
quite difficult. You should remind yourself of this. If you think that what you are doing here is 
just a waste of time, that it is useless and of no benefit, then you should understand that what 
you are studying here actually helps you to establish the foundation for being a Buddhist and without this, nothing works. It is basically to come to an understanding of what is a buddha, the 
object of our faith.

Impromptu quiz:  What is the  definition of generation stage?  
 Geshe -la: Don’t look at the book. This is not the time to look at the book. If you can write, you 
just write. If you cannot write,  that is all right. If you know the answer, that is good. If you don’t, then you have to work a bit harder.  The reason I asked this question is to see if you can remember or not. The point is that we have to revisit this material every now and then so as not to forget it.  
 
*****************  
We are now  looking at the etymology (not the definition) of the term, mental isolation.  
• The object is the mind.  
• The mind referred to here is the mind that is the root of samsara together with its 
mount, the wind.  
• The meaning of isolation in this context is the med itation on the yoga of non -dual bliss 
and emptiness.  
 
It is also taught in the Paramitayana that one must realise emptiness in order to achieve liberation. Without this realisation, one will not be freed from samsara. The introduction to the 
 
Lesson  39          Page 264 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
nature of the  mind given in the Causal Perfection Vehicle and even in the three lower classes of 
Tantra is different from that given in Highest Yoga Tantra. It is only in Highest Yoga Tantra that 
one is fully introduced to the nature of the mind, hence the need to ente r (the path of) Highest 
Yoga Tantra.    
 In Highest Yoga Tantra, one is introduced to the existence and the nature of the subtle mind and in the path of Highest Yoga Tantra, one transforms the entity of that mind into the nature 
of great bliss. One then uses that mind of great bliss, to realise e mptiness. This is only taught in 
Highest Yoga Tantra.  
  
The simultaneously born joy that is generated on the path of Highest Yoga Tantra must be used 
to realise emptiness, otherwise that joy will not lead one out of samsara and one will continue to circle  in samsara.  In this sense, it is the same as the Paramitayana which clearly explains the 
need to realise emptiness in order to achieve liberation. This is what we are actually referring to when we talk about the true path. These studies can be seen as another way of introducing us to the meaning of the Dharma Jewel.  
 The meaning of mental isolation is to generate the mind which is the root of samsara into the entity of non -dual bliss and emptiness.  
 One initially achieves the mental isolation with the ge neration of the wisdom of appearance 
after which the wisdom of increase is generated followed by  the wisdom of near -attainment 
and clear light.  
 
There is a section in the text eliminating qualms as to whether the appearance, increase and near- attainment that arise during mental isolation are necessarily fully qualified empties. To 
summarise this discussion, while on the path, the empties that arise during the first two isolations (i.e., physical and verbal isolation) are not  fully qualified empties. Even the empties 
that arise during the mental isolation are not necessarily  fully qualified empties.  
  This is why I ask you to read before coming to class so when I summarise these things in class, you can check whether you have arrived at the same  conclusio n.  
  
When are the fully qualified empties generated on the path?  The fully qualified empties are 
generated on the path during the example clear light of the final mental isolation. Why is this so? It is because during the final mental isolation, all the w inds are able to dissolve into the 
indestructible drop (at the heart) just like they would naturally do at the time of death.  
• During the time of the basis, the fully qualified empties arise only at the time of death.  
• At the time of the path, the fully qualified empties arise only during the final mental 
isolation.  
 
There are differing views on this. Some experts even claim that the empties which arise during physical isolation are fully qualified empties. For our purp oses, what we need to know is what I 
have just said: The fully qualified empties only arise during final mental isolation at the time of 
the path and the reason why this is so.  
 
Lesson  39          Page 265 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
During final mental isolation, all the winds including the pervasive wind dissolve into the 
indestructible drop and through that dissolution, the fully qualified empties arise. Although the empties do arise during the physical and speech isolations, they are not the same as the empties that arise due to all the winds dissolving in the indestructible drop during (final) 
mental isolation. The (empties) that arise prior to (final) mental isolation are not fully qualified empties.  
 Question: After the final mental isolation, i.e, after one has achieved the example clear light (of final mental isolation), henceforth the four empties of the basis of the forward process will 
never ever occur again because they have been purified?  
Answer : Yes.  
 Question:  With reference to the four empties, what is the meaning of “empty” in this context?  
Answer : When we talk about the four empties  in general, not about the path,  the word “empty” 
refers to their being empty or free from the 80 indicative conceptions. When we divide the 80 indicative conceptions according to the first three empties:  
• the first empty has 33 indicative conceptions  
• the second empty has 40 indicative conceptions and  
• the third empty has 7 indicative conceptions,  
For example, when the mind of white appearance manifests, all the minds of the 80 indicative conceptions  are stopped.  
 Question: Although the example clear light (at final mental isolation) is said to be a fully 
qualified empty, is there still dualistic appearance at that time?  
Answer: During the time of the basis, one has to understand when the fully qualified four 
emptie s arise and when they do not arise. During the time of the basis, the fully qualified four 
empties only arise at the time of death. The four empties also arise during the time of deep sleep but they are similar in type but are not fully qualified empties.  
 At the time of the basis, when does the clear light arise? The clear light arises at the time of death, i.e. when the mind of clear light of death manifests. What must happen during the time of the basis for the fully qualified clear light to arise? The time of death when all the winds naturally dissolve into the indestructible drop.  
 When we are talking about the path, the fully qualified empties do not arise during physical 
and verbal isolations. The fully qualified empties do not necessarily arise during mental 
isolation but the fully qualified empties arise during the final  mental isolation.  
 
Question:   Is the example clear light during final mental isolation an inferential cognition of 
emptiness?  
Answer:  The example clear light is not a direct perceiver. The example clear light is an 
inferential cognition which makes it a conceptual consciousness. It is a conceptual valid 
cogniser.  
 Question: (With reference to the earlier question), what are the empties of the forward process 
 
Lesson  39          Page 266 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
at the time of the basis “purified” of?  
Answer : Instead of using “purified,” one should understand this to mean will never occur again. 
After one realises the example clear light (of the final mental isolation), then what? After that , 
the empties of the reverse process arise. Simultaneously with the first empty (of the reverse 
order), one arises into the impure illusory body.  
 Answer to student’s question: During the time of the basis, when the white appearance arises, 
it is empty of the 80 indicative  conceptions. (If this is so), then at the time of the path,  of 
course (these 80 indicative conceptions) will stop. In order for it to be the wisdom of white appearance, it has to be free from the 80 indicative conceptions.  
 In this conte xt, “fully qualified” means the real thing. Although the empties do arise when we 
are sleeping they are not the real empties. The real empties arise during the time of basis, at the time of death, when all the winds dissolve naturally into the indestructible drop. Why do the real empties only arise during the time of the basis at the time of death? This is because at the time of death, the winds dissolve into the indestructible drop.  
When you understand this, then it will be easy for you to understand what happens at the time of the path.   During the time of the path, the actual (real) empties only arise during the final mental isolation. The similitudes do arise during physical and verbal isolation but they are not the real 
empties.  
 
The wisdoms of appear ance, increase and near -attainment that arise during mental isolation 
are not necessarily  the real things but the actual  wisdoms of appearance, increase and near-
attainment do arise during final  mental isolation. There is a basis for this difference. Why is 
there a time when they are not fully qualified and a certain time when they are? The difference comes in whether the winds actually completely dissolve into the indestructible drop or not. You must understand this very clearly. It is only when all the wi nds dissolve into the 
indestructible drop –  be it at the time of the basis or the time of the path that the fully 
qualified empties of the respective states arise.  
 In Highest Yoga Tantra, one tries to join two things – emptiness and bliss. Depending on the 
tenet system, emptiness can refer to:  
1. the emptiness of the self -sufficient substantially existent person.  
2. the emptiness of subject and object being different substantial entities  
3. the emptiness of existing by way of its own uncommon mode of subsistence w ithout being 
posited by the force of appearing to a non- defective awareness.  
4. the emptiness of inherent existence  
 From the perspective of the sharp -facultied trainees of the Guhyasamaja Tantra, the view of 
emptiness of those jewel -like disciples is the view of the Consequence Middle Way School in 
which all phenomena are empty of existing by way of their own character. But, on the path, the emptiness that can be conjoined with great bliss can also be the emptiness that is posited by the Autonomy Middle Way School or the Mind Only School. The emptiness of the self- sufficient 
substantially existent person is not mentioned in the text, i.e., that that can be conjoined with 
 
Lesson  39          Page 267 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
great bliss.  As to the reason why this is so, I am not sure.  One can analyse this furthe r: Can the 
bliss that arise during physical and verbal isolations be conjoined with the emptiness 
expounded by the lower schools such as the Autonomy Middle Way School or the Mind Only School?  
 Here we are talking about the emptiness that is to be conjoin ed with bliss but the bliss we are 
referring to here has to be the bliss that is generated from the completion stage of the physical 
isolation onwards which is the bliss that arises due to the winds entering, abiding and 
dissolving in the central channel. For a person on the completion stage at the level of physical or  verbal isolation, can that person conjoin the bliss he has generated with the emptiness that is expounded by the Autonomy Middle Way School and the Mind Only School?
