LESSON NO: 40   DATE: 23RD AUGUST 20 09  
 The sutras say that the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha are the objects of refuge for those seeking 
liberation. We should reflect on why they are  objects of refuge and why we need to depend on them especially for those who are seeking liberation.  
 
What is refuge? It is the understanding that the Buddha, Dhar ma and Sangha  are definitely 
able to protect us from the fears of the suffering of samsara, particularly the suffering of the 
lower realms. This is the faith of conviction that they have the ability to do this. How do they protect us? When we analyse this and see from the (depths of our hearts) that the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha  are able to do so, we will really develop faith in them.  
 This is important and we should try and see whether we can develop this refuge. All of us consider ourselves to be Buddhis ts. Some of us may even say we have been Buddhists for a 
long time. We may  also say we have been practising for a long time but when we actually check to see whether real refuge -  that thing that determines whether we are Buddhists or not 
– exists in our hearts, I guess it will probably be difficult to say with conviction that we are 
really Buddhists.  
 Therefore, refuge is not simply a verbal exercise. We should strive to practise the real refuge 
that arises in our hearts and minds. It is important we try  to do this for the next five minutes, 
reflecting and seeing whether we are able to produce this sense of real refuge in our hearts.  
 
If you were asked, “Where is the Buddha that you take refuge in? Where does he abide?” what 
will be your answer?  
 Geshe -la: Is the Buddha on the throne, at the altar or everywhere? What do you think? It is not 
easy. You are a Buddhist and you go for refuge but you don’t even know where the Buddha is. Someone asked a Muslim: “Where is Allah?” He replied that Allah is everywhe re. That is why 
they do not have anything on their altar. Do you think it is same for Buddhists?  
 Geshe -la (in response to students’ answers):   
• If you say Buddha is everywhere and you have to sit on something, you will have no choice 
but to sit on the Buddha!  
 
 
• If you say every sentient being is a buddha, that means you are a buddha. Yes, there is 
buddhanature but buddhas and sentient beings are different. If you are a buddha, you cannot be a sentient being. If you are a sentient being, you cannot be a buddha.  
• If you say that a buddha depends on what you believe in, does that mean that if you believe 
someone is a buddha that someone becomes a buddha?  
• Or do you think that the thangkas and statues are buddhas?  
 Some of you say that the Buddha is everywhere, some say the statue is the Buddha and some 
say the thangka is the Buddha. Where exactly is Buddha? So actually we don’t know. How then

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
can we have strong devotion towards and faith (in him)?  
 
(If you say that in the Sanghatasutra, it says that the Buddha abides  there), why did you only 
choose the Sanghatasutra? First you need to identify and posit what is the Sanghatasutra? Even that is difficult to recognise.   
 When we think of the Buddha, what should we be thinking of? When we say we take refuge in 
the Buddha and we think about how Shakyamuni Buddha got enlightened and so forth, that is 
comfortable for the mind.(But beyond that….).  
 
*****************  
Mental isolation is generated through the coming together of the two conditions -  the internal 
and external co nditions - when the four root  and five secondary winds dissolve into the 
indestructible drop and the four empties are generated serially. First there is (the wisdom of) 
appearance, followed by increase and near -attainment, culminating in clear light. The four 
empties that arise at this stage of mental isolation bear some similarity to but are not fully 
qualified empties.  
 This person who has achieved mental isolation is literally the mental isolationist. By  depending on the internal and external conditio ns and repeatedly meditating on them, this person will be 
able to cause all the winds, including the pervasive winds which abide in all the joints of the 
body, to dissolve into the indestructible drop such that the four empties –  the minds of white 
appearance, red increase, black near- attainment and clear light -  which normally arise during 
the time of death, are generated serially. They become the four fully qualified empties and this 
culminates in the realisation of the example clear light at the final me ntal isolation, which is a 
conceptual consciousness that realises emptiness conceptually.  
 
One will not be able to liberate oneself from samsara when one does not realise emptiness. In 
this context, until one actualises the example clear light at the final mental isolation (which is a 
realisation of emptiness), one will not  be able to free oneself from samsara. This is the 
distinguishing feature of Tantra. It is in Highest Yoga Tantra that one is introduced to the  most subtle mind. When one realises empti ness with this mind, then there is hope for one to achieve 
enlightenment.  
What is mental isolation? Mental isolation is produced in dependence on the two conditions which are the internal and external conditions. By depending on them, the winds, which refe r 
to the four root and five secondary winds, dissolve into the indestructible drop at the heart. When this happens, the four empties - the wisdoms of appearance, increase, near- attainment 
and clear light - are generated serially. From the moment the wisdom of appearance is 
generated, one is abiding on the state of mental isolation and is, literally, a mental isolationist.  
 Again by depending on the internal and external conditions, when one is able to cause all the 
winds (including the pervasive winds that abide in all the joints) to dissolve into the 
indestructible drop, the three empties (appearance, increase, near -attainment) are generated 
serially, culminating in the fourth empty, clear light. This clear light is the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 
Lesson  40          Page 270 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
• Are the three empties (appearance, increase, near -attainment) of mental isolation 
necessarily fully qualified empties? No.  
 • Do (the wisdoms of) appearance, increase and near- attainment exist during  mental 
isolation? Yes.  
 
In relation to mental isolation and the empties, we only talk about the three empties (i.e, appearance, increase and black near- attainment). We do not talk about the four empties. The 
question then arises, “Does that mean  clear light does not exist during  mental isolation?” No, 
clear light does exist.   
 
“Then why are we only talking about the three empties?” Because clear light is subsumed 
within the third empty of near -attainment and not specifically mentioned.  
 “Why do we not talk explicitly about the fourth empty?” Because the near -attainment and the 
clear light of mental isolation are similar in possessing duality, which has not subsided. It is only when one has actualised the meaning clear light that the fourth empty, clear light is mentioned separate ly because the meaning clear light is a direct realisation (of emptiness) with complete 
subsidence of all duality. Therefore, it is mentioned separately in the fourth stage.  
 If it is a clear light of mental isolation, is it necessarily the example clear light of final mental isolation?   
Geshe -la: Automatically, the answer must be no. You must get this idea. If you don’t get this, 
then you are not getting anything. Give me an illustration of this…..  
 
If it is an example clear light of the final mental isolation, is it necessarily an example clear 
light of mental isolation?  
 Is the example clear light of mental isolation necessarily a fully qualified clear light?  
The answers to the questions I am posing have already being explained and I am asking these questions to see whether you understand those explanations.   
 Geshe -la: You must first know the boundary between the fully qualified and not fully qualified 
clear light.   
 Regarding that, someone says: It follows that if it is any (of the three: white) a ppearance, (red) 
increase or (near) attainment of mental isolation it is pervasive that it is actual (dissolution) because those actual states commence from mental isolation.  
 
Ngawang Palden: There is no pervasion because although it is stated that actual (dissolution) 
commences from mental isolation it is not stated that any of the three (types of) primordial awareness of mental isolation are actual (dissolution). That is because although the Ornament of Realisation ( mngon rtogs rgyan) states that signs of  irreversibility (phyir mi ldog pa ) occur 
from the Great Vehicle path of preparation on, if one is a practitioner of the Great Vehicle path of preparation it is not pervasive that one obtains the signs of irreversibility.  
 
Lesson  40          Page 271 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
(Also your assertion) is unaccept able because in order for the actual (white) appearance, (red) 
increase and (near) attainment to occur at the time of the path, all the action winds ( las rlung ) 
must dissolve into the indestructible (drop) at the heart in just the way they do in the stages  of 
death. And though one obtains mental isolation there are many who are not capable of gathering all the pervasive winds in the heart. (Pgs. 43 –  44 of the root text)  
 
The text is saying that if it is the example clear light of mental isolation, it is not  necessarily the 
fully qualified clear light which requires that all the action (karmic) winds must  dissolve into the 
indestructible drop at the heart.  
That being the case, one has to say that when one generates the example clear light of mental 
isolat ion, all the pervasive winds do not necessarily  dissolve into the indestructible drop.  
 Student : I am having problems with the terminology here. You use the term, example clear 
light which I understand to refer only to the clear light that arises at the s tage of final mental 
isolation. It now sounds like example clear light actually refers to any clear light which arises during the stage of mental isolation, which is something completely different.…………….  
 Ven. Gyurme:  So you are operating from the premise that if it is an example clear light, it is 
necessarily the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 
Student : Yes. Is that understanding correct?  
 
I have already clearly established earlier that if it is an example clear light of mental isolation, it 
is not necessarily an example clear light of final mental isolation.   
 Geshe -la: That one must be clear.  
 Question: Does that mean that the term “example clear light” is used to refer to any clear light generated during the process of mental isolation, be it a fully qualified clear light or not? Basically, what is the boundary of the example clear light?  
Answer : The boundary is mental isolation.  
 It was established earlier that if it is an example clear light of mental isolation, it is not  
necessari ly an example clear light of final mental isolation.   
The point here is the process by which we reach this conclusion:  The text is basically saying that if it is an example clear light of mental isolation, it is not necessarily the fully qualified (actua l) clear light because for the actual clear light to arise, all the karmic winds must dissolve 
into the indestructible drop at the heart. But an example clear light of mental isolation does not necessarily arise from the dissolution of all the karmic winds  into the indestructible drop.  
 Question:  Can we say that the reason for calling it an example clear light as opposed to the 
non-fully qualified clear lights of physical and verbal isolations is because that clear light of 
mental isolation has already bee n absorbed into the indestructible drop? That is why we call it 
an example clear light even though it is not fully qualified.  
Answer : Of course the actual  example clear light is the example clear light of the final mental 
 
Lesson  40          Page 272 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
isolation. But example clear light on its own, why is it called “example” clear light?  It 
symbolises (or is an analogy for) the meaning clear light. It symbolises the fruit of the cause.   
 The actual example clear light occurs during final mental isolati on. That means prior to that,  
the path clear light is not generated  
 Question: What is the substantial cause of the example clear light of the final mental isolation? 
I am trying to clarify whether it is the wisdom of mental isolation or is it fully qualified clear 
light of the basis .  
Answer : I guess one can posit the example clear light of mental isolation to be the substantial 
cause of the example clear light of the final mental isolation. The winds which are the mount of the example clear light of  mental isolation are the co -operative conditions for the generation of 
the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 This is something we really have to understand: what is the substantial cause, i.e., the main producer, of the example clear lig ht of final mental isolation:  
• What precedes the example clear light of the final mental isolation?  
• It is the mind of the black near -attainment.  
• What comes before the mind of the black near -attainment?  
• It is the mind of red increase.  
• What comes before the mind of red increase?  
• It is the mind of white appearance.  
• These are the stages of the different minds arising during the forward process: The mind of 
white appearance   the mind of red increase  the mind of black near attainment  
culminating in the clear  light.  
 Earlier, I had posited that the example clear light of mental isolation is the substantial cause of the example clear light of the final mental isolation. But  
the example clear light of the final mental isolation is necessarily preceded by the mind of black near- attainment? Is that mind of black near -attainment the substantial cause that continues on 
to become the example clear light of the final mental isolation?  
 
Let us talk about the death dissolution process where the sense consciousnesses di ssolve into 
one another in relation with the four elements.  What does it mean when we say the earth 
(element) dissolves into the water (element)? Are we saying that the earth becomes water? If not, then how does the earth dissolve into water?  
 When we say  the earth dissolves into water, it means that the ability of the earth element to 
act as a support for the consciousness has diminished and it is no longer able to function as that element. It does not mean that earth becomes water.  
 The sense consciousne sses will gradually dissolve into one another and there will come a time 
when the sense consciousnesses cease to manifest. At that time, the minds of the 80 indicative conceptions will arise. Have the sense consciousnesses transformed into the entity of th e 
minds of the 80 indicative conceptions? No. The minds of the 80 indicative conceptions  
 
Lesson  40          Page 273 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
gradually dissolve in a certain order and cease to operate. This also happens in stages: first the 
33 indicative conceptions related to appearance dissolve and ceases  to operate, then the 40 
indicative conceptions related to increase manifest (and dissolve) and so forth (up to) the seven indicative conceptions related to near attainment  
 When we say the 33 indicative conceptions related to appearance dissolve and ceas es to 
operate, it means the ability of this mind to apprehend its respective objects degenerates. This 
is what we mean by “dissolves.”  
 In relation to the 80 indicative conceptions, at the end of the dissolution process, the mind of white appearance arise s which then dissolves and the mind of red increase arises. The mind of 
red increase dissolves and then the mind of black near- attainment arises. When the mind of 
black near -attainment dissolves, the mind of clear light then arises.  
 These are the stages in which the mind arises but the qualm is this:  
• Does the mind of white appearance become the mind of red increase?  
• Does the mind of red increase become the mind of black near -attainment?  
• Does the mind of black near -attainment transform into the entity of clear light?  
Earlier on, we had categorised the body and mind into three levels: coarse, subtle and most (extremely) subtle. Can the coarse mind then become a subtle mind? If one says that the coarse mind can transform into the entity of the subtle mind, then the reverse must also be true: that the most subtle mind can become a subtle or coarse mind. It is very difficult to say 
that the most subtle mind can become coarser. That can never happen.  
 
  
We had also talked earlier about the indestructible dro p, of which there are two: (1) the 
indestructible drop pertaining to this life and (2) the everlasting indestructible drop. The latter 
is referring to the most subtle wind and mind which will remain forever like that. It is very difficult to accept that the most subtle mind can become coarser.  
 That being the case, what then is the substantial cause of the example clear light of the final mental isolation? Where does it come from?

LESSON NO: 41   DATE: 25TH AUGUST 20 09  
 We should reflect on the meaning of the prayer of refuge and generating bodhicitta:  
• With respect to going for refuge, we have to identify the object of refuge and generate the 
faith of conviction.  
• With respect to the I who is going for refuge, we should refl ect on how that I exists as 
something merely designated through the force of conceptuality.  
• We should see whether we have faith in the Three Jewels and whether that faith moves the mind.  
 
When we recite this prayer, we should be affirming our practice of refuge and our faith in the 
Three Jewels and seek out the Three Jewels so that we can be of benefit to all living beings. This is something we should pray for all the time and reciting the refuge prayer is a verbalisation of that wish.  
 When we talk abou t sentient beings, we should focus on the people around us, sitting on our 
left and right and check whether we can see the reasons as to why we should have compassion for them. Once that is done, we can try and see whether we can develop that compassion. W e 
should try for the next five minutes to reflect on these points and see whether there is some sort of feeling in the heart.  
 
It is really important to meditate on refuge and bodhicitta because one will accumulate the 
collection of merit and by generating  the altruistic intention, one purifies a lot of negativities 
and one will naturally stop engaging in activities that will harm others.  
 When our practice of refuge and bodhicitta is good, whatever virtuous activities we undertake, such as listening to the  teachings or doing our  daily commitments, we will do them happily. 
Since the majority of you have studied quite a bit, you should be able to understand the point behind the statements I have made –  such as, “There is a lot of benefit to going for refuge 
because one can accumulate a lot of merit” -  and the reasons that render them true.   
 It is said that going for refuge and generating the altruistic intention are extremely important. We should try our best to do this in our daily lives even though we can  spend no more than one 
or two minutes on it. This is something we have to do everyday.  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.2  THE CONCENTRATION S OF TWO TRUTH S 
 
This has two (subdivisions):  
1 Completion stage of the individual two truths  
2 Completion stage of the indivisible two truths  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.2.1  COMPLETION STAGE OF THE INDIVIDUAL TWO T RUTHS.  
 This has two (subdivisions):  
 
Lesson  41          Page 275 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
1 Completion stage of the relative illusory (body)  
2 Completion stage of the ultimate clear light  
 
2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.2.1.1  COMPLETION STAGE OF THE RELATIVE ILLUSORY (B ODY)  
 
The completion stage (practice) (1) from establishing the actual body of the deity adorned with signs and marks through the metaphoric clear light of the final mental isolation acting as the co-active condition (lhan cig byed rkye n) and the fundamental winds ( gnyug rlung) that serve 
as its mount acting as the substantial cause (nyer len), concurrent with establishing near attainment of the reverse order through the winds slightly moving from the metaphoric clear light of the final mental isolation, (2) up to generating the proximate causes which are the 
means of (achieving) the actual clear light, is the nature of the stage of the illusory body.  
 (Interpreter ’s correction: The first heading should read as “ THE CONCENTRATION OF TWO 
TRUTHS .”)  
 When the wind which is the mount of the example clear light of the final mental isolation stirs, the mind becomes coarser. When the mind of black near -attainment of the reverse order 
manifests, concurrently that is the time when the impure illusory body is achieved (or first established).  
• The substantial cause for the impure illusory body is the subtle wind upon which the 
example clear light of the final mental isolation is mounted.   
• The co -operative condition for the impure illusory body is the example clear light of the 
final mental isolation.  
By depending on these two conditions, one achieves the impure illusory body which is said to be the actual body of the deity adorned with the signs and exemplifications.  
 
In relation to the (minds of) ap pearance, increase and near attainment;  
• The forward order is the process by which the mind becomes subtler i.e. the mind moves 
from the coarse to a subtler level. First the coarse sense consciousnesses dissolve into one 
another followed by the dissolution of the minds of the 80 indicative conceptions. After that, the mind of white appearance manifests and then dissolves into the mind of red increase which dissolves into the mind of black near- attainment, culminating in the most 
subtle mind of clear light.  
• The reverse order is the opposite of the forward order in that the mind becomes coarser. Starting from the most subtle mind of clear light, the next minds which are coarser than 
that are the minds of black near - attainment followed by red increase, white a ppearance 
and the 80 indicative conceptions after which all the sense consciousnesses start to 
manifest.  
  When you do not understand the forward and reverse orders, then you will not understand how the mind becomes coarser from the mind of clear light. For example, (you will not understand) how when the mind of near -attainment arises, how that is concurrent with 
entering the intermediate state.   
 
 
Lesson  41          Page 276 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
The example clear light of the final mental isolation is the most subtle mind. What kind of mind 
will arise after that? It is the mind of black near -attainment and concurrent with the 
manifestation of that mind is the  actualisation of the impure illusory body, i.e., the impure 
illusory body arises concurrently with the mind of black near- attainment of the reverse process 
from the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 In order to achieve the impure illuso ry body, it must be preceded by the example clear light of 
the final mental isolation.   The deities’  bodies that are meditated on during the generation stage and completion stage of the physical, verbal and even mental isolations are not the actual bodie s of the deities.  They 
are mere mental projections of the mind and it is a meditation through mere belief. The actual deity’s body only arises at this time.  
 The boundary of the impure illusory body is said to be “ up to generating the proximate causes 
which are the means of (achieving) the actual clear light.” That is the nature of the stage of the 
illusory body.  
 This part is difficult. If you don’t want to look at the definition in greater  detail but prefer to keep it simple, you just need to know that the impure illusory body exists from the time of the 
mind of near -attainment of the reverse order which arises from the example clear light of the 
final mental isolation up to just before one attains the meaning clear light. Everyone should know when the impure illusory body exists: It is from the time it becomes into being up to just before the meaning clear light.  
 But when one analyses the definition further, it doesn’t  say the illusory body exists up to prior 
to the attainment of the meaning clear light.  
 Just keep this in your mind: This illusory body exists from the near -attainment of the reverse 
process up to but not including the attainment of the meaning clear ligh t.  
 I am not stating this as the definitive conclusion but I think we may have to differentiate the impure illusory body from the stages of the impure illusory body, that there is a difference between the two. The definition in the text describes the enti ty of the stage of the (impure) 
illusory body.  
 The impure illusory body  is established concurrently with the mind of near -attainment. After 
that mind is the mind of increase followed by the mind of appearance. This is the reverse order and these are the stages of the illusory body. There are also the stages of the forward process 
– appearance, increase and near- attainment – which can also be posited as the stages of the 
illusory body.  
 After one has achieved the illusory body, one meditates repeatedly on the stages of dissolution 
of the two concentrations (apprehending the entirety and subsequent destruction) in order to try to achieve the meaning clear light until one receives a sign that one is about to achieve the meaning clear light. I think we can po sit that sign to be the proximate cause.   
 
Lesson  41          Page 277 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Prior to achieving the meaning clear light, one goes through the (minds of) appearance, increase and near- attainment of the forward process again which then culminates in the 
meaning clear light. That meaning clear light together with the three minds preceding it are posited to be the stages of the meaning clear light.  
 This is my view. If we take this sign to be the proximate cause, after one has received the sign 
then one is going to actualise the meaning clear light. But before one can do that, one has to go 
through the appearance, increase and near- attainment of the forward process again. These 
three are not clear lights but they are stages of the meaning clear light. Including the meaning clear light, those four are also not stages of the (impure) illusory body.  
 When we posit such a difference, then we can explain the definition in the text which tries to establish the boundary of the illusory body “ up to generating the proximate causes which are 
the means of (achieving) the actual clear light .” (Actual clear light = meaning clear light)  
 When does the impure illusor y body  exist? It exists from the time it comes into existence up to 
just before achieving the meaning clear light.  
 To understand what I have just said, it shows how important it is to look at the material continuously as everything is interrelated. If you  do not understand the stages of mental 
isolation, example clear light and the stages of the meaning clear light, then you will find it difficult to understand whatever I have said so far.   
 
This is what I think. I think that it is better to posit the app earance, increase and near-
attainment that precede the meaning clear light to be the stages of the meaning clear light. If not, one will run into difficulties.  
 It will be good if you are interested in the definition and look at it in more detail and discuss it among yourselves. If not, at least you need to understand how this impure illusory body comes into being when the two conditions –  the substantial cause and the co -operative condition – 
come together.  
 
• The illusory body here is the actual  body of th e deity. It cannot be the coarse body of the 
person who has the realisation as it is something separate from the coarse body.  
• At the time of achieving the illusory body, what arises is not only the deity’s body but the 
entire supported and supporting mandala, which arise from the mere winds and mind.  
• The impure illusory body is a fully qualified wisdom body the nature of which is  
consciousness. It is not consciousness but it is the reflective appearance of the mind of 
clear light.  
• The impure illusory body is also referred to as a vajra body but it is not the actual (or fully qualified) vajra body. It is something similar. Why not? For it to be a fully qualified vajra body, it has to be immutable but the continuum of this vajra body of a similar type is severed and ceases upon achievement of the meaning clear light so it is not immutable and therefore not a fully qualified vajra body.  
 
 
Lesson  41          Page 278 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Keep in mind what has been said and on this basis, read the text (again)  and then discuss 
among yo urselves and see whether there are any contradictions. In this way, both of us will 
benefit.  
 
• When one achieves the illusory body, there is no more intermediate state. Such an illusory 
body is the path sambhogakhaya .  
• I have mentioned earlier that when one  achieves the example clear light of the final mental 
isolation, that is the thing that eliminates ordinary death. In place of ordinary death, one 
achieves the example clear light of the final mental isolation which is the path dharmakhaya.  
• Why is the impu re illusory body called “impure”? It is so called because it is not purified of 
the obstructions to liberation (afflictive obstructions) and is also termed the contaminated body.  
 From this we can see that in order to cut the continuum of samsara, one must achieve mental isolation. Cutting the continuity of afflictions is not an easy thing because one cannot do it without mental isolation. In order to develop mental isolation, it mu st necessarily be preceded 
by the realisations of verbal isolation, which in turn is preceded by physical isolation. Before that is the generation stage.   Sentient beings have both a gross, temporary body and mind and a more subtle, fundamental body and m ind.  As long as the gross body performs actions such as moving and so on, for that 
long the fundamental body does not arise in a similar aspect (bzhin lag). If one stops (chad) the 
gross body performing actions such as moving and so on, by the strength of dissolving all gross 
winds without exception in the indestructible drop at the heart, the fundamental body arises in a similar aspect (bzhin lag) and performs the actions of coming and going.  
 Although both gross and subtle mental consciousnesses simultaneously exist in the continuums of sentient beings, it does not contradict sutra nor is there the error that in consequence there would be two continuums because for as long as the gross mind performs the actions of 
holding its focal object and aspect, for that long subtle mind does not hold its focal object and aspect. When the subtle mind holds its focal object and aspect, the gross mind is stopped and does not exist. (Pg. 47 of the root text)  
 We have both a coarse and subtle body and mind. When the coar se body and mind are 
functioning, although the most subtle body and mind are there, they are not functioning. The 
most subtle body and mind only function when the coarse body and mind have ceased. As long 
as the coarse mind performs the action of apprehend ing its object, the most subtle mind 
cannot do likewise.  
 With respect to the place the illusory body arises, there are three schools of thought:  
• One school of thought asserts that the illusory body arises only internally which refers to inside the heart in the central channel..  
• Another school of thought asserts that the illusory body arises only externally, i.e., outside 
of one’s body.  
 
Lesson  41          Page 279 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• Yet another school of thought asserts that the illusory body can arise both internally and 
externally which means that t he yogi has a choice. It depends on how he projected his 
intention. If he intends for the illusory body to arise internally, then the illusory body will arise internally. If he has projected his intention for the illusory body to arise outside of his body,  then it will arise outside the body. It is up to the yogi.  
 Jetsun Chokyi Gyaltsen  believes the third school of thought through quoting scriptural 
authority. He asserts the illusory body can arise both internally and externally. When we look at the monastic texts of both the upper and lower tantric colleges, they assert the illusory bod y 
necessarily arises externally. Whereas Khedrub Nolsan Gyatso and Galwa Ensapa assert that 
the illusory body initially  arises internally.  
 Ngawang Palden: One should analyse which of the three assertions is the (final) intention of Je Rinpoche. (Pg. 48 o f the root text)  
 What I have said pertaining to the arising of the illusory body can be found in pages 47 and 48 of the root text. Upon reading, if your conclusion is different from what I have mentioned, you 
can bring it up.  
 
You need to know the follow ing: 
1. The time the illusory body is attained.  
2. The illusory body is:  
• in the entity of consciousness and is a fully qualified wisdom body  
• similar to the vajra body but is not a fully qualified vajra body  
• a fully qualified deity’s body with signs and exemplifications which is different from the ordinary coarse body  
3. It is called an impure illusory body because it is not purified of the obstructions to liberation.  
 Question: The minds that accompany the impure illusory body, what are those minds?  
Answer:   The example clear light of the final mental isolation is the condition for the impure 
illusory body to arise. The impure illusory body arises concurrently with the mind of near -
attainment. So when the mind of near - attainment manifests, the example clear  light of the 
final mental isolation ceases.   Only when the example clear light of the final mental isolation ceases can  the coarser mind of near- attainment manifest. When the mind becomes (even) coarser, the mind of near -
attainment ceases and the mind o f increase manifests and so forth.  
 At that time the illusory body is present. It is not the most subtle mind. It is in the nature of the most subtle wind but it is not the most subtle mind. There are different views on this as some assert that the illuso ry body is form and is not consciousness and neither is it atomically 
established.  
 Is the illusory body a path? Yes, it is. In Highest Yoga Tantra, a path is not necessarily a 
consciousness.  
 
Lesson  41          Page 280 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Question: Does the coarse body still exist at the time of the impure illusory body?  
Answer : At that time of the impure illusory body, the relationship with the coarse body has not 
yet been severed.  
 Question: What does the sense consciousnesses of that coarse body perceive at that time?  
Answer : Everything appears as pure.  
 (Interpreter ’s correction: On page 46 of the root text, “ Differentiating the subtle and gross 
bodies through the strength of meditation; Differentiating the subtle and gross bodies through karma and affliction, ” the word “differentiating” should read as “separating.” )

LESSON NO: 42   DATE: 27TH AUGUST 2009  
 
I have been saying that meditating on refuge and generating bodhicitta is very important. 
When we say we receive the blessings of the Three Jewels, the real b lessing is when we 
develop refuge and are able to generate the altruistic intention in our minds. That is the real 
blessing of the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha .  
 
When we make requests to the Three Jewels, we say, “Please grant me blessings. Please bless 
me.”  In Tibetan, the word “blessing” or “jin lab” has the connotation of transforming into 
something better, to improve (oneself).  
 
When we have refuge in our hearts and develop the altruistic intention that is when the 
blessings of the Three Jewels enter our mental continua. What happens then? As I had 
mentioned before, along with those blessings, we accumulate an immeasurable collect ion of 
merit and purify a vast amount of negativities.  
 
It is not enough simply to receive those blessings, but from our side we have to ensure that 
they enter our hearts by our generating refuge and bodhicitta. By bringing the objects of refuge to our mi nds and thinking about suffering, we go for refuge to the Three Jewels and, through 
our contemplation of the suffering of sentient beings, we develop compassion and bodhicitta.  
 We should try to do this in the next five minutes and see whether we are able  to develop those 
feelings of refuge and bodhicitta in our hearts and produce a happy mind as we do so. When we manage to generate some sort of feeling from this, then we would have received the blessings of Three Jewels within this short time.  We stand to gain from that. Meditation and 
reflection are not about the mind becoming very dull or not thinking of anything at all. Meditation is done with an alert and active mind.  
 
*****************  
The completion stage (practice) (1) from establishing the actual body of the deity adorned with signs and marks through the metaphoric clear light of the final mental isolation acting as the 
co-active condition (lhan cig byed rkyen) and the fundamental winds ( gnyug rlung) that serve 
as its mount acting as the substantial cause (nyer len), concurrent with establishing near 
attainment of the reverse order through the winds slightly moving from the metaphoric clear 
light of the final mental isolation, (2) up to generating the proximate causes which are the 
means o f (achieving) the actual clear light, is the nature of the stage of the illusory body . (Pg. 
46 of the root text)  
“Up to  generating the proximate  causes” means “includes generating the proximate causes.” It 
should read, “ Up to but not including generating t he proximate causes, ” because the proximate 
causes for achieving the meaning clear light are part of the stage of meaning clear light and not part of the stage of the illusory body.  
 
 
Lesson  42          Page 282 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
In relation to this definition, as I said in the last lesson, one has to differentiate the illusory 
body from the stage of the illusory body.  
• When does the impure illusory body exist?  It exists from the second moment when one 
arises from the example clear light of the final mental isolation up to but not including the attainmen t of meaning clear light.  
• When does the stage of the illusory body exist?  That exists from the mind of near -
attainment of the reverse order, after arising from the example clear light of the final mental isolation, up to but not including the generation of the proximate causes for 
achieving the meaning clear light.  
 The reason why I say that there is a difference between the illusory body and the stage of the illusory body is due to the (above) definition which is of the stage of the illusory body. The 
boun dary is up to but not including generating the proximate causes which are the means of 
achieving the meaning clear light. Otherwise, if we do not make such a differentiation, we are 
going to have problems.  
 In order for the person who has obtained the impure illusory body to achieve the meaning clear light, he meditates over and over again on the stages of dissolution of the two concentrations (apprehending the entirety and subsequent destruction). Through that meditation, he will receive a sign that he is about to achieve the meaning clear light. On his way to achieving the meaning clear light, he will generate the mind of appearance which will dissolve and the mind of increase will arise. This mind of increase then dissolves and the mind of near -attainment  arises, subsequently culminating in the meaning clear light.  These three 
minds (white appearance, red increase and black near- attainment) prior to the meaning clear 
light are the stage of the meaning clear light and not the stage of the illusory body.  
 
The achievement of the meaning clear light is not necessarily  preceded by the impure illusory 
body. For example, in the case of Shakyamuni Buddha, when he was on the Sutric path and on 
the 10
th bodhisattva ground, he was conferred the third initiation, the  wisdom knowledge 
initiation. Through receiving that initiation, he generated the minds of appearance, increase, and near -attainment and subsequently, he manifested the meaning clear light. In the text, at 
which path did Shakyamuni Buddha enter Tantra? He entered the Tantric path at the fourth stage of meaning clear light. Those three minds (white appearance, red increase and black near- attainment) prior to that are necessarily the stage of the meaning clear light.   
 If you want to keep it simple, then pertaining to the impure illusory body, it exists concurrently with the mind of black near -attainment of the reverse order (after arising from the example 
clear light of the final mental isolation) up to just prior to th e achievement of the meaning clear 
light.  
 
Question:  The beginning when the impure illusory body exists and the beginning of the stage of 
the illusory body –  are they the same?  
Answer : The impure illusory body is achieved concurrently when the mind of near -attainment 
of the reverse order is manifested from the example clear light of the final mental isolation. 
The stage of the illusory body starts with the mind of near -attainment.  
 
 
Lesson  42          Page 283 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Question:  When does the impure illusory body and the stage of the illuso ry body end?  
Answer:  In terms of terminology, we say that the impure illusory body “ceases” but there is no 
terminology describing the cessation of the stage of the illusory body. If we want to look at its 
cessation, w e can say that the stage of the illuso ry body ceases when the mind of white 
appearance prior to the achievement of meaning clear light arises. When that mind of white appearance arises, that is the stage of the meaning clear light. Since that is not the stage of the illusory body, it ceases at  that point.  
 Question: At the last class, it was posited that the proximate causes are attaining the signs for the (impending) achievement of the meaning clear light. Now it sounds like the proximate causes are achieving the mind of white appearance prior to the arising of meaning clear light. 
So do the proximate causes and the mind of white appearance (prior to the achievement of the meaning clear light) arise serially or concurrently?  
Answer : When we posit that the stage of the meaning clear light starts  from the mind of white 
appearance prior to the achievement of meaning clear light, what is the fault in saying this?  
 Question:  That means the signs of achieving the meaning clear light falls under the illusory 
body and they are not the proximate causes,  right?   
Answer : When one is about to achieve the meaning clear light, one will get a sign but this sign 
is not the cause of the meaning clear light. “Proximate” means near to, i.e., one is near to the 
achievement of meaning clear light. This is something  worth pondering over. If we do not posit 
in such a way, then we will have difficulty dealing with the definition.  
 
******************  
When one has achieved the meaning clear light, the impure illusory body dissolves into the meaning clear light.  
 What is the basis for actualising the impure illusory body? The basis is the  example clear light 
of the final mental isolation. When compared to the meaning clear light, this example clear light would be considered coarse so when one achieves the meaning clear lig ht, the mind 
becomes subtler.    The impure illusory body, which is the mount of the example clear light of the final mental isolation, dissolves into the meaning clear light. This is according to Khedrup Je’s view. There is also a qualm related to this view.   
• We are trying to establish the reason why the impure illusory body ceases upon the achievement of the meaning clear light.    
• The qualm is this: if both the example clear light of the final mental isolation and the meaning clear light are extremely subtle minds, how does one posit that one mind is subtler than the other?  
• The answer is that the example clear light of the final mental isolation which is mounted on the impure illusory body is a conceptual consciousness while the meaning clear light is a  
non-conceptual consciousness. When the meaning clear light manifests, the example clear 
light of the final mental isolation has transformed into the entity of a direct perceiver. Therefore, both the winds and the mind (the example clear light of the final  mental 
isolation and the impure illusory body) dissolve into the meaning clear light.  
 
Lesson  42          Page 284 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Question: What is the reason for the impure illusory body dissolving when the actual clear light is generated?  
 Ngawang Palden: The Notes of the Teachings of Khedrub J e states:  
 
When the actual clear light first dawns, the winds which are the mount of the 
primordial awareness of metaphoric clear light become extremely subtle and by the 
strength of that, the impure illusory body also condenses (bsdus) and dissolves into 
clear light.  
 However, both the metaphoric clear light of the final mental isolation and the actual meaning clear light are fundamental minds and similarly both the winds that are the mounts of those two are fundamental winds. Therefore one must analyse ho w a gross and subtle difference 
exists.  
 Although both the metaphoric clear light of final mental isolation and the actual meaning clear light are similar in being extremely subtle fundamental minds, there is a difference in their being conceptual and non -conceptual (rtog med). Since all gross and subtle conceptions 
together with (their) winds must dissolve at the time of actual meaning clear light, the winds that are the mount of conception and act as the direct substantial cause of the illusory body 
must dissolve. ( Pgs. 49 –  50 of the root text ) 
 
The impure illusory body is “purified” upon achievement of the meaning clear light which 
means that it no longer exists. Here we are trying to establish the reason why the impure illusory body ceases upon the achievement of the meaning clear light.  
 It is not easy to understand the reason why the impure illusory body disappears upon the achievement of the meaning clear light. What is the reason stated in the text? The answer is this: What is the basis of actualising the impure illusory body? The basis upon which the impure illusory body depends is the example clear light of the final mental isolation which is a conceptual consciousness. When one actualises the meaning clear light, the extremely subtle mind transfor ms from the entity of a conceptual consciousness into a direct perceiver. Due to 
this transformation, the impure illusory body that was dependent on the earlier conceptual consciousness disappears because that conceptual consciousness, which is its basis, has ceased 
and disappeared.   
 
Before you analyze and discuss this, you have to understand what the text says about this issue. You need to understand why the impure illusory body, which is a deity’s body, disappears upon the achievement of the meaning clear light.    
Question: Is the b asis of actualising the impure illusory body and its substantial cause the same 
thing?  
Answer : It is not the same. The basis for actualising the illusory body is both the subtle wind  
and mind.  
 
 
Lesson  42          Page 285 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Question: During the stage of the illusory body, when the exam ple clear light manifests, does 
the illusory body exist?  
Answer : Yes, there is no reason for it to cease (at that time ).  
 
You must understand why the impure illusory body ceases upon the achievement of the meaning clear light. The text is saying that when  the meaning clear light is achieved, all the 
conceptual consciousnesses must necessarily dissolve which means the example clear light of 
the final mental isolation must necessarily dissolve. When this happens, the impure illusory 
body which depends on tha t example clear light must also dissolve because that example clear 
light is the basis for actualising the impure illusory body.  
 
***************  
We had talked earlier about the indestructible drop of which there are two:  
1. the indestructible drop pertaining  to this life  
2. the everlasting indestructible drop  
 The everlasting indestructible drop refers to the extremely subtle wind and mind which are immutable because they last forever. If one accepts this, does the example clear light, which is an extremely subtle mind, go out of existence? (If it does go out of exis tence, that means) the 
example clear light which is an extremely subtle mind is not indestructible and not immutable!  
 
Is the nature of our extremely subtle wind and mind the same as the Buddha’s? In The 
Tathagata Essence , it is said all sentient beings po ssess buddha lineage, i.e., all sentient beings 
can become buddhas. The factor of clarity and knowing is posited to be the conventional 
buddha lineage. The ultimate buddha lineage is the emptiness of that factor of clarity and knowing.  
 From the perspective of Highest Yoga Tantra, all sentient beings not only possess buddha lineage but they also possess the extremely subtle winds and minds that enable them to actualise the Buddha’s form and truth bodies since beginningless time. The extremely subtle mind acts as the cause to actualise the dharmakaya (truth body) while the extremely subtle winds act as the cause to actualise the rupakaya (form body).  
 That being the case, the extremely subtle mind will go all the way to enlightenment. It follows then that t he example clear light of the final mental isolation goes all the way to clear light…..   
 The impure illusory body that is attained is a deity’s body but that deity’s body is separate from the original body that one has. Although this is so, does it mean that one has severed the connection with one’s original body?  The answer is no.  
 When one achieves the impure illusory body in this life, at that time one has not severed the connection with the coarse body. The impure illusory body is not able to fully discard the coarse body and therefore has to re -enter it. That does not mean that the impure illusory body 
is stuck inside the coarse body. It can separate from it and go out to do other tasks. The impure illusory body will then re -enter the coarse body after completing those tasks. This is what is 
meant by mixing (or blending) of birth with the nirmanakaya of the waking state.  
 
Lesson  42          Page 286 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• When one achieves the example clear light of the final mental isolation, that is the mixing 
of the dharmakaya.  
• When one achieves the impure illusory body, that is the mixing of the sambhogakaya.  
• When the impure illusory body re -enters the coarse body again, that is the mixing of the 
nirmanakaya.  
 In the last lesson, I had mentioned that the impure illusory body is a vajra body but is not a fully 
qualified vajra body because it is not immutable. This is because it ceases upon the 
achievement of the meaning clear light.  
 You need to remember this:  
• The impure illusory body is established from the subtle wind which is the mount of the exam ple clear light of the final mental isolation .  
• The impure illusory body that one achieves has not severed its connection with the coarse 
body of this life.  
• When one achieves the meaning clear light, the impure illusory body ceases.  
• The impure illusory body ceases because the basis for its coming into being,  the example clear light of the final mental isolation, which is a conceptual consciousness, dissolves when 
one manifests the meaning clear light. Since its basis has disappeared, the impure illusory  
body will also cease.  
 Having said that, this qualm arises: The example clear light and impure illusory body are the extremely subtle winds and mindd and if they can go out of existence, it is akin to saying the extremely subtle winds and minds can go out  of existence. Do one’s extremely subtle winds 
and minds go on to enlightenment? If they do go on to enlightenment, what about the example clear light?  
 If one is very certain that one’s extremely subtle winds and minds will go to enlightenment, then one s hould not doubt the example clear light.  
 In response to a student’s assertions (inaudible): One can analyse your assertion in this way:  
You accept that the extremely subtle winds and mindd will go to enlightenment, but you say 
that the example clear ligh t is a conceptual consciousness and (therefore) will not go to 
enlightenment. If it does not go on to enlightenment, it follows that it is a coarse mind. It follows that a coarse mind is a conceptual consciousness. It follows yours is a subtle mind and therefore is a direct perceiver. That is the consequence. (Your assertions are completely 
wrong!)  
 It is good to debate about this as there are many things to think about. What you are bringing up is good because you will learn. People who are shy and don’t say anything will not learn anything. If you keep quiet and at the same time think that you know the material that is no way to learn.   When you bring things up for discussion and are able to present your points, 
then you can really say that you understand .  
 
 
Lesson  42          Page 287 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Of course when we are debating it is natural for us to blush or feel embarrassed because of our 
ego. What we are doing here is the real cause for learning the subject. There is no reason to feel ashamed for making mistakes because we are all learning a nd this is part of the process. 
For those who laugh at those who make mistakes, do you think they understand? I think it is difficult to say they do.  
 Question: At the time of the basis, when all the 80 indicative conceptions have dissolved into 
the four empties, since all the conceptual consciousnesses have dissolved, can we say that the 
clear light of death which manifests is a direct perceiver though we do not realise it since we 
are in a fainting state?  
Answer : What does such a direct perceiver perceiv e?  
Student : It is a non -valid direct perceiver!  
 Question:  Are the extremely subtle winds and mind impermanent phenomena?  
Answer : Yes, one is form and the other is consciousness and they are impermanent 
phenomena, undergoing momentary change.  
 Question: W here are the predispositions of karma  deposited?  
Answer : The predispositions of karma are something very difficult (to explain) . To make it easy, 
is it all right to say that they are deposited on the most subtle mind? Do you feel comfortable with this statement?  
 I think it is all right because it is the most subtle mind that goes from life to life and therefore they have to be the carrier of the karmic imprints. That is why we are in samsara because what 
make us circle in samsara are these karmic imprints.   
 The imprints of what one does are placed by the coarse mind and at that time, one’s subtle mind is not manifest. If that is so, how can we place something on it? This particular point is extremely complicated. When we don’t think about it, things seem easy . In the twelve links, 
there is the link of compositional activities (karma) and in the end, we say that the karmic imprints are planted on the consciousness which moves from life to life and so forth.  
 The reality is that nothing is easy because all the things that look very easy become difficult when we start to think about it.  
 
We will try to finish the section on the illusory body in the next class. Please try to read up the 
section on the illusory b ody again and it will be good if you can bring up some good questions 
pertaining to this topic in the next class

LESSON NO: 43   DATE: 1ST SEPTEMBER 2009  
 There is a saying which goes, “If one doesn’t see the hell realms, one w on’t have faith in the 
Buddha”. To have real refuge in our hearts, we must first generate the refuge that is shared with the persons of small capacity and develop the fear of the suffering of samsara in general 
and, in particular, the suffering of the lowe r realms.  
 
If we are unable to develop the refuge of the persons of small capacity, then there is no way we can develop the refuge of the persons of medium capacity. Without that, we can forget about generating the refuge of the (persons of) great capacity.  
 Therefo re, it is very important for us to strive in the beginning to develop this refuge (of the 
persons of small capacity) not just today but continuously. We will spend the next five minutes to reflect on the suffering of the lower realms and see whether or not a real fear of that 
suffering arises in our minds.  
  
********************  
Impromptu quiz:  
1. What is the final basis for actualising the illusory body?  
2. What are the boundaries for the illusory body?  
3. Is the illusory body a buddha or not?  
4. After one achieves the illusory body, does one achieve enlightenment with that illusory body?  
5. After having achieved the illusory body, does one achieve enlightenment in that same lifetime or not?  
 There are different explanations in answer to Question 5.  
 
Now, (someone says :) a jewel -like disciple does not attain enlightenment in one life and one 
body because when obtaining union he must exchange his former gross body.  
 Ngawang Palden: There is no pervasion because there is no pervasion that in exchanging his gross body he d iscards it.  
 Moreover, in the notes composed by our guru Yanchen Galo (dbyangs can dga’ blo) to the statements of Shingza Rinpoche (shing bza’), it is stated that when the former Panchen (Lama) conferred the empowerment of Guhyasamaja at Drak yepa (brag g.ye pa) to (the monks of) Gyuto, he stated that the Tibetans had asked Atisha whether attaining enlightenment in one body and one life existed. To this Atisha replied that attaining enlightenment in one life existed but attaining enlightenment in one body did not exist. ( Pg. 49 of the root text)  
 There is the view that a jewel -like disciple can achieve enlightenment in one lifetime and in one 
body but according to this text, Lama Atisha had said there is enlightenment in one life but not in one body . When one achieves the learner’s union, one exchanges the body but one does not 
 
Lesson  43          Page 289 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
discard it.  
 
Even when one achieves the impure illusory body, one has not severed the relationship with the coarse body. The impure illusory body still has to re -enter the co arse body whereas the 
pure illusory body has severed the relationship with that body. It can enter the old coarse body again and abide there but it does not necessarily have to do so. In fact, the pure illusory body is able to enter and abide in another bo dy other than one’s own coarse body.  
 
**************  
 To help us to gain an understanding of the illusory body, 12 analogies are used to illustrate it:  
 (Concerning) the illusory body, the Compendium of the Vajra Primordial Awareness (ye shesrdo rje kyi kun las btus) states the twelve examples such as the illusory, the moon in water and so on to illustrate (the illusory body).  
 When it is generated as the body of Vajradhara from mere winds and mind:  
 1. It is like an illusory person since it is nothing but mere wind and mind though it is complete in all the main and secondary limbs.  
2. It is like the (reflection of the) moon in water since it pervades everywhere.  
 It arises everywhere to thos e who are to be subdued.  
 
3. It is like an hallucinated (mig yor) shadow body of a man, since it lacks flesh and bones etc.  
4. It is like a mirage (smig rgyu) since it moves in each moment.  
5. It is like a dream body separated from the gross body in a dre am, since it is a body 
established from mere wind and mind.  
6. It is like an echo (brag ja) since it appears to others though it belongs in the same continuum 
as the maturational body.  
7. It is like the city of Gandharas (dri za’i grong khyer) since it is the supporting and supported mandala of that body.  
 The city of Gandharas refers to the smell eaters. When they appear, the city they live in appears also. Likewise when the illusory body arises, it does so together with the the 
supporting and supported m andala.  
 
8. It is like a apparition (mig ‘phrul) since it is one but appears as many.  
 It is like seeing one moon appearing as two moons.  
 9. It is like a rainbow (‘ja’ tshon) since the five colours of the body appear unobstructed and unmixed (ma ‘drespa).  
 The illusory body is said to be able to emanate five -coloured lights without the colours mixing 
up and these lights are said to radiate for a great distance.  
 
Lesson  43          Page 290 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
10. It is like lightning in the midst of clouds since it abides inside the aggregates of the maturation body.  
11. It is like a bubble (chu bur) erupting (rdo!) in extremely clear water since it arises suddenly 
(thol) from within an empty state.  
 The illusory body arises from clear light (suddenly) like bubbles appearing suddenly on the 
water surface.  
 
12. It is like the appearance of a reflection (gzugs brnyan) of Vajradhara in a mirror since it is complete in all main and secondary limbs. ( Pg. 50 of the root text)  
 Amongst these analogies, a dream body is said to be the best one. As we fall asleep, we have almost similar experiences of the (minds of) the appearance, increase and near -attainment 
which then culminates in the clear light of sleep. Within that state, a dream body can arise from the clear light of sleep and when the dream body re -enters the coarse body, one wakes 
up from the dream.   Question: Does the dream body leave the coarse body when one dreams? Do the winds have to enter the central channel for the dream body to come about?  
Answer : When we dream, we have a subtle dream body. Is it necessary for the winds to enter 
and dissolve in the central channel during the dream state? I don’t see why that is necessary. As for the dream body, I guess the dream body does not need to remain within the coarse body.   
Question: What is the experience of achieving the impure illusory like? For example, if I were to sit in the same room as the yogi having this realisation, would I be able to see his illusory body? Does it mean when the yogi looks at his coarse body, he sees a deity’s body instead?  
Answer : Remember the three assertions of how the illusory body can arise -  internally, 
externally or both? You have to know these assertions.   What is the basis for attaining the illusory body? The basis for at taining the illusory body is the 
extremely subtle wind and mind of the final mental isolation. The illusory body is something separate from the coarse body so obviously it is not the coarse body that becomes the deity. In the case of a Guhyasamaja practiti oner, when he achieves the illusory body, that illusory body 
looks exactly like Guhyasamaja complete with the faces, hands and so forth. It is not the coarse 
body that becomes the deity but rather it is the (subtle) wind that is the mount for the example 
clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 It is like when we dream, we have a dream body which arises from mere wind and mind and is not the coarse body. It is mentioned in many teachings that it is more difficult to understand the illusory body than (to understand) clear light.  
 Question: When a being is reborn in the lower realms, not from the womb and so forth, is that a manifestation of the subtle winds?  
Answer : Yes, it has to do with the subtle winds.  
 
 
Lesson  43          Page 291 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Question: (inaudible)  
Answer :  It is difficult to say that the illusory body arises as an animal because the illusory body 
is a deity’s body. One is trained to see everything that manifests as the deity. If the illusory 
body appears as an animal, then one cannot say everything appears as the deity.  
 Question : It is said that the illusory body arises concurrently with the (mind) of near- attainment 
of the reverse process. Since the illusory body is the mount of the example clear light, if at the 
time when the illusory body arises and the example clear ligh t is not manifest, how then does 
the illusory body arise externally?  
Answer : The example clear light of the final mental isolation is the basis that allows one to 
actualise the illusory body.  The illusory body arises concurrently with the arising of the mind of 
near- attainment of the reverse process. At that time the example clear light of the final mental 
isolation has already ceased.  
 
The illusory body is not a subtle consciousness and is not a consciousness. It is subtle form. We are not saying that the  mind of near -attainment of the reverse process is the illusory body 
because the illusory body arises concurrently  with the mind of near -attainment of the reverse 
process from the (example clear light of the) final mental isolation.  
 The illusory body is achieved as something separate from the coarse body. The illusory body exists during the mind of near -attainment of the reverse process, it exists during the mind of 
increase and also during the mind of appearance. The impure illusory body is there until o ne 
has achieved the meaning clear light. If one accepts what has been said so far, then what are the difficulties that one will encounter?  
 Student : I can accept the existence part. When it exists, it does not necessarily manifest. For 
example, the most subtle mind exists while the coarse consciousness is manifesting. Likewise, the illusory body exists but it doesn’t necessarily manifest. My qualm is  that the mind of which 
this illusory body is the mount is the example clear light. So although this example clear light exists, but when it is not manifesting, the impure illusory body should not manifest. So I have problems with the first moment of its e xternal manifestation being asserted to be concurrent 
with the mind of near -attainment.   
Response :  When the illusory body is attained, it is not consciousness, it is  subtle form. At the 
time when the illusory body arises, the example clear light, subtle mind, has already ceased. Its 
arising is concurrent with the mind of near- attainment of the reverse process. The mind of 
near- attainment is not clear light. Is there a problem?  
 Nine mixings (or nine blendings)  
A. There are three mixings of the waking st ate. 
• One meditates on the four empties, especially the all empty, using that mind to ascertain emptiness. That is the mixing of the dharmakaya  of the waking state.  
• One arises from that clear light as an illusory body. That is the mixing of the illusory bo dy of 
the waking state.  
• The illusory body then re -enters the heart of the old body in the form of a wisdom being, 
that is the mixing of the nirmanakaya of   e waking state.  
The fully qualified three mixings of the waking state only occur when one achieve s the example 
 
Lesson  43          Page 292 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
clear light of the final mental isolation from which one  one arises as the illusory body which 
then enters the coarse body. This practice of the three mixings of the waking state can also 
occur at the level of verbal and mental isolations.  
 
B. There are three mixings of the sleeping state:  
• Mixing the clear light of sleep with the dharmakaya.  
• Mixing the dream body with the sambhogakaya  
• Mixing of the waking with the nirmanakaya.  
 C. There are three mixings during death.  
• Mixing the clear ligh t of death with the dharmakaya.  
• Mixing the intermediate state with the sambhogakaya  
• Mixing of rebirth with the nirmanakaya.  
 You just need to know what these nine mixings are. In general, we talk about the three mixings:  
 
• the mixing of the  dharmakaya  
• the mixing of the sambhogakaya and  
• the mixing of the nirmanakaya.  
 
When these are expanded, there are the three mixings of the waking state, the three mixings of the sleeping state and the three mixings during death.  
 
In order to be able to do the three mixings during the sleeping state and during the time of 
death, one must be proficient in doing the three mixings during the waking state.  
 
In order to be able to do the three mixings of the three bodies during the completion stage, this must be preceded by the proficient practice of taking the three bodies into the path during the generation stage. This is one of the reasons why the generation stage is the ripener of the completion stage.  
 It is mentioned in the teachings that the merits accumulated from just the mere understanding of the illusory body is similar to (that accumulated from) having consummated the generation stage. The benefits one will experience with just an understanding of the illusory body are inconceivable.

Paths an d grounds of Highest Yoga Tantra.  
 
Generation stage  Path of accumulation  
Physical isolation  Heat level of the path of preparation  
Verbal isolation  Peak level of the path of preparation  
Mental isolation  Forbearance level of the path of preparation  
Impure illusory body  Supreme mundane quality level of the path of 
preparation

FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
When one achieves the impure illusory body on the supreme mundane quality level of the path of preparation, that acts as the substitute for accumulating merit over the three gr eat 
countless eons mentioned in the Paramitayana. Here, “substitute” refers to comparing the same level of realisation. That means when one achieves the impure illusory body at this level of the path of preparation, the merit one accumulates is equal to the merit that one accumulates on the Sutric path over three great countless eons.  
 Question: On the Sutric path, the merit accumulated on the paths of accumulation and preparation only covers one countless great eon?  
Answer : Yes, but this is the tantric pat h of preparation!  
 These are the things you must know:  
1. The illusory body is established from mere wind and mind.  
2. It is something that is separate from the coarse body.  
3. At the time when this impure illusory body achieves the meaning clear light, it dissolves like 
a rainbow dissolving in the sky.  
4. The impure illusory body is purified when one achieves the meaning clear light and you have to understand the reason for this. The main reason that is given in the text is that the example clear light is a co nceptual consciousness and the meaning clear light is a non -
conceptual consciousness. When the meaning clear light is achieved, the example clear light 
dissolves and when this happens, the impure illusory body, which is dependent on that 
example clear ligh t necessarily dissolves as well. It is not sufficient just be able to say this. 
You have to read the text to understand this.  
 Question:  The lifetime that is posited in the text, when we relate it to the four stages of 
existence, when does this lifetime e nd?

LESSON NO: 44   DATE: 3RD SEPTEMBER 2009  
 Bring to mind the three causal objects of refuge – the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha  and on that 
basis, see whether one can develop the thought: “How wonderful it will be if I myself can become the Three Jewels.” It is important to see whether one is able to generate refuge for one’s own purposes and for the purposes of others. We will sp end the next few minutes to 
check on this.  
 Developing that thought for oneself and generating the interest in attaining the Three Jewels through reflection are extremely important. It is not an easy thing to do because in order to develop that intention, one really has to understand what the Three Jewels are, their powers and their qualities. One should have an understanding of this: “If I were to become the Three Jewels, what benefit would I get and what good would it do me ?”  
 On the other hand, one also  needs to understand very clearly the disadvantages of oneself not 
becoming the Three Jewels. This approach towards the Three Jewels applies to everything one works for in this life. The reason one wants something is because one understands its value and benefits and the faults and disadvantages of not having that object  
 When one understands the value of the object, there will be strong interest in getting that 
object and one will put in the time and effort to be able to do so.  
 
One of the reasons we are  still stuck in samsara and not achieving any spectacular results or 
improvements despite having practised the Dharma for a long time is because our practice of refuge is not good. Real refuge is  the interest in wanting to become the Three Jewels ourselve s. 
This is what refuge is.  
 This is something we must think about since all of us here consider ourselves to be Buddhists. We should reflect on whether we have that real refuge in our hearts. This refuge which is the heartfelt desire of wanting to attain the Three Jewels is something we should strive to achieve 
at all times.  
 I have mentioned this before. At the end of the day, one of reasons for these studies, be they Sutra or Tantra, boils down to generating and improving our practice of refuge. If we c ondense 
the subject matter of all the teachings, they are basically explaining what is the Buddha, what is the Dharma and what is the Sangha . The purpose of studying here is to clarify our 
understanding of what the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha  are so that we will  develop the wish 
to achieve them.
