*******************************  
2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.2.1.2  COMPLETION STAGE OF THE ULTIMATE CLEAR LIGHT  
The completion stage (practice) (1) from the proximate causes which are the means for 
manifesting the actual clear light (2) up to (but not including) the obtainment of union (zung 
‘jug)’ is the nature of the clear light of the fourth stage. ( Pg. 52 of the  root text)  
The entity of the meaning clear light is the completion stage starting from the proximate causes which are the means for manifesting the meaning clear light up to but not including the obtainment of union.  
 I think it should be all right to posit the proximate causes to be the (minds of) appearance, increase and near -attainment of the forward process that precede the manifestation of the 
meaning clear light. “Union” here can be posited as the abandonment union. (I have 
mentioned before that there are two types of union: (1) the abandonment union and (2) the realisational union.)   One must also distinguish between meaning clear light  and the stage of meaning clear light . 
The definition presented here is the definition of the clear light of the fourth stage  and is not  
the entity of the meaning clear light.  
 Question:  Are the proximate causes themselves the stage of the meaning clear light?  
 
Lesson  44          Page 296 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Answer:  They are not  the same because the proximate causes are subsumed by the stage of 
the meaning clear light.  
 
Do you have any further questions about this definition? If not, we will continue with the class.  
 Meaning clear light is basically the most subtle mind that re alises emptiness directly. When the 
most subtle mind realises emptiness directly for the first time, at that time, that mind becomes 
meaning clear light.  
 
The minds of appearance, increase and near- attainment precede the manifestation of the 
meaning clear light so they are the stages leading up to the attainment of the meaning clear light. Therefore they are part of the stage of the meaning clear light. Are those minds clear 
lights? No, they are not but they are the minds that precede the meaning clear light and are therefore subsumed by the stage of (meaning) clear light.  
 The clear light of the fourth level is explained to be external manifest purification (phyi mngon byang) through being the unique component (Idog cha) manifesting at the time of dawn 
through transcending the three external states: the moon, sun and evening darkness. It is explained to be internal manifest purification (nang mngon byang) through being the unique component (Idog cha) of all empty clear lig ht through the dissolution of the three internal 
(states): appearance, increase and near attainment . (Pg. 52 of the root text).  
 The meaning clear light is called:  
• the external manifest enlightenment because it manifests at the time of dawn which is 
the time that has transcended the three external states of the  moon, the sun and the darkness.  
• the internal manifest enlightenment because the meaning clear light is achieved 
through the dissolutions of the minds of appearance, increase and near -attainment.  
 
Obtainment of the actual clear light of the fourth level, perceptual comprehension (direct realization) of emptiness through innate great bliss, obtainment of the Highest Yoga Tantra path of seeing and obtainment of the first ground of that (path) are sim ultaneous. (Pg. 52 of 
the root text ).  
 Question: What does ‘transcend the three external states of the moon, the sun and the 
darkness’ mean?  
Answer: This means that when the meaning clear light manifests for the first time, it does not 
do so when the sun or moon are shining brightly or at night, when it is dark. I guess there is some external reason for this since the mind tends to be more alert and fresh early in the morning. Guru Shakyamuni Buddha manifested the clear light at dawn. This is something for  us 
to think about: Is it necessary for the meaning clear light to be actualised for the first time at dawn? If so, what is the reason?  
 If clear light is classified by mode of expression (sgras brjod) there are two:  
1. Object clear light (yul ‘od gsal)  
2. Subject clear light (yul can ‘od gsal).  
 
Lesson  44          Page 297 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
1. The first is equivalent to emptiness.  
2. Again, regarding subject clear light, gross mental awareness {yid shes rags pa) 
comprehending emptiness is for example the common general meaning of clear light of the 
perfection vehicle and the three lower tantras. Melted bliss (zhu bde) comprehending emptiness is for example the common genera meaning of clear light of the generation  stage. 
Metaphoric clear light of mental isolation comprehendingemptiness is clear light of the hidden meaning (sbas don) because appearance is a hidden state (sbaspa) of mind, and that is 
metaphoric clear light.  
The metaphoric clear light subsuming the s tages of physical isolation, verbal isolation and the 
illusory body is also clear light of the hidden meaning because I think stating hidden meaning to be three is the main (presentation). Also the Commentary of the Compendium of All Vajra Primordial Aware ness states that vajra recitation and physical isolation are subsumed within 
the hidden. (pg. 53 of the root text) 
The real clear light refers to the object clear light (also translated as objective clear light) which 
is emptiness.  
 There are different ways of explaining the subjective clear light:  
• There is the clear light that is common or shared with the Paramitayana, the three lower 
classes of Tantra and Highest Yoga Tantra which is the coarse mind that realises emptiness.  
• There is also a way of positing the clear light that is shared by the generation and 
completion stages of Highest Yoga Tantra which is literally referred to as melted bliss. The 
bliss here refers to the bliss that arises from the melting of the constituents and it is that 
blissful mind that realises emptiness.  
• The uncommon explanation of the clear light according to Highest Yoga Tantra is the 
example clear light and the meaning clear light.  It says here that the example clear light 
occurs not just on the stage of mental isolation but also during verbal and physical 
isolations.  
 I believe I mentioned before that the example clear light occurs only  on the stage of mental 
isolation. That is a mistake. The example clear light  occurs also on the stages of verbal an d 
physical isolations, i.e., there is the example clear light of physical isolation, there is the example clear light of verbal isolation and there is the example clear light of mental isolation. All these are example clear lights.  
Actual clear light is f inal actual clear light because the final meaning is not taken to be the final 
progression on the path, but it is taken to be the final comprehension of thatness by innate bliss. ( Pg. 53 of the root text).  
 The reason the meaning clear light of the fourth  stage is called the final meaning clear light  is 
because it is the final realisation of the emptiness, having realised emptiness in its entirety, i.e., there is nothing more about emptiness to be realised.  But the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is not the final meaning clear light because it has reached the end of the path as there is 
 
Lesson  44          Page 298 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
still some way to go.  
 
This meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the final uninterrupted path which is the direct antidote to the obstructions to liberation (afflictive obstructions) in accordance with Highest Yoga Tantra.  In order to achieve enlightenment, one must enter the Tantric path, in particular, the path of Highest Yoga Tantra.   
 
How about those bodhisattvas who have progressed to the tenth ground o n the Paramitayana 
path or who have done so in dependence on the path of the three lower classes of Tantra? At 
what level do they enter the path of Highest Yoga Tantra?  
 According to the Paramitayana (Sutric) path, there are two types of bodhisattvas on th e tenth 
ground:  
1. Bodhisattvas who will achieve enlightenment in that lifetime.  
2. Bodhisattvas who will achieve enlightenment in one more lifetime.  
 The bodhisattva on the tenth ground who enters the path of Highest Yoga Tantra at a certain level,  is that bodhisattva who will achieve enlightenment in that very lifetime, i.e., he is in his 
final existence as a sentient being.  
 What about the bodhisattvas who are at the first to the ninth grounds of the Paramitayana 
path? Do these bodhisattvas enter the Tantric path at any  point? Most scholars assert that 
bodhisattvas who have achieved the arya path do not enter the Tantric path. Once the 
bodhisattva is on the Paramitayana path, he remains (on the Sutric path) from the first to the 
ninth ground. It i s only when such a bodhisattva is in his final existence as a sentient being on 
the tenth ground that he will enter the Tantric path.  
 Question: At which point does this tenth ground bodhisattva who is in his final existence as a sentient being enter the Tantric path?  
Answer : One can posit that to be at the uninterrupted path at the end of the continuum (of a 
sentient being).   
 While in meditative equipoise, that bodhisattva in his final existence (as a sentient being) will be roused by the buddhas  who wi ll say to him: “You will not achieve enlightenment by  
remaining in meditative equipoise. In order to achieve enlightenment, you have to manifest 
the meaning clear light.”   
 At midnight, the buddhas  will confer the wisdom initiation upon the bodhisattva and through 
that, the bodhisattva generates the minds of  appearance, increase and near- attainment but he 
does not manifest the meaning clear light yet.  
 During that time before the bodhisattva manifests meaning clear light , the buddhas will 
instruct him on clear light and unification (union). The  buddhas then bestow on the bodhisattva 
the fourth initiation, the word initiation. In the early hours of 3 – 4am, that bodhisattva 
manifests the meaning cle ar light and arises with the (pure illusory) body of the learner’s 
union. At the third part of that time of dawn, at first light, the bodhisattva achieves the 
 
Lesson  44          Page 299 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
unification of no -more learning.  
 
There are different explanations on how bodhisattvas who have progressed to the tenth ground by depending on the Paramitayana path or the path of three lower classes of Tantra enter the path of Highest Yoga Tantra and achieve enlightenment. Let us use the above explanation as the basis for further discussion.   
Quest ion: If a practitioner propounds the view of emptiness of the lower tenet systems, how 
can he, in the next moment, directly realise emptiness in accordance with the view of the 
Consequence Middle Way school? It seems that the result does not accord with its cause.  
Answer : One can re -phrase the question by asking whether the example clear lights of the 
three isolations realise emptiness?  Are there example clear lights of the three isolations that do not realise emptiness?  
 The emptiness asserted here is that propounded by the Consequence Middle School and not that of the lower tenet systems. The example clear lights of verbal and mental isolations do realise emptiness. However, there is some debate as to whether one realises emptiness during physical isolat ion, i.e., at the beginning of the Highest Yoga Tantric path of preparation, 
through the union of calm abiding and special insight. Some say that there is no realisation of emptiness by the example clear light of physical isolation.  
 
But the discussion do esn’t end there. One may wonder are there proponents of the Mind- Only 
School who have attained verbal isolation? Are there proponents of the Autonomy Middle Way 
School who have attained verbal isolation? Basically it does not mean that if one is a physical or 
verbal isolationist, one necessarily achieves the realisation of emptiness according to the Consequence Middle Way School.  
 For our purposes, let us say that there are realisations of emptiness in verbal and mental isolations but whether this happens at physical isolation is debatable.  
 Question: If one has to achieve enlightenment on the basis of having received empowerments, 
how did the first Buddha achieve enlightenment?  
Answer : Is there a first Buddha in the first place? Our most subtle wind and mi nd do not have a 
beginning nor will they come to an end.  
 
Student : The statement in the text that the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the 
realisation of emptiness in its entirety and relating this to the earlier question, it sounds like it 
doesn ’t matter if you realise the emptiness of the lower schools before the level of meaning 
clear light as long as that direct realisation of emptiness is based on the emptiness posited by the Consequence Middle Way School!  
 Response : It boils down to whether the example clear light of the final mental isolation realises 
emptiness or not? Does it necessarily have to realise emptiness? Emptiness here is that asserted by the Consequence Middle School. The example clear light of the final mental isolation must realise emptiness because in order to cut the root of samsara, one must realise emptiness. So it is very clear that the example clear light of the final mental isolation definitely 
 
Lesson  44          Page 300 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
realises emptiness.  
 
Question: Is the meaning clear light a non -conceptual co nsciousness ? 
Answer: Yes.  
 Question:  The bodhisattva on the first to the ninth grounds of the Paramitayana path has to 
enter the Tantric path on the tenth ground. When he is on the tenth ground, the bodhisattva 
manifests the meaning clear light which is the direct realisation of emptiness. H ow is that direct 
realisation of emptiness different from the direct realisation of emptiness that he has already 
achieved on the first ground? Are those two emptinesses the same?  
Answer:  I guess one of the reasons you raised this question is because you didn’t understand 
the text when it referred to the common general meaning of clear light.   
 There is a way of explaining clear light that is shared with the Paramitayana, the three lower classes of Tantra and Highest Yoga Tantra. This is the coarse wisdom t hat realises emptiness. 
The bodhisattva from the first to the tenth ground of the Paramitayana path do realise emptiness.  There is no difference in the object, emptiness, that is realised but there is a difference in the mind that realises emptiness.  
 The bodhisattva on the tenth ground of the Paramitayana path realises emptiness with a 
coarse mind. It is insufficient to have coarse wisdom realising emptiness. One must realise 
emptiness directly with the extremely subtle mind. That is the reason why the tenth ground 
bodhisattva who depends on the Paramitayana path or the path of the three lower classes of Tantra have to manifest the meaning clear light.  
    Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme

LESSON NO: 45   DATE: 8TH SEPTEMBER 2009  
 
You can spend the next five minutes in silence meditating on a subject of your choice. During 
that time, whether you are doing analytical or placement meditation, the main thing is that the 
mind should be very alert. If the mind becomes alert due to that m editation, then you have 
achieved the purpose of meditating. Otherwise when the mind is too relaxed, laxity  sinks in.  
 
Sometimes we think of meditation as a form of relaxation. That is not real meditation. You 
don’t get anything out of it. Why do we medi tate? The whole point is to benefit the mind, to 
transform it for the better. For meditation to be a positive experience, the mind has to be very 
alert as change comes from that alert state of mind. Otherwise if the mind is not alert, how is it 
possible to  get any kind of positive experience?  
 
To a certain extent, it can’t be helped because it is the end of the day and you come from work so you are a bit tired. Because you are tired, when we meditate, those five minutes actually 
become a rest period and th e mind becomes very dull and relaxed.  
 
It is really important to put aside time to do meditation every day, even for    five or ten minutes. You really have to train to do this. When we talk about  meditating, it doesn’t mean you must sit in the meditation  posture and not move. If the objective is to develop calm 
abiding or when you are doing analytical meditation, your physical posture helps a lot and you shouldn’t be moving about. But it doesn’t mean that analytical meditation must only be done by sitting  still. You can also do analytical meditation by moving around or even when you are 
lying down because analytical meditation is basically just thinking about, reflecting and analysing things.  
 The main thing is you have to know the importance of keeping t he mind  alert during 
meditation, be it analytical or placement meditation as positive change and transformation can only come from within that state of alertness. That is not going to happen overnight and one should develop the habit of  meditating every day for five or ten minutes.  
 
*************************  
Impromptu Quiz  
1) What is the meaning of non- dual (inseparable) bliss and emptiness?  
2) What does the indivisibility of the two truths mean?  
3) Is the impure illusory body the substantial cause of the Buddha’s form body?  
4) Is the meaning clear light of the fourth stage necessarily an uninterrupted path that is a 
direct antidote to the afflictive obstructions (obstructions to liberation)?  
 What is the meaning of non- dual (inseparable) bliss and emptiness?  
(Geshe -la asks the students for their answers).  
 This phrase “inseparable bliss and emptiness” is commonly used in Tantric literature so it is important to know its meaning. In this context, what does bliss mean and what does emptiness refer to? The term basically means the bliss that realises emptiness.  
 
Lesson  45          Page 302 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
As I have mentioned before, that bliss realising emptiness is of two types: (1) a conceptual consciousness  and (2) a direct perceiver. There is a conceptual bliss that realises emptiness and there is th e bliss that realises emptiness directly.  
 The bliss that realises emptiness conceptually obviously cannot be inseparable (with emptiness) because that realisation is not of one taste with emptiness. The real inseparability of bliss and 
emptiness only come s when that bliss realises emptiness directly.  
 
When the bliss that realises emptiness does so directly, that experience is like pouring water into water. The bliss and emptiness become one taste, such that to the mind these two are inseparable, appearing  as one entity. When the mind is in that state, that blissful mind realises 
emptiness directly. That is when the real inseparability of bliss and emptiness occurs.  
 Question: When you say, “bliss realising emptiness,” “bliss” seems to refer to the mind re alising 
emptiness whereas in English, this word “bliss” means great happiness. Can you please clarify what is the meaning of “bliss”? The term, “inseparable bliss and emptiness” suggests to me that the subject is the mind, the object is emptiness and these  two are inseparable. Are the 
winds the substantial cause of this bliss?  
 Answer : Through the method of striking the vital points of the vajra body, one causes the white 
bodhicitta to melt. When that happens, a special form of tactile contact arises and ba sed on 
that, the body consciousness experiences this physical feeling of bliss.  Due to that, the mental consciousness is also transformed into the entity of bliss and it is this mental consciousness 
that is used to realise emptiness.   
 What does the indiv isibility of the two truths mean?  
(Geshe -la asks students for their answers).  
 In this context of Highest Yoga Tantra, the two truths are:  
- the conventional truth which is the illusory body and  
- the ultimate truth which is the meaning clear light.  
 In the context of the learner’s union , the union of the two truths means the union of meaning 
clear light and the illusory body.  
 If it is a union, is it necessarily the union of the two truths?  
 The meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the uninterrupted pa th which is the direct 
antidote to the afflictive obstructions. In the second moment, hasn’t one abandoned the afflictive obstructions already? At that time, isn’t the learner’s union attained when the afflictive obstructions are abandoned and one becomes an arhat (foe destroyer)? In other words, when one initially becomes an arhat, has one achieved union?  
 Response to student’s answer: If you say you have achieved union upon the abandonment of the afflictions, is that the union of the two truths?  
 
Lesson  45          Page 303 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
There are two types of learner’s union:  
1. the abandonment union and  
2. the realisational union.  
 In term of the sequence of achieving these unions, one first achieves  the abandonment union followed by the realisational union.  
 
The union of the two truths refers to  the union of the illusory body and the meaning clear light, 
specifically, it is the union of the pure illusory body and the meaning clear light (which does not refer to the meaning clear light of the fourth stage) . 
 I guess it is all right to posit the re alisational union and the union of the two truths to be the 
same thing. The “meaning clear light” of the union of the pure illusory body and the meaning clear light refers to the uninterrupted path that is the direct antidote to the knowledge obstructions.  That meaning clear light in unification with the pure illusory body is the union of 
the two truths.  
 If that is the meaning of the union of the two truths, then going back to the original question: If it is a union, is it necessarily the union of the two truths?  
 
With respect to the union of the two truths, in the first place you have to know what the two 
truths are:  
- the conventional truth refers to the illusory body and  
- the ultimate truth refers to the meaning clear light.  
The union of these two truths means the unification of the illusory body and the meaning clear light.  That is the minimum you must know but it is not enough just to be able to say what the two truths are and what union means. You have to think about what it really means. The more you think about it, the clearer it becomes. We are not just looking at the words but we have to reach an ascertainment that it is based on reasoning so that we can explain when we are asked this question.   Is the impure illusory body the substantial cause of the Buddha’s form body?  
For those of you who say no, then if a fully qualified deity’s body cannot be the substantial cause of the Buddha’s form body, what can act as the substantial cause?  
 We had been searching for the real  cause of the Buddha’s form body in Sutra and Tantra. In 
both Sutra and the three lower classes of Tantra there are no explanations on how to actualise the Buddha’s form body. Such an explanation appears only in Highest Yoga Tantra.  
 When one achieves mea ning clear light, the example clear light, which is a conceptual 
consciousness, dissolves along with the impure illusory body, the basis of which is that example clear light. If that impure illusory body dissolves into the meaning clear right, how can it g o on 
to achieve enlightenment? When one achieves the meaning clear light, one’s extremely subtle wind and mind cease.   
 
 
Lesson  45          Page 304 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
What does it mean when we say the impure illusory body is purified? When one purifies one’s 
negative karma, does one transform it into virtue? When the impure illusory body is purified, it ceases (disappears).  
 Geshe -la : Why does it need to disappear?  
 We have to think about this because in all the texts it says the impure illusory body is purified 
when one achieves the meaning clear light of the fourth stage. What does purified mean?  
Does it mean going out of existence?  
 One of the reasons why the impure illusory body is purified when one achieves the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is because the basis from which it came is co ntaminated. Since 
the impure illusory body is based  
on something that is contaminated, therefore it has to be purified. That being the case, I guess the impure illusory body can be called the contaminated body.  
 Since it is a contaminated body, therefore  it has to be purified. It has to be removed and cease. 
In that case, the impure illusory body cannot progress to enlightenment.  
 Does our coarse body go on to enlightenment?  
 
Geshe -la: We also need to think about this: This physical body goes to enlighte nment or not? 
(The answer is) not easy.

 The purpose of meditation is to make the mind alert and within that alert state of mind, one attempts to transform the mind into a virtuous st ate. When the mind is alert, the imprints that 
are placed on the mind will be stronger. So whatever meditation one is going to do, be it 
analytical or placement meditation, it should be done with an alert mind.  
 It is recommended for beginners like us to attempt to visualise the Buddha clearly. There are inconceivable benefits for doing this. We are accumulating the merit to meet the Buddha in the future.  
 So, for the next five minutes, with an alert state of mind, try to transform the mind into a virtuou s state. The teachings always mention that simply reciting the name of the buddhas 
alone can purify eons of negative karma. If that is the case, it goes without saying that remembering the Buddha and visualising him will bring great benefit as well.  
 
****************************  
 The last question of the four that I brought up in the last class was: If it is the meaning clear 
light of the fourth stage, is it necessarily an uninterrupted path that is the direct antidote to the 
obstructions to liberation? I guess the answer is yes.   The Sun of Siddhis (of the) Actual Completion Stage (rdzogs rim dngos dngos grub snyi ma)  
states:  
 
The (stage) prior to being able to directly generate the illusory body through properly loosening (grol) the channel knots is subsumed in mental isolation. The (stage) from 
being able to directly generate the illusory body up to purifying that is subsumed in the stage of self blessing. Then, manifesting the meaning of thatness (de kho na nyid) and the proxima te causes that are the means (for attaining) that are called ‘the stage of 
clear light’ and ‘the stage of actual enlightenment’. (Pg. 52 of the root text)  
 (Interpreter ’s comment: “The Sun of Siddhis ” should read as “The Cluster of Attainments” ) 
 The term “ self blessing ” refers to the illusory body. In the Guhyasamaja Tantra, the illusory 
body is called the illusory body. In the Heruka Chakrasamvara Tantra, the term “self blessing” is used to refer to the illusory body. In the Vajrabhairava (Yamantaka) Tant ra, the term “yoga of 
shape” is used.  
   The basis of the designation of the label “I” or person is the extremely subtle wind and mind. When this extremely subtle wind and mind is transformed into the deity’s body, due to that transformation (or blessing),  it is called self -blessing. This is because we have transformed the 
 
Lesson  46          Page 306 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
basis of designation of the person or the ‘I’ into the deity’s body and in that sense, the self is 
blessed.  
 Question: At the end of the quotation, what is “ the stage of clear light”  and “the stage of actual 
enlightenment” ? 
Answer : They refer to the same thing.  
 
(Interpreter ’s comment: In Tibetan, the same term mngon byang is used. “Actual 
enlightenment” and “manifest purification” means the same thing and normally this is 
translated as “manifest enlightenment,” so it should read as “the stage of manifest enlightenment .”) 
 As mentioned before, the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is called the external manifest 
enlightenment and the internal manifest enlightenment.  
 Someone says: It follows that the explanation that the proximate causes for manifesting actual clear ligh t are the fourth level, is unacceptable because the third stage exists in the continuum 
of one about to obtain actual clear light. That is because the impure illusory body in their continuum is the third stage, because that is the impure illusory body.  
 Ngawang Palden: That is either not pervasive or otherwise it should be examined. ( Pg. 55 of 
the root text)  
 The qualm is that the three minds of white appearance, red increase and black near -
attainment precede the manifestation of the meaning clear light and  are therefore, not the 
fourth stage of clear light. Instead, they should be the stage of the illusory body. Is this the position of this text? Are these three minds part of the third or fourth stage? And is this the fourth stage or  
the stage of the fourth stage?  
 The fourth stage  necessarily  refers to the meaning clear light but there is a need to distinguish 
between the fourth stage and the stage of the fourth stage because of the definitions of the clear light of the fourth stage and the stage of the ill usory body.  
 The completion stage (practice) (1) from establishing the actual body of the deity adorned with 
signs and marks through the example clear light of the final mental isolation acting as the 
cooperative condition and the fundamental winds that s erve as its mount acting as the 
substantial cause, concurrent with establishing near attainment of the reverse order through the winds slightly moving from the example clear light of the final mental isolation, (2) up to but not including generating the pr oximate causes which are the means of achieving the 
meaning clear light, is the nature of the stage of the illusory body. ( Pg. 46 of the root text ) 
 The completion stage practice (1) from the proximate causes which are the means for manifesting the actual clear light (2) up to (but not including) the obtainment of union (zung 
jug) is the nature of the clear light of the fourth stage. ( Pg. 52 of the root text ).  
 
 
Lesson  46          Page 307 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
 By looking at these two definitions, we can see the difference between the meaning clear light  
of the fourth stage and the stage of the fourth stage. We can conclude that the minds of white appearance, red increase and black near -attainment that precede the manifestation of the 
meaning clear light are stages of the fourth stage and not  stages of t he third stage.  
 
You have to understand how we arrived at this conclusion by comparing the two definitions 
which define not the illusory body and meaning clear light but are definitions of the stage of the illusory body and the stage of the  meaning clear light.  
 The proximate causes which are the means for manifesting the meaning clear light are basically these three minds of white appearance, red increase and black near -attainment that 
immediately precede the manifestation of the meaning clear light. During the time of the proximate causes, does the impure illusory body exist? Yes, it does.  
 2.1.3.1.2.1.2.2.2.2  THE COMPLETION STAGE OF THE INDIVISIBLE T WO TRUTHS  
 The completion stage (practice) (1) from when one arises in the pure illusory body simultaneously with the slight movement in the wind from the actual clear light of the fourth stage dependent on the wind which is the mount of that clear light acting as it s substantial 
cause and the mind of clear light acting as its co -active condition (2) up to the actual clear light 
at the limit of the learner (ground) is the nature of learner’s union or the completion stage of the indivisible two truths.  
 Arising in nea r attainment of the reverse order from the actual clear light of the fourth stage, 
obtaining the pure illusory body, obtaining the abandoning union, abandoning afflictive obscurations (nyon sgrib), transferring to the second ground and obtaining the path o f 
meditation are concurrent. This is (asserted) from the perspective of those of definite Highest (Yoga) mantra lineage. One must assert that the clear light etc., occurring at the end of the other paths is primordial awareness of the tenth ground and I wo nder whether learner’s union 
is indivisible from the ninth ground of meditation.   
 Innate afflictions are abandoned by the clear light of the fourth stage and the Illuminating Lamp states that they are abandoned at the time of first obtaining learner’s uni on. ( Pg. 55 of 
the root text)  
 
• The illusory body exists from the time it comes into existence up to and before the 
(manifestation of the) meaning clear light of the fourth stage which acts as the direct antidote to both the intellectually acquired and innate obstructions to liberation ( afflictive 
obstructions).  
• When there is a slight movement in the wind from the meaning clear light of the fourth 
stage, that meaning clear light of the fourth stage acts as the cooperative condition and the 
wind that it is mounted on acts as the substanti al cause for one to arise as the pure illusory 
body.  
 
Lesson  46          Page 308 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• From the time one arises as the pure illusory body up to the meaning clear light at the limit 
of the learner’s ground is “ the nature of learner’s union or the completion stage of the 
indivisible two trut hs.” 
 Now we will discuss some qualms regarding the above definition of the learner’s union which defines the entity of the learner’s union or the completion stage of the indivisible two truths.  
• Is the first moment of achieving the pure illusory body a learner’s union?  
  
• The definition is posited to be the entity of the learner’s union and the completion stage of 
the indivisible two truths but are these two the same thing?  
 
The definition is saying that the learner’s union and the completion stage of the indivisible two truths are the same thing since both share the same entity. If that is the case, let us refer to our discussion in the previous lesson on the meaning of inseparable bliss and emptiness and the indivisible two truths. The two truths are: ( 1) the conventional truth which is the illusory body 
and (2) the ultimate truth which is the meaning clear light. The indivisible two truths mean the indivisibility of these two. Is the entity of that the same as the entity of the learner’s union?  
 We have  to ascertain these two things:  
• The meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the direct antidote to the obstructions to 
liberation (afflictive obstructions).  
• Immediately after the afflictive obstructions are abandoned, the mind of near- attainment of 
the reverse order arises. Concurrent with that, one arises as the pure illusory body.  
 The substantial cause of this pure illusory body is the extremely subtle wind which is the mount of the meaning clear light of the fourth stage. At the same time when one ac hieve the pure 
illusory body, it is said one achieves the learner’s union. This is clearly stated in the text: “Arising in near attainment of the reverse order from the actual clear light of the fourth stage, 
obtaining the pure illusory body, obtaining the  abandoning union, abandoning afflictive 
obscurations (nyon sgrib), transferring to the second ground and obtaining the path of 
meditation are concurrent.”  
 Union means bringing two things together so when the pure illusory body is achieved, at that moment  the learner’s union is achieved. What is unified here? In order to understand this, you 
need to know that there are two kinds of learner’s union: (1) the abandonment union and (2) the realisational union. Of these two, it is the abandonment union that is achieved concurrently with the attainment of the pure illusory body.  
• At the time when that person achieves the pure illusory body, has that person achieved 
the realisational union? No.  
• When the person has achieved the pure illusory body, has that person  achieved the 
meaning clear light? Yes.  
 One achieves the learner’s union when one first attains the pure illusory body. One has to know that at that time when one achieves the pure illusory body, one also attains the 
 
Lesson  46          Page 309 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
abandonment union. The abandonment union is the union of: (1) the pure illusory body and (2) 
the state of abandonment of afflictive obstructions.  
 According to the definition, at the time of achieving the abandonment union, one also achieves the completion stage of the indivisible two truths. If that is the case, one has to say that the two truths here are: (1) the conventional truth which is the pure illusory body and (2) the ultimate truth which is the state of abandonment of the afflictive obstructions.  
 According to the Consequence Middle Way School, the state of abandonment of the afflictive obstructions is the ultimate truth. The Autonomy Middle School says the state of abandonment of afflictive obstructions is a conventional truth.  
 The learner’s union can also be be divided into two: (1) the principal and (2) the ordinary.   Although it is not stated clearly in the text, by following the same line of reasoning, I guess it should be all right to look at the completion stage of the indivisible two truths in the same way, i.e., there is a main (completion stage of the indivisible two truths) and (2) an ordinary (completion stage of the indivisible two truths).   You need to know:  
1. What a union is?  
2. When is that union achieved ? 
3. There are two kinds of unions: (1) the abandonment union and (2) the realisational 
union.  
 Question: Is a union necessarily the union of the two truths?  
Answer : I don’t see any problem. If it is the completion stage of the indivisible two truths, it is 
not necessarily the main one which means it is not necessarily the realisational union, i.e., it can also be the abandonment union.  
 In general, when we talk about the indivisible two truths, we are refering to the pure illusory 
body and the pure mind of meaning clear light.  
 Question: Why is the state of abandonment of the afflictive obstructions an ultimate truth according to the Consequence Middle Way School?  
Answer : When the mind which is accompanied by the afflictions is  separated from the 
afflictions, that factor of having abandoned the afflictions is not something separate from the mind. It is the nature of the mind. So when the afflictions are separated f orm the mind, then 
we say the nature of the mind is revealed .  
 That factor of having abandoned the afflictions is true cessation which is a permanent phenomenon and is the emptiness of the mind.  
 Question: Why does the Autonomy Middle Way School posit that to be a conventional truth?  
 
 
Lesson  46          Page 310 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Answer:  According to the Autonomy Middle Way School, they posit that factor of having 
abandoned the afflictions which is true cessation to be a conventionality and not emptiness.  
 
 Question: When Shakyamuni Buddha entered the path of Highest Yoga Tantra at the meaning clear light of the fourth stage which includes the minds of white appearance, red increase and near- attainment that precede it, did he have an illusory body?  
Answer : The Buddha at that time does not have an ill usory body, be it impure or pure. 
According to the Paramitayana, the bodhisattva on the 10
th ground who is about to achieve the 
meaning clear light of the fourth stage does not have an illusory body but has a mental body adorned with all the signs and exem plifications. As stated earlier, that is an exception. That 
bodhisattva at that time has yet to achieve the pure illusory body.  
 Question: Can we say that the consciousness of a sentient being is not necessarily an impermanent phenomenon?  
Answer : If it is a consciousness, it is necessarily impermanent.  
 Question: But isn’t true cessation a mind and that is permanent?  
Answer : No, it is the emptiness of the mind. Is there a mind that is permanent?  If the mind is 
permanent, then there is no hope because there is no way to achieve enlightenment since the mind cannot be transformed. So the mind is definitely impermanent.

LESSON NO: 47   DATE: 15TH SEPTEMBER 2009  
 
Try to bring to the mind how:  
• the objects of refuge -  the Buddha, Dharma and Sangha - are mere designations, merely 
labelled by the mind;   
• the person who goes for refuge is merely designated through the force of conceptuality;  
• the merits and virtues accumulated are merely designated through the force of 
conceptuality; and  
• the Three Jewels we will attain by depending on that merit are also merely designated 
through the force of conceptuality.  
 
How wonderful it will be when we attain the state of the Three Jewels! We must definitely d o 
so. In relation to Tantra, the practice of refuge is related to the cultivation of pure appearance. 
Whatever appears is empty and that emptiness is bliss. When taking refuge, see whether you 
are able to bring to the mind how everything is a display of em ptiness and how that emptiness 
is great bliss.  
 
***************  
 There are two types of union:  
(1) the ordinary union which refers to  the abandonment union of the pure illusory body and the state of abandonment of the afflictive obstructions; and  
(2) the principal union which refers to the union of the two truths,  meaning clear light and pure illusory body.  
 Between the abandonment and realisational unions, the abandonment union is achieved first. The realisational union is the union of the holy body which is the pure illusory body and the holy mind which is the meaning clear light. It is a union of a pure body (the pure illusory body) and a pure mind, a mind which is purified of the afflictive obstructions.  
 
The meaning clear light of the fourth stage acts as the direct antidote to the afflictive 
obstructions. Simultaneously with the abandonment of the afflictive obstructions one achieves 
the pure illusory body. The meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the uninterrupted path. 
Subsequent to t hat, one arises with the mind of near -attainment of the reverse order.  
 
With regards to that mind of near- attainment of the reverse process, there is a discussion as to 
whether that mind  is a wisdom of  meditative equipoise, i.e., basically whether it is the path of 
thorough release (translated as “ liberated path” in the text)?  
 Different masters have different views. From the Sutric point of view, the uninterrupted path and the path of thorough release occur in a single session of meditative equipoise. The uninterrupted path is the antidote to the afflictions (which are being abandoned) and the path of thorough release is the time when those afflictions are abandoned. Both are wisdoms of meditative equipoise as both occur in one session of meditative equipoise.  
 
 
Lesson  47          Page 312 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
In the context of Highest Yoga Tantra, the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the 
uninterrupted path which is the direct antidote to the afflictive obstructions. When the afflictive obstructions are abandoned, that mind is already the mind of black near- attainment 
of the reverse order. When one arises from the meaning clear light of the fourth stage, i.e. when one arises into the mind of near -attainment of the reverse order, although there are 
different positions as to what that mind of near -attainment is, I guess it would be all right to 
assert that it is a path of thorough release.  
 If we take that position, asserting the mind of near- attainment of the reverse order to be the 
path of thorough release, then we have to consider whether that path of thorough release is a 
mind that realises emptiness. This is because the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is a mind that directly realises emptiness.   If that mind of near -attainment of the reverse order is a path of thorough release, does i t have 
to realise emptiness directly? The answer is no, it is not a direct perception of emptiness:  
• Why? Because it is a conceptual consciousness.  
• Why is it a conceptual consciousness? Because the minds of appearance, increase and near -
attainment are neces sarily conceptual consciousnesses. Therefore it follows that the mind 
of near -attainment of the reverse order that arises after the meaning clear light of the 
fourth stage is a conceptual consciousness and therefore it does not realise emptiness directly.  
 There is a difference in the subtlety of these two minds: the mind of near -attainment of the 
reverse order and the meaning clear light of the fourth stage. The latter is an extremely subtle 
mind while the mind of near- attainment of the reverse order is c oarser. The consciousness that 
precedes the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is a subtle mind. The consciousness that 
precedes the mind of near -attainment is likewise a coarser mind.  
 
Can the extremely subtle mind be transformed into the entity of the mind of near -attainment? 
No, that is not possible. That being the case,  
(likewise) the mind of near -attainment cannot become the extremely subtle mind.  
 The extremely subtle mind (or the fundamental mind) is never changing, i.e., it will always remain in the entity of an extremely subtle mind and it will not become coarser. That is why it is called “forever indestructible.”  The extremely subtle wind upon which the extremely subtle mind is mounted is also forever indestructible and always remains extremely subtle.  
 There are various views with regards to this point. It is not that we cannot hold different views. I am not saying that this is the only view and the correct view but we take this position as a starting point and work from here.  
 There is also much debate about the nature of that mind of near - attainment. It is a path of 
thorough release but is it the wisdom of meditative equipoise? There are man y masters who 
say that it is not the wisdom of meditative equipoise but it is the wisdom of subsequent attainment.  
 
 
Lesson  47          Page 313 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
There is a particular view of some masters that for it to be a wisdom of meditative equipoise in 
this case, it has to be an extremely subtl e mind. When one arises from that meditative 
equipoise of the extremely subtle mind, whatever one arises into have to be the wisdom of subsequent attainment. Do you have any qualm regarding this?  
 Question: The qualm is that when one arises from the meaning clear light, one achieves the pure illusory body concurrently with the mind of near- attainment. The pure illusory body is the 
extremely subtle wind which is one entity with the extremely subtle mind (which is the meaning clear light). So when the pure illusory body is achieved, at that time, it seems that the meaning clear light should be manifest because they are one entity. If that is the case, are there two minds manifesting at the same time?  
Answer:  In the first place, the pure illusory body is not  a consciousness. It is form. Its causes are 
both the extremely subtle wind and mind but it is not consciousness. At the time of achieving the pure illusory body, one achieves the abandonment union.  
 At the very first moment when one achieves the pure illuso ry body, does the meaning clear 
light manifest? What will be the problem if the meaning clear light manifests?  
 At the initial moment when one achieves the pure illusory body, the meaning clear light is not manifest. This is because concurrent with that in itial moment of achieving the pure illusory 
body, one achieves the abandonment union. If the meaning clear light manifested at that time, 
that would not be an abandonment union but a realisational union which would mean that one 
achieves the abandonment union and the realisation union together!   
At the initial moment when one achieves the pure illusory body, the extremely subtle mind is not manifesting. (What manifests) is the mind of near -attainment. If we say the extremely 
subtle mind manifests (at that time), then that extremely subtle mind will have to be the meaning clear light.  
 Question: What is the nature of the pure illusory body?  
Answer:  It is like the subtle body of the intermediate state being and  similar to the dream 
body. It is a subtle body that is separate from the coarse body. We have discussed this earlier in the illusory body of the third stage. When the impure illusory body arises from the extremely subtle wind and mind, it is said that it is something separate from our coarse body.  
 
Question : At the first moment of achieving the pure illusory body, it is concurrent with the 
mind of near -attainment. At that time does the practitioner have the extremely subtle mind – 
the meaning clear light?  
Answer : Yes, the practitioner has the extremel y subtle mind but it is not manifesting. At the 
first moment of achieving the pure illusory body, the meaning clear light is not manifesting 
because (the arising of the pure illusory body) is concurrent with the mind of near -attainment. 
That time coincides  with the abandonment union which is the union of the pure illusory body 
and the state of abandonment of the afflictive obstructions. At that time the meaning clear 
light has yet to be achieved.  The meaning clear light does exist but it does not manifest.  
 
 
Lesson  47          Page 314 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Response from student:  Yes, the pure illusory body is manifesting but my qualm is that they 
(i.e., meaning clear light and pure illusory body) are one entity with two isolates…..  
Answer : At that time, one would not say that the pure illusory body is one entity because for it 
to be of one entity, there must be at least two things. Here, only one is manifest and the other 
one is not manifest. So at that time, we cannot say it is a single entity.  
 Question: Can we say that that pure illusory body is in the same entity of the state of 
abandonment?  
Answer:  I think you are permitted to say that.  
 
Question: So where does the extremely subtle mind abide when it is not manifesting?  
Answer : It abides quietly!  
 Let us not talk about illusory body. Let’s talk about ourselves. Do we have the extremely subtle wind and mind?  
 Question: What then is the consciousness isolate of the impure illusory body?   Answer : Why must the impure illusory body be of one entity with something?  
 Student : Because I am trying to account for the substantial causes of each stage that produce 
the preceding consciousness and form. In this case, the substantial cause of the impure illus ory 
body of the third stage is the subtle wind of the example clear light of the final mental isolation. But the mind which is the same entity with the impure illusory body has not been 
accounted for yet ………  
Response : When one arises as an illusory body b e it impure or pure, the illusory body is form 
and it is not consciousness. But in the continuum of the person who possesses that illusory body, at that time the mind of near -attainment manifests. It is not the extremely subtle mind 
that manifests.  
  
You h ave to remember this: The extremely subtle mind must come from a previous moment of 
the extremely subtle mind. According to Tantra, this extremely subtle mind cannot transform to something that is coarser. The coarse mind can never become the extremely sub tle mind. 
You have to keep this in your mind.   
Again, since there is a difference in subtlety of both clear light and near attainment and also a 
difference in their individual continuums, they are not similar to the two paths explained from another perspe ctive. This clear light and near attainment are different in subtlety because the 
former is a fundamental mind and the latter is slightly grosser, and because it is pervasive that any of the three: appearance, increase and attainment are not fundamental minds, and because if a fundamental mind becomes a non -fundamental mind it would not be suitable to 
explain it to be an innate mind. (Pg. 56 of the root text).   
 Look at this statement: “The three: appearance, increase and attainment are not fundamental 
minds, and because if  a fundamental mind becomes a non- fundamental mind it would not be 
suitable to explain it to be an innate mind.”  
 
Lesson  47          Page 315 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
“Since there is a difference in subtlety of both clear light and near attainment and also a difference in their individual co ntinuums, they are not similar to the two paths explained from 
another perspective ”: This statement is basically saying that the (minds of) clear light and near - 
attainment came from separate continua and they are  separate continua.  
 This is a difficult point. It means the mind of clear light, that extremely subtle mind, must come 
from a previous moment of the extremely subtle mind. Similarly, the mind of near- attainment 
must come from a previous moment of the mind of near -attainment. Likewise the mind of 
increase must come from a previous moment of the mind of increase and the mind of 
appearance must come from a previous moment of the mind of appearance. If it is a coarse mind, it must come from a previous moment of a coarse mind.  
   If that is the case, must we always maintain that there is a continuum of a similar type? Must the non- virtuous state of mind always be preceded by a non -virtuous state of mind? Must the 
virtuous state of mind always be preceded by a virtuous state of mind?  
 Won’t you have to say that there isn’t a sentient being that has never developed a virtuous thought? Because of (the fact that every sentient being has developed a virtuous thought), we 
say that Buddha’s activities pervade all sentient beings.  
 
If it is a virtue in the continuum of a sentient being, it is necessarily the enlightened activity of 
the Buddha.  If that is the case, won’t we have to say that the Buddha’s enlightened activities pervade all sentient beings? If one accepts that, is it possible  to find an instant in our past when 
one has never developed a virtuous thought?  
 In terms of the virtuous and non -virtuous minds, must they  necessarily arise from the previous 
moment of a mind of a similar type? You must think about this in terms of the continuum of a similar type.  
 In this context, when we talk about the mind of near -attainment, the question arises: Must this 
mind of near -attainment necessarily  arise from a previous moment of a mind of near -
attainment? Must there be that continuum of a similar type?  
 If your answer is no, you have to give a reason. Did the mind of near -attainment arise from the 
extremely subtle mind? The conclusion is that the mind of near- attainment must come from a 
former moment of the mind of near -attainment.  
 The m inds of appearance, increase and near -attainment are necessarily  conceptual minds 
whereas the fundamental innate mind of clear light can be either a conceptual or non -
conceptual mind.  
 Question:  What is the substantial cause of the meaning clear light of the fourth stage?  
 
Lesson  47          Page 316 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Answer : What if we say that it is the example clear light of the final mental isolation? What else 
could it be other than this? We have to posit something. There must be a mind that existed 
prior to (the meaning clear light of the fourth stage). That is the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 When one posits the example clear light of the final mental isolation to be the substantial cause of the meaning clear light of the fourth stage, won’t that mean a conceptual 
conscious ness can be the substantial cause of a non -conceptual consciousness, i.e. the 
conceptual consciousness is transformed into the entity of non -conceptual consciousness?  
 
 What is the problem in saying that? Isn’t there a difference in subtlety?  
 From anothe r point of view, the example clear light of the final mental isolation is 
contaminated and the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is uncontaminated.  
 Earlier on, we had looked at the reason why the impure illusory body has to be purified because it i s based on a contaminated phenomenon. If that is the case, doesn’t the meaning 
clear light of the fourth stage also need to be purified since it is dependent on a contaminated phenomenon, the example clear light of the final mental isolation.  
 From this, one can see that this (issue) is not so straightforward. When we really think about it, 
there are so many things to think about. That process is so important. It is not just saying yes or 
no but we have to think over the reasons for our answers. We have to  arrive at our conclusions 
based on this process of thinking and reasoning.  
 Going back to my question: Is there any sentient being who has never ever developed a virtuous mind? What is your answer: yes or no? Please give the reasons for your answer.  
 Response to student’s answer : Are you saying that all sentient beings will become enlightened? 
Every sentient being becoming enlightened does not necessarily prove that everyone must have had a virtuous thought in the past. It does not mean that if you have not had  a virtuous though t before you cannot achieve enlightenment.

Impromptu Quiz:  
1. There are two types of union: the abandonment union and the realisational union. Give an 
illustration of each of them.  
2. Give an illustration of the inseparable bliss and emptiness.  
3. Give an illustration of the afflictive obstructions and the knowledge obstructions that are 
posited in Tantra.  
4. Is the extremely subtle wind and mind the ‘I’ or person?  
5. Is the illusory body the ‘I’ or person?  
6. In HYT, it said that one can achieve enlightenment in one brief life time of this degenerate age. Is his or her body a buddha?  
 
Question 1  
Response to student’s answer to Question 1:  The mind of near - attainment when one arises 
from the meaning clear light of the fourth stage, is that an abandonment union? What is the 
abandonment union a union of?  
 
Geshe -la: If you say yes, you must have a reason and if you say no, you must have a reason. 
Why you say yes, why you say no. I thought we had al ready covered what is abandonment and 
realisational union? I thought we made it clear in the last class.  
 Response to student’s answer:  The question is asking whether near - attainment is 
abandonment union or not? Not the other way round. You can see that this is not so straight 
forward. Usually we go through class, thinking we are getting it but when a question is asked, it shows that we got nothing.  
 Question 2:  
Is it all right to posit the meaning clear light of the fourth stage as an illustration of in separable 
bliss and emptiness?   
 
Is it all right to say the pure illusory body is an illustration of abandonment union?  
 
We are not talking about pervasion here. The question is whether this can be an illustration or 
not? In this case, we are not saying whether it is necessarily so or not?  
 
Is the clear light at the end of the learner’s union an illustration of realisational union?  Is there any fault in positing that as an illustration?  
 Response to student’s answer: You are saying that when you enter the meaning clear light of the last moment, the illusory body is dissolved or purified?  
 Question 3 :

Response to student’s answer:  According to Tantra, he is saying the afflictive obstructions are

is something that you must remember.  
 
It is not that I did not mention this in class but the point of bringing up these questions is to help you to remember some of these things. You should have the wish to want to know. If you 
don’t know, then you should memorise it.  
 Question 4:  
Response to student’s answer:  Your most subtle wind and mind are you? They are sleeping 
now, aren’t they? But you are not sleeping….  
 Response to student’s answer:  It doesn’t mean that because we grasp at it therefore it is an I.  If whatever that is grasped by the mind is valid, then whatever we grasp at is necessarily correct. But most of our minds are mistaken. Because we grasp at the I, therefore we think it exists. If we use this as the reason, it is an invalid reason.  
 You also gave the reason that the extremely subtle wind and mind that move from life to life are the I because the I is the person who moves from life to life. Therefore that is the I. But that also is not necessarily the reason to prove that is the I. Whatever goes from life to life is not 
necessarily the I.  
 Response from student: They are the I because they have the entity of immutability and they go on to enlightenment.  
 Response to student’s  answer :  Is the person the extremely subtle wind? Is the I form?  
Answer from student: Yes  
 Is the extremely mind the person also?  
Answer from student: Yes  
 So, there are two persons?  
 Response from student:  No pervasion, one entity two isolates. Person is both mind and form.  
 
Response to student’s answer :  (According to you) the extremely subtle mind is the person. 
Therefore when you look for the person you should be able to find it, isn’t it? The imputed 
object when searched for can be found. When you l ook for the person, you should be able to 
find it, isn’t it?  
 Likewise, at the end of the analysis, if the extremely subtle wind is the I, that means the I can be found, isn’t it? The imputed object when searched for can be found, isn’t it?  
 When we talk about the I, what is the I that exists? If it is an I, it is necessarily the I that is 
merely imputed through the force of conceptuality.   
 
Lesson  48          Page 319 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Is the I and the merely labelled I necessarily the same? They are necessarily the same. If it is an 
I, it is necessarily the merely labelled I. If it is the merely labelled I, it is necessarily the I.  
 When you say the extremely subtle wind or mind is the person, this means the I can be found when searched for.  
 Geshe -la: It is not a question of where the I is labelled. The question is: what is the I? My 
question is: are the subtle wind and mind a person or not?  
 In response to this question, the extremely subtle wind and mind are not  the person. The 
extremely subtle wind is not the person. The extremely subtle mind is not  the person. We have 
covered this in Lorig.  
• The extremely subtle wind is not the person because it is form.  
• The extremely subtle mind is not the person because it is consciousness.  
There is a basis of designation of the I or person. The coars e I and the subtle I depend on the 
basis of the designation. The subtle basis of designation of the I are the extremely subtle wind and mind. The extremely subtle wind and mind are the basis of designation of the I. The extremely subtle wind and mind which are the basis designation of the I is not I.  
 Question 5:  
Response to student’s answer: Isn’t that a fully qualified deity? Then why is it not a person? A deity is not a person? Isn’t the pure illusory body a deity? If it is not a deity, then what is it?   
 Response to student’s answer :  It is the body of the deity. It follows that you are a deity when 
you are a learner.  
 
Is the pure illusory body a person or not? Earlier on, we had already ascertained that the 
extremely subtle wind is not the person because it is form. This means that form is not the person. There is no common locus between form and person. (Common locus means you cannot posit something that is both form and person at the same time). Earlier we have already seen how the illusory body is fo rm. Therefore it cannot be a person.  
 
****************************  
 The meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the uninterrupted path which is the direct antidote to the afflictive obstructions as posited in Highest Yoga Tantra. That single uninterrupted path abandons all the afflictive obstructions.  
  
The meaning clear light at the end of the continuum of the learner abandons the knowledge obstructions. That single uninterrupted path abandons all the knowledge obstructions.  
 There are some scholars wh o assert that the meaning clear light of the fourth stage is the 
single uninterrupted path that abandons all the afflictive obstructions but when it comes to the knowledge obstructions, there are nine levels of the knowledge obstructions. According to thes e scholars, one has to generate the meaning clear light nine times in order to fully abandon 
the knowledge obstructions which are abandoned sequentially.  
 
Lesson  48          Page 320 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
For those scholars who assert that the meaning clear light at the end of the continuum of the learne r abandons all the knowledge obstructions at one time, do they also assert this 
presentation of the nine grounds? They also posit the presentation of the nine grounds but for them, these nine grounds are not posited in terms of generating an uninterrupted path and then entering a path of thorough release. They posit the nine grounds on the basis of accumulating the collection of merit for the purpose of making the meaning clear light at the 
end of the continuum of the learner powerful.  
 
First you have to keep in mind that:  
• The meaning clear light of the fourth stage abandons all the afflictive obstructions. What are the afflictive obstructions that are abandoned by the meaning clear light of the fourth stage? These afflictive obstructions are the ordinary conceptions.  
• The meaning clear light at the end of the continuum of the learner abandons all the 
knowledge obstructions. The knowledge obstructions that are abandoned are the ordinary 
appearances. When those knowledge obstructions are abandoned, one achiev es the non-
learner’s union (sometime translated as the union of no -more learning).  
 All the way up to the meaning clear light at the end of the continuum of the learner, the minds of white appearance, red increase and black near attainment exist. Once one achieves enlightenment, one is always in the state of clear light.  The minds of appearance, increase or 
near attainment are necessary conceptual minds.   
 We have two more lessons to go. We will look at the aspiration prayer at the end of the long 
Guhyasam aja sadhana and based on that, we will try to summarise the whole path.  
 
There is a debate in the text about the duration of the meaning clear light. Please read up 
these sections. The text is saying that the meaning clear light last for a very short time  and, at 
times, it can last for eons. Please think about this.  
 Question: (inaudible )  
Answer :  If one follows the first system where all the knowledge obstructions are abandoned, 
then the meaning clear light is the meaning clear light at the end of the c ontinuum of the 
learner which means tat means the realisational union has to be on the tenth ground.  
 Question: Does this mean that the second to the ninth grounds will occur during the abandonment union?  
Answer : According to this system, then would have t o say that is so since the realisational 
union only occurs at the tenth ground.  
 
 
 Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme
