Lesson  48          Page 321 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
LESSON NO: 49   DATE: 22ND SEPTEMBER  2009  
 We will spend five minutes to adjust our motivation to ensure that whatever we do here becomes Dharma. Our actions should be conjoined with the attitude of refuge and the motivation of bodhicitta. Whatever that is done that is conjoined with refuge becomes Buddhist practice and when conjoined with bodhicitta becomes Mahayana practice.  So, in 
order to mak e whatever we do here Dharma, it should be conjoined with the attitude of refuge 
and the motivation of bodhicitta.  
 From our side, we have to put in the effort to develop these attitudes in our minds to make whatever we engage in Buddhist and Mahayana pra ctices. Otherwise, simply because the 
subject matter we are dealing with here is Buddhadharma or Mahayana Dharma does not necessarily mean that the activities we do in relation to it will become Dharma. That has to come from our side.  
 
******************** *** 
 
Question: Must we have taken the Guhyasamaja initiation to read this prayer?  
Answer: That is not necessary. As long as you have received a Highest Yoga Tantric initiation, 
you are permitted to do so because this prayer summarises what we have covered so far on 
the generation and completion stages.  
 
********************* 
 This particular Guhyasamaja Prayer was composed by Lama Tsong Khapa and is a summary of the generation and completion stages of Guhyasamaja. We are looking at this prayer as a way of concluding this Basic Program which has lasted for the past six years and use it as an 
aspirational prayer as well as a basis for making our dedications.   
 
3   
Thus, within the state of appearing as a body of a deity,  
Appearing yet empty, like an illusion  and a dream,  
In order to generate delight with marvellous bliss  
In the host of deities of the mandala of Akshobhyavajra - 
The condensed quintessence of all the countless conquerors of the ten directions  
I strive here to make outer, inner and secret offeri ngs, 
Praises, meditations, recitations and so forth.  
 
 
4 
By whatever virtue I have thus accumulated:  
 
The lines that I have just read out indicate the substance that one is dedicating.  
 
“Thus, within the state of appearing as a body of a deity, appearing yet empty, like an illusion 
 
Lesson  49          Page 322 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
and a dream ”: In this practice, all that appear are empty and great bliss. From within that 
state, one arises as a deity.  
 
Here, one makes offerings, praises and so forth to the deities of the mandala of 
Akshobhyavajra who are the combined essence “ of all the countless conquerors of the ten 
directions.” Why does one do this? It is for the purpose of generating “ delight with marvellous 
bliss.”  
 
What are the offer ings that one is making to the host of deities of the mandala of 
Akshobhyavajra? They are the “ outer, inner and secret offerings/ Praises, meditations, 
recitations and so forth.”  
 
In the context here, the roots of virtue that one are dedicating are the roo ts of virtue acquired 
from making and doing all these different offerings  and practices -  outer, inner and secret 
offerings, praises, meditations on the supported and supporting mandala, recitations and so forth.  
 Up to this point, the prayer is referri ng to the objects that one is dedicating. Then, everything 
following this shows the manner in which one dedicates. This starts with the line, “By whatever virtue I have thus accumulated.”   
 
4 (contd)  
Having seen that just as I have fallen into the ocean of existence  
Likewise too have all mother migrating beings.  
May I become skilled in the supreme mind of enlightenment  
That takes on the responsibility of liberating migrating beings.  
 
This shows the generation of aspirational bodhicitta so first one  dedicates the root of virtues 
for the generation of aspirational bodhicitta.  
 
Question: (inaudible)  
Answer: When one looks at the third verse, it starts with oneself as a deity. All Tantri c practices 
are done from within deity yoga. One always arises as the deity in the beginning.  
 
In the Nyung Na practice, there is the self- generation and front generation. One makes 
offerings to both. That is done with oneself as Chenrezig. Then one does the practice of 
Vajrasattva in the sadhana. One sees oneself as the deity but one still performs the Vajrasattva 
practice. One also performs the seven -limb prayer to accumulate merit and to purify 
obscurations. That is also done with oneself as Chenrezig ( or whatever deity one is practising). 
The point is that all these are done within deity yoga.  
 
If you have any doubts, you can clarify them in the future as we have to finish this prayer 
within two classes.  
 
5 
Having seen enlightenment cannot be achieved  
 
Lesson  49          Page 323 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
By my simply generating a mind wishing for that to come about  
Unless I also practise the three kinds of morality,  
May I train in the vows of the conqueror's children with intense effort.  
 
Here one dedicates the roots of virtue towards the generation of engaged bodhicitta.  
 
6  
When I have become a vessel trained in the common path,  
May I enter easily the holy entrance of the fortunate beings,  
The supreme of all vehicles, the Vajrayana.  
 
Here one dedicates the roots of virtue towards oneself becoming a suitable basis for Tantric 
practice.  
 
The three verses we have covered so far show that one first dedicates the roots of virtue 
towards generating the aspirational bodhicitta, followed by engaged bodhicitta. One only 
becomes a suitable vessel  after having  trained in the common or shared path. One then 
dedicates that one becomes a suitable basis for Tantric practice. After becoming a suitable 
vessel for the Tantric practice, one takes the initiations.  
 
7 
By the conferring of the vase empowerment with streams of Ganges water  
May the stains of appearance of and adherence to ordinariness be cleansed.  
By tasting the nectar of exalted wisdom during the secret empowerment  
May the winds, the source of speech, dawn as mantras.  
 
8 
By the hook of the co -emerge nt bliss of the third empowerment  
May the mind be drawn into the sphere of clear light.  
By being introduced to the meaning of union during the fourth empowerment 
May all hallucination about the ultimate aim be severed  
 
• By the conferring of the vase empower ment , the stains of ordinary appearance and 
ordinary concept are cleansed. When one receives the vase empowerment, one is 
empowered to practise the generation stage.  
• After the vase empowerment, the next empowerment to be conferred is the secret 
empowerment . The secret empowerment empowers one to practise the stages of physical, 
verbal and mental isolations and illusory body.  
• After the secret initiation, the wisdom knowledge empowerment  empowers one to 
meditate on the meaning clear light.  
• By receiving the fourth empowerment, the word empowerment , one is empowered to 
meditate on union.  
 
At the time of receiving these empowerments, one takes on various vows, pledges and 
commitments, which one has to keep purely. Therefore, the prayer continues:  
 
 
Lesson  49          Page 324 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
9 
At that time, having gained genuine conviction that 
Keeping completely pure pledges and vows  
Is the basis of achieving the two types of attainment.  
May I always guard them at the cost of my life.  
 
It is extremely important to keep the vows and samayas that one has  taken purely because, as 
mentioned clearly here: “ Keeping completely pure pledges and vows i s the basis of achieving 
the two types of attainment, ” the supreme and common attainments.  Therefore there is a 
need to keep all the vows and samayas purely.  
 
After having received the empowerments, one has to train in the generation and completion 
stages. Therefore one dedicates the root of virtues to be able to practise and generate those 
realisations of these two stages.  
 
Verse 10 summarises the generation stage  
 
10 
At that time, having gained genuine conviction that  
Of the coarse and subtle first generation stage,   
The four branches of application and approximation in four sessions,  
Which thoroughly gather the collection of great waves of deeds effortlessly   
With all the movements of body, expressions of speech, and thoughts of mind  
That have abandoned the appearance of and  
The conception adhering to ordinariness,  
May whatever appears dawn as the circle of deities.  
 
Here, in the summary of the generation stage, after having abandoned ordinary appearance 
and concept, in relation to whatever one does –  “With all the movements of body, expressions 
of speech, and thoughts of mind” -   one gathers “great waves of deeds ” (meaning merit) easily.  
 
One trains in “ the four branches of application and approximation” which are the c oarse and 
subtle generation stages. During these stages, one trains to see the environment and the 
inhabitants as the supporting and supported mandala. This is the practice that is done in the 
generation stage.  
 
The following verses then give a detailed explanation of the generation stage practice.  
 
11  
By relying on the perfect field of the mind mandala  
And acting in accordance with the rites of accumulating the collections,  
 
The practices such as making offerings within the three pure spheres,  
May my continuum be completely purified.  
 
Prior to the main practice of the sadhana, one visualises the merit field as the basis on which 
 
Lesson  49          Page 325 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
one does the practices of prostrations, making offerings, confessions, rejoicing, taking refuge 
and generating both the aspirational and engaged bodhicitta, dedicating the merit and taking 
the vows. All these practices are done in relation to the merit field: “By relying on the perfect 
field of the min d mandala.”  
 
It is called the mind (or heart) mandala because the merit field that one visualises in front is 
the merit field that one invokes. With oneself purified as the deity and emitting light rays 
from one’s heart to the ten directions, one invokes into the space in front of oneself the 
entire mandala and mandala deities which are in separable from one’s root guru. Therefore it 
is called the mind (or heart) mandala.   
 
Before one can train in the generation stage, one must first   accumulate merit. That is why 
one invites the merit field into one’s  presence and on the basis of that, one accumulates 
merit. The purpose of doing so is to thoroughly purify the mind for the practice of the 
generation stage.  
 
Even when one’s mind is thoroughly purified for the practice of generation stage, during the 
actual practice, one will probably encounter various obstacles. In order to stop or eliminate 
these obstacles, one meditates on the protection wheel. This is highlighted in the next verse:  
 
12 
By the meditative stabilisation of the glorious wrathful Akshobhya  
Encircled by the retinue often wrathful ones,  
Annihilating all those with harmful intent wandering in the ten directions,  
May enlightened activities be accomplished.  
 
We do not have the time to go into details or even to explain what the protection wheel is. For 
those of you who are familiar with the Guhyasamaja long sadhana, you can make a comparison 
by relating  these verses to the long sadhana.  
  
Next comes the actual generation stage practice which is divided into three meditative 
stabilisations or concentrations. They are:  
1. the meditative stabilisation of initial preparation  
2. the meditative stabilisation of the supreme victory mandala  
3. meditative stabilisation of the supreme victorious activities  
 
The first meditative stabilisation of initial preparation is for the purpose of “ perfecting one’s 
own welfare .” The profound meaning of initial preparation is “ initial union ” which is what is 
translated here. Verse 13 says:  
 
13 
Where all the enlightened fields have been purified, may the initial union be accomplished,  
That holy method that will manifest the state of the three bodies,  
Which acts to purify all appearances of and adherence to  
Ordinary death, intermediate state, and rebirth 
By generating in the continuum respectively the supreme vajra that accordingly carries  
 
Lesson  49          Page 326 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Death into the Truth body, intermediate state into the Enjoyment body  
And rebirth into the Emanation body  
Through the meditative stabilisation of the way of great desire,  
The quick path of perfecting one's welfare,  
Skill in the method of devoting to the Conquerors.  
 
One creates the place where one will be enlightened, the pure field. This  is basically one’s 
celestial mansion and that is done for one’s own pur pose. In order to arise into the body of the 
deity that will benefit others, one brings to the path death, the intermediate state and rebirth. 
One accomplishes that by bringing death into the path of the dharmakaya, the intermediate state into the path of the sambhogakaya and rebirth into the path of the nirmanakaya.  
 
In order to stop ordinary appearance and concept in relation to death, the intermediate state 
and rebirth, one meditates on the first meditative stabilisation of initial preparation.  
 
Ven Gyurme: I would like to highlight the translation difficulties here because Tibetan and 
English work differently. Therefore, here they have combined two verses into one and 
whichever is the later verse is translated first followed by the earlier verse.  But in Tibetan the 
verses are the other way round and the commentary is given according to the Tibetan text. So 
there may be some difficulties when we present it in English.  
 
The quick path for actualising the three bodies is the practice of entering into  union with the 
consort. “ Through the meditative stabilisation of the way of great desire” in Verse 13 refers to 
entering into union with the consort  and that is again highlighted in the second half of Verse 14:  
 
14 
Born from the bodhicitta drop which is melted by the Chandali inner fire,  
Ignited by the joining of the sphere of space and the vajra,  
And has traversed the continuous path of the Avadhuti central channel  
To reach the pistils of the wisdom consort's lotus.  
 
Verse 13 is the verse on the first me ditative stabilisation of initial preparation. Verse 14 verse 
refers to the second meditative stabilisation of the supreme victory mandala. Vijaya  is the 
Sanskrit word for victory. This second meditative stabilisation is the meditation on the 
enlightened a ctivities that accord with the pure activities of the holy body of the Buddha.  
 
14 
May I quickly accomplish the Vijaya mandala  
That thoroughly cleanses all fields of the environment and the inhabitants  
By filling the extent of space with clouds of emanations  
From the supported and supporting mandalas  
Born from the bodhicitta drop which is melted by the Chandali inner fire,  
Ignited by the joining of the sphere of space and the vajra,  
And has traversed the continuous path of the Avadhuti central channel  
To reach the pistils of the wisdom consort's lotus.  
 
 
Lesson  49          Page 327 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Next is the meditative stabilisation of the supreme victorious activities (or supreme royal 
activities). That is the time when the practice of the subtle generation stage is done with the 
repetition o f mantra and so forth. Basically this meditative stabilisation is essentially the 
meditation that accords with the activities of the holy mind of the Buddha.  
 
15 
May I thoroughly perfect the subtle yoga that sets in equipoise,  
Clearly without mixing for as  long as one wishes,  
The complete cycle of the mandala, within the insignia,  
Or within the subtle drop of bodhicitta the size of a mustard seed,  
The five coloured jewel with the nature of the five Sugatas,  
Having a radiating net of light rays,  
Residing at the tip of the path of the vitality -holding and downward- voiding winds,  
And may I perfect as well the outer and inner recitations.  
 
• The “ path of the vitality -holding” wind  refers to the upper tip of the central channel where 
one visualises a very subtle im plement of the deities.  
• The path of “ downward -voiding ” wind refers to the tip of the secret place where one 
visualises a subtle drop the size of a mustard seed.  
• After achieving stability in the coarse generation stage, one then meditates on this subtle 
drop as stated here: “ The five coloured jewel with the nature of the five Sugatas,/H aving a 
radiating net of light rays. ”  
• When one has achieved stability with respect to that, then one visualises inside that the 
entire supported and supporting mandala.  
• When one is able to sustain this visualisation “ clearly without mixing” all the subtle details 
and one is able to do that “ for as long as one wishes, ” that is the measure of having 
attained the subtle generation stage.  
• “And may I perfect as well the outer and inner recitations”:  The inner recitation refers to 
mental recitation.  
 
After the subtle generation stage and after the practice of the repetitions, one seeks to 
achieve the common attainment.  
 
16 
Then in dependence on substance, mantra, yogic exercise, and concentration  
May I accomplish the karma vijaya mandala  
Through accomplishing the eight great attainments  
And all the countless activities of pacifying and so forth.  
 
The “ karma vijaya mandala” is the supreme victory mandala. One seeks to accomplish the 
eight great attainments which are considered common attainments. They are:  
1. the ability to perceive buried treasures  
2. the pure attainment, i.e. the ability to make blessed pills  
3. the sword attainme nt 
4. the ability to fly in the sky  
 
Lesson  49          Page 328 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
5. the swift legs attainment  
6. immortality  
7. the ability to pacify sicknesses  
8. invisibility  
We always like to focus on the results but first we have to understand what the causes are and 
see whether we can achieve them. Nothing w ill happen by just thinking of the results. The main 
point is to train in the causes. When the causes are present, then the results can be achieved.   
 
The generation stage is covered up to here. The verses that follow deal with the completion 
stage. We wi ll look at these in the next lesson.

GUHYASAMAJA PRAYER  
 
1 
By this virtue  
May I quickly attain the state of Vajradhara,  
The whole essence of all Buddhas,  
And may all migrating beings also attain that state.  
 2 
For the sake of enlightenment,  
May I practise the two conducts  
Those taught by the perfect Buddhas  
And those taught by Bodhivajra.  
 
3 
Thus, within the state of appearing as a body of a deity,  
Appearing yet empty, like an illusion and a dream,  
In order to generate delight with marvellous bliss  
In the host of deities of the mandala of Akshobhyavajra –  
The condensed quintessence of all the countless conquerors of the ten directions.  
I strive here to make outer, inner and secret offerings,  
Praises, meditations, recitations and so forth.  
 4 
By whatever virtue I have thus accumulated:  
 
Lesson  49          Page 329 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Having seen that just as I have fallen into the ocean of existence  
Likewise too have all mot her migrating beings.  
May I become skilled in the supreme mind of enlightenment  
That takes on the responsibility of liberating migrating beings.  
 
 5 
Having seen enlightenment cannot be achieved  
By my simply generating a mind wishing for that to come about  
Unless I also practise the three kinds of morality,  
May I train in the vows of the conqueror’s children with intense effort.  
 6 
When I have become a vessel trained in the common path,  
May I enter easily the holy entrance of the fortunate beings,  
The suprem e of all vehicles, the Vajrayana.  
 7 
By the conferring of the vase empowerment with streams of Ganges water May the stains of appearance of and adherence to ordinariness be cleansed.  
By tasting the nectar of exalted wisdom during the secret empowerment  
May  the winds, the source of speech, dawn as mantras.  
 8 
By the hooks of the co -emergent bliss of the third empowerment  
May the mind be drawn into the sphere of clear light.  
By being introduced to the meaning of union during the fourth empowerment  
May all hallucination about the ultimate aim be severed.  
 9 
At that time, having gained genuine conviction that  
Keeping completely pure pledges and vows  
Is the basis of achieving the two types of attainment.  
May I always guard them at the cost of my life.  
 10 
At that time, having gained genuine conviction that  
Of the coarse and subtle first generation stage,  
The four branches of application and approximation in four sessions,  
Which thoroughly gather the collection of great waves of deeds effortlessly  
With all the movements of body, expressions of speech, and thoughts of mind  
That have abandoned the appearance of and  
The conception adhering to ordinariness,  
May whatever appears dawn as the circle of deities.  
 11 
By relying on the perfect field of the mind mandala

And acting in accordance with the rites of accumulating the collections,  
The practices such as making offerings within the three pure spheres,  
May my continuum be completely purified.  
 
12 
By the meditative stabilisation of the glorious wrathful Akshobhya  
Encircled by the retinue of ten wrathful ones,  
Annihilating all those with harmful intent wandering in the ten directions,  
May enlightened activities be accomplished.  
 13 
Where all the enlightened fields have been purified, may the initial union be accompl ished,  
That holy method that will manifest the state of the three bodies,  
Which acts to purify all appearances of and adherence to  
Ordinary death, intermediate state, and rebirth  
By generating in the continuum respectively the supreme vajra that  
accordingly carries  
Death into the Truth body, intermediate state into the Enjoyment body  
And rebirth into the Emanation body  
Through the meditative stabilisation of the way of great desire,  
The quick path of perfecting one’s welfare,  
Skill in the method of devoting  to the Conquerors.  
 14 
May I quickly accomplish the Vijaya mandala  
That thoroughly cleanses all fields of the environment and the inhabitants  
By filling the extent of space with clouds of emanations  
From the supported and supporting mandalas  
Born from the  bodhicitta drop which is melted by the Chandali inner fire,  
Ignited by the joining of the sphere of space and the vajra,  
And has traversed the continuous path of the Avadhuti central channel  
To reach the pistils of the wisdom consort’s lotus.  
 15 
May I thoroughly perfect the subtle yoga that sets in equipoise,  
Clearly without mixing for as long as one wishes,  
The complete cycle of the mandala, within the insignia,  
Or within the subtle drop of bodhicitta the size of a mustard seed,  
The five coloured jewel  with the nature of the five Sugatas,  
Having a radiating net of light rays,  
Residing at the tip of the path of the vitality -holding and downward- voiding winds,  
And may I perfect as well the outer and inner recitations.  
 16 
Then in dependence on substance, mantra, yogic exercise, and concentration  
May I accomplish the karma vijaya mandala

This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Through accomplishing the eight great attainments  
And all the countless activities of pacifying and so forth.  
 
17 
May I accomplish the five levels of the completion stage a long with the three conducts,  
The profound path which includes the six branches –  
Individual withdrawal, concentration, vitality -exertion,  
Apprehension, subsequent mindfulness, and meditative stabilisation  
 18 
May I generate the four joys of downward -desce nding and upward -stabilising  
Which are induced by the stages of the forward and reverse process  
Of the stream of nectar from the moon melted by the sun  
In dependence on the mind in the subtle drop at the jewel.  
 19 
Having arisen from that, may I perfect the individual withdrawal and concentration  
Of the isolation of body through the meditative stabilisation of the body vajra  
Where all appearances that have arisen  
Dawn as one hundred, five, three or a sole lineage.  
 
20 
May I gather into the indestructible drop  
The twelve winds that produce the conceptions of apprehended and apprehender  
Through familiarising with the supreme mantra drop, the king of drops,  
At the point of the lotus in the heart.  
 
21 
May I break open the eggshell of ignorance at my heart  
With  the isolation of speech, the meditative stabilisation of vajra speech,  
Through making the tones of the light drop at the bridge of the nose  
Dawn as the uncontrived three vajras.  
 22 
Having cleared away the darkness of the winds of indicative conception  
Through meditating on vitality -exertion of the substance drop  
At the point of the two supreme secret channels of method and wisdom,  
May the sun of clear light dawn at the centre of my heart.  
 23 
May I generate the isolation of mind of the three empties,  
Whic h resemble the cloudless sky  
Pervaded by moonlight, sunlight, and darkness,  
Through mediating on the vitality -exertion wind at the three drops at  
the three points.

This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
24 
By being skilled in the essential points of the oral instructions,  
Perfecting the nine types of mixing, three types for each of the three – 
The illusory enjoyment body, the clear light truth body and the diverse emanation  
Body  
 
25 
May I thoroughly perfect the apprehension  
Of the conventional illusory body and ultimate clear light,  
The subsequent mindfulness of reverse order,  
The meditative stabilisation of union,  
And the three supreme conducts – elaborate, unelaborate, and highly unelaborate.  
 26 
If, at the time of death I have not achieved the meditative stabilisation  
Which accomplishes the supreme attainment in this life or the intermediate state,  
May I be able to mix the four empties of the stages of death  
At the time of the basis with the four empties of the path.  
  27 
At the time of the intermediate state, may I mix it with  
The illusory m editative stabilisation, and at the time of taking birth  
May I take rebirth at will in a supreme place of birth,  
Just as the Enjoyment body sustains the birth of the emanation body.  
 28 
In short whether it is death, intermediate state or birth that dawns  
Having realised it as an exhortation to virtuous practice,  
A reminder of the three types of bringing into the path cultivated previously,  
May my mind overflow with joy at the time of death.  
 29 
Thus, may the culmination of this marvellous path  
Be accomplis hed accordingly without obstacles  
May it spread and expand throughout all directions and times  
And may all migrating beings be liberated by this skilful means.

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
LESSON NO: 5 0  DATE: 24TH SEPTEMBER 2009  
 
We will spend five minutes to reflect on the verse:  
Do not commit any non- virtuous actions,  
Perform only perfect virtuous actions,  
Subdue your mind thoroughly  
This is the teaching of the Buddha.  
 
You can also reflect on the verse from the Vajra Cutter Sutra:  
A star, a mirage, a flame of a lamp,  
An illusion, a drop of dew, or a bubble,  
A dream, a flash of lightning, a cloud,  
See conditioned things as such.  
 
There are many ways to interpret the verse that starts with “ Do not commit any non- virtuous 
actions…”  Looking at it in a general way, “ Do not commit any non- virtuous actions”  can be 
taken to mean that one should give up all non- virtues committe d with one’s body and, 
conversely, it can also mean that one should accumulate virtue with one’s body.  
 “Perform only perfect virtuous actions” can be related to abandoning the non -virtue of speech 
and in its place, one performs only perfect actions of spe ech, e.g., doing well various 
recitations, daily prayers and so forth to stop the negative karma of speech such as meaningless speech.  
 “Subdue your mind thoroughly” can refer to whatever one does with one’s body and speech. 
One makes sure they are done wi th a good motivation and a good heart, abandoning negative 
thoughts such as the thought of harming others and so forth.  
 So, there are different ways of interpreting this verse. Generally speaking, this verse is related to the three scopes. The first line is related to the path that is shared with the persons of small capacity. The second line is related to the path that is shared with the persons of medium 
capacity. The third line is related to the path that is shared with the persons of great capacity.  
 When one reflects on that verse according to this explanation, one can relate it to oneself and 
see whether one is able to abandon the non- virtues of body and speech and whether one is 
able to cultivate the good heart and be a disciplined and calm person. When one is able to do 
that, I guess one can call oneself a practitioner.  
 For us beginners, this is where we should start. It is difficult for us to be able to generate the paths of the three types of person. But at least we should start from here to see  whether we 
can modify the behaviour of our bodies, speech and minds.  
 
One can see that from just this one verse, there are many ways of looking at and interpreting 
its meaning. There are different messages that one can extract from that verse . You can do this 
according to what was explained and put (the verse) into practice.

17 
May I accomplish the five levels of the completion stage along with the three conducts,  
The profound path which includes the six branches –  
Individual withdrawal, concentration, vitality -exertion,  
Apprehension, subsequent mindfulness, and meditative stabilisation  
 This is a summary of the completion stage. This enumeration of the six branches is found in the Continuation Tantra and are: “Individual withdrawal, concentration, vitality -exertion, 
apprehension, subsequent mindfulness, and meditative stabilization.”  
 These six branches can be condensed into the five profound stages of the completion stage:  
• Individual withdrawal and meditative stabilisation are included in       physical isolation.  
• Vitality -exertion is included in verbal isolation.  
• Apprehension is included in clear light  
• Subsequent mindfulness and concentration are included in union.  
 Geshe -la: This is a summary of what we have done long time  ago. I have already explained 
everything and made it very clear in the beginning. Now is not whether you know or not because there is no time. If you want to know, you must go back and read.  
 “The three conducts ” here refers to:  
• the conduct with elaborati on,  
• the unelaborated conduct and  
• the highly unelaborated conduct.  
These three conducts are the practices to enhance one’s practice of the completion stage.  
 This is just a very brief presentation of the completion stage. When we expand the completion stage, we have:  
• the completion stage of the three isolations and  
• the completion stage of the two truths  
 The completion stage of the three isolations is:  
• physical isolation  
• verbal isolation and  
• mental isolation.  
 
When the physical isolation is elaborated, there are:  
• practices that are done in meditative equipoise  
• practices that are done in post- equipoise  
 What practices are done in physical isolation while in meditative equipoise? This is shown in the next verse:  
 
Lesson  50          Page 335

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra

18 
May I generate the four joys of downward -descending and upward- stabilising  
Which are induced by the stages of the forward and reverse process  
Of the stream of nectar from the moon melted by the sun  
In dependence on the mind in the subtle drop at the jewel.  
 
The practices that are done in physica l isolation while in meditative equipoise are:  
• One visualises oneself as Vajradhara, father and mother.  
• At the secret place in the middle of the jewel, one visualises the subtle drop  
• Through focussing on the subtle drop in the middle of the jewel, the winds are gathered and cause the tumo (here referred to as the “ sun”) to blaze.  
• The sun melts the “ moon ,” which refers to the white bodhicitta at the crown. Through the 
blazing of the tumo at the navel, the white bodhicitta at the crown is melted.  
• Throug h that, the four joys of the forward and reverse processes are induced.  
 
During meditative equipoise, one generates the four joys. The bliss that is induced is used to ascertain emptiness. Here, one tries to bring together bliss and emptiness. Once one ha s 
brought these two together, one remains in meditative equipoise focussing on that.  
 After one arises from meditative equipoise, what does one do in post-  meditative equipoise? 
This is explained in the next verse:  
 19 
Having arisen from that, may I perfec t the individual withdrawal and concentration  
Of the isolation of body through the meditative stabilisation of the body vajra  
Where all appearances that have arisen  
Dawn as one hundred, five, three or a sole lineage.  
 Having arisen from that meditative equ ipoise, one trains to see whatever appears as the 
display of bliss and emptiness. This is the practice of individual withdrawal. I guess “ Where all 
appearances that have arisen” is related to the practice of individual withdrawal.  
 “May I perfect the individual withdrawal and concentration”:   “ Concentration” is translated as 
meditative stabilisation in our root text.  
• In post- meditative equipoise in the completion stage of the physical isolation, there is also 
the practice of concentration.  
• In the practice of individual withdrawal, one sees everything that appears as the display of bliss and emptiness.  
• In the practice of concentration, one sees whatever appears as the display of the 100 immaculate lineages.  
• The 100 immaculate lineages can be condensed into the five buddha lineages.  
• The five buddha lineages can be further condensed into the three lineages of the vajra body, speech and mind.  
• Those three lineages can be condensed into the great secret “ sole lineage” where one 
 
Lesson  50          Page 336 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
trains to see whatever appears to oneself as the display of Vajradhara.  
 
What happens after the isolation of body (physical isolation)? The next level is the isolation of speech (verbal isolation). During the completion stage of verbal isolation, one meditates on the 
three stages of practice:  
• meditating on the mantra drop at the heart  
• meditating on the light drop at the upper end of the central channel  
• meditating on the substance drop at the lower end of the central channel (at the secret 
place)  
 
The meditation on the mantra drop at the heart is explained in the next verse:  
 20 
May I gather into the indestructible drop  
The twelve winds that produce the conceptions of apprehended and apprehender  
Through familiarising with the supreme mantra drop, the king of drops , 
At the point of the lotus in the heart.  
 Having consummated the practice of physical isolation, one proceeds to the next practice where one visualises the red and white king of drops (i.e., the indestructible drop) at the heart in the central channel. In the middle of that indestructible drop, one visualises the supreme 
mantra drop, the white syllable AH. This is done whilst seeing oneself as Vajradhara, father and 
mother. Through familiarity with that meditation, one causes all the winds to enter, abide and 
dissolve in the central channel and through that, the four empties arise.  
 Here “twelve winds ” are mentioned: there are six winds of the apprehender and six winds of 
the apprehended. Through meditation, one attempts to gather all the twelve winds into  the 
indestructible drop at the heart.  
 The next verse describes the meditation of the light drop at the upper end of the central channel which is “ at the bridge of the nose .”  
 21 
May I break open the eggshell of ignorance at my heart  
With the isolation of speech, the meditative stabilisation of vajra speech,  
Through making the tones of the light drop at the bridge of the nose  
Dawn as the uncontrived three vajras . 
 The yogi, seeing himself clearly as Vajradhara, father and mother,  visualises the light dr op at 
the upper end of the central channel which is at the bridge of the nose. Through that meditation, the yogi tries to make the uncontrived tones (i.e., the natural tones of the inhalation, exhalation and abiding) inseparable from the tones of the three  syllables OM AH 
HUM: “T hrough making the tones of the light drop at the bridge of the nose/ Dawn as the 
uncontrived three vajras. ” That vajra recitation is meant to completely loosen the channel 
knots at the heart which is referred to in the first line: “ May I break open the eggshell of 
ignorance at my heart. ”

FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
The next verse deals with the meditation on the substance drop at the secret place.  
 22 
Having cleared away the darkness of the winds of indicative conception  
Through meditating on vitality -exertion of the substance drop  
At the point of the two supreme secret channels of method and wisdom,  
May the sun of clear light dawn at the centre of my heart.  
 The “method ” refers to the father and “ wisdom”  refers to the mother, the consort. The lower 
ends of “ the two supreme secrets channels ” (i.e., the two central channels of the father and 
mother) meet and it is at this meeting point that one meditates on the substance drop. This meditation causes all the winds to gather and from it, “ the sun o f clear light dawn at the centre 
of my heart .” 
 The next verse refers to mental isolation.  
 23 
May I generate the isolation of mind of the three empties,  
Which resemble the cloudless sky  
Pervaded by moonlight, sunlight, and darkness,  
Through mediating on t he vitality -exertion wind at the three drops at the three points.  
 “Through mediating on the vitality -exertion wind at the three drops at the three points ,” one 
generates “ the isolation of mind of the three empties .” Here, only three empties are mentioned.  
As I have explained before, it is not that clear light does not exist during mental isolation. It does exist but it is subsumed under the explanation of the mind of near - attainment. Therefore 
the reference is only to the three empties.  
 This is followed  by the presentation of the completion stage of the two truths which have 
different levels. There are:  
• the completion stage of the conventional illusory body,  
• the completion stage of the clear light and  
• the completion stage of the inseparable two truths.   
 In order to arise as the illusory body, one has to meditate on the oral instructions pertaining to 
the nine mixings (or blendings). Therefore the next verse says:  
 
24 
By being skilled in the essential points of the oral instructions,  
Perfecting the nine  types of mixing, three types for each of the three – 
The illusory enjoyment body, the clear light truth body and the diverse emanation Body  
 I have talked about the nine mixings before. They are:  
• the three mixings of the waking state  
 
Lesson  50          Page 338 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
• the three mixings of the sleeping state  
• the three mixings at the time of death  
 
One has to be skilled in these nine mixings.  
• In order to practise the three mixings at the time of death, one has to be able to do the practice of the three mixings at the time of sleep.  
• In orde r for one to be able to practise the three mixings at the time of sleep, one must be 
able to do the practice of the three mixings of the waking state.  
• In order for one to be able to practise the three mixings of the waking state, one must perform well the  practice of taking the three bodies into the path at the time of practising 
the generation stage.  
 
25 
May I thoroughly perfect the apprehension  
Of the conventional illusory body and ultimate clear light,  
The subsequent mindfulness of reverse order,  
The m editative stabilisation of union,  
And the three supreme conducts – elaborate, unelaborate, and highly unelaborate . 
 
The “ conventional illusory body ” refers to the impure illusory body. With the extremely subtle 
wind upon which the example clear light of the final mental isolation is mounted acting as the substantial cause and the example clear light of the final mental isolation acting as the co -
operative condition, one attains the illusory body, a fully characterised deity body that is said to be in the na ture of wisdom. That body is something different from one’s coarse body. 
Having achieved this impure illusory body, by depending on the two stages of dissolution of meditative stabilisation, one causes all the winds to dissolve, culminating in the fourth e mpty, 
clear light . The two stages of dissolution of meditative stabilisation are: (1) apprehending the 
entirety and (2) subsequent destruction  
 25 
May I thoroughly perfect the apprehension  
Of the conventional illusory body and ultimate clear light,  
The subsequent mindfulness of reverse order,  
The meditative stabilisation of union,  
And the three supreme conducts – elaborate, unelaborate, and highly unelaborate . 
 When the mind of clear light perceives emptiness directly, the yogi achieves meaning clear light or the “ ultimate clear light .” Of the six branches, he achieves the branch of “ apprehension .” 
 “The subsequent mindfulness of reverse order”: The reverse order here refers to the mind of near- attainment of the reverse order. Concurrent with the mind of near -attainment of the 
reverse order, after arising from the meaning clear light, one achieves the pure illusory body and at the same time, one achieves the abandonment union. Once the pure illusory body is achieved, the continuum of that pure illusory body will go on forever until samsara is emptied.  
 
 
Lesson  50          Page 339 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
After achieving the pure illusory body, the yogi then manifests billions of bodies going out into 
the ten directions to make offerings and so -forth in order to accumulate the merit needed to 
be able to abandon the obstructions to omniscience (knowledge obstructions).   There comes the time when the yogi achieves sufficient merit and is able to abandon the knowledge obstructions and achieve the realisational union which is able to overcome the knowledge obs tructions. Once the knowledge obstructions are abandoned, the yogi achieves 
the non- learner’s union . 
 As mentioned earlier, the three conducts -  elaborate, unelaborate, and highly unelaborated -  
help to enhance one’s practice of the completion stage and re fer to the conduct of enjoying 
the five desire objects and the ways of relying on the consort.  
• The elaborate and unelaborated conducts are performed in relation to the action seal, an actual consort.  
• The highly unelaborated conduct is performed in relati on to a visualised consort. There is 
also a particular diet to be adopted and also the practice of eating very rich foods that induce deep sleep so as to sustain the practice of sleep yoga. Besides performing the sleep yoga, the practitioner will not be do ing anything else except eat, sleep and defecate. That 
practitioner is focussing mainly on sustaining the clear light of sleep.  
 All the practices mentioned so far up to here are practices to be done for one’s whole life. What if one is not able to achiev e the example clear light of the final mental isolation and 
illusory body in this life? How does one continue to practise in future lives? The next verse deals with this in the form of prayers that one can make:  
 26 
If, at the time of death I have not ach ieved the meditative stabilisation  
Which accomplishes the supreme attainment in this life or the intermediate state,  
May I be able to mix the four empties of the stages of death  
At the time of the basis with the four empties of the path.  
 27 
At the time of the intermediate state, may I mix it with  
The illusory meditative stabilisation, and at the time of taking birth  
May I take rebirth at will in a supreme place of birth,  
Just as the Enjoyment body sustains the birth of the emanation body.  
 28 
In short whether it is death, intermediate state or birth that dawns  
Having realised it as an exhortation to virtuous practice,  
A reminder of the three types of bringing into the path cultivated previously,  
May my mind overflow with joy at the time of death.  
 Let’s say  one is unable to actualise the illusory body that will allow one to achieve 
enlightenment in this life or one is unable to achieve the example clear light of the final mental isolation at the time of death which will allow one to achieve enlightenment in the 
 
Lesson  50          Page 340 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
intermediate state.  What is one to do?  
 
If that is the case, then:  
• at the time of death, one mixes the four empties that occur at that time together with the 
four empties of the path.  
• during the intermediate state, one mixes the intermediate state w ith “ the illusory 
meditative stabilisation .”  
• at the time of taking birth, one mixes taking rebirth with the emanation body.  
 
In short, whatever arises be it death, bardo or birth, “ having realized it as an exhortation to 
virtuous practice,” one sees it a s “a reminder of the three types of bringing into path cultivated 
previously.”   The point is that through the Tantric practice of bringing the three bodies into the 
path and the nine mixings, one develops that confidence which arises at the time of death. With such practices and confidence, one is able to die joyfully and in the next life, one again attains a suitable body for the practice of Tantra. Then again in that life one trains in the two stages. That is how one gains enlightenment in successive live s.  
 29 
Thus, may the culmination of this marvellous path  
Be accomplished accordingly without obstacles  
May it spread and expand throughout all directions and times  
And may all migrating beings be liberated by this skilful means.  
 
May the practices of this marvellous path, the path of these two stages –  the generation stage 
and five levels of the completion stage –  “be accomplished accordingly without obstacles ”  
arising even for a single moment. May all the excellent explanations of the teachings on th is 
marvellous path “ spread and expand in all directions and times .” May all fortunate disciples be 
liberated from the problems of both samsara and nirvana by this skilful method.  
 
It is good to read this prayer sometimes and to think about it now that we have gone through it. 
Even though we may not understand everything, we do have some understanding. Even if we 
are not able to do these practices (now), we can pray to be able to do so using this prayer.  
 
We can now recite this prayer as a way of concluding  the six years we have spent on the Basic 
Program to dedicate the merit that we have accumulated.  
 
There will be sessions in the morning and afternoon this Sunday. It will be good if you can 
come. We will start at 10am. I am not sure what I am going to talk about this Sunday.

and the vajra lineage lower. Why is the tathagata lineage considered superior (the highest)?

intermediate state and birth?

 CLARIFICATION BY GESHE  CHONYI OF DISCUSSION QUESTIONS 4 OF 29
TH MARCH 2009  
 
Question 4: What is the difference between Yoga with Signs and Yoga without Signs?  
The non -dual yoga of profundity and clarity exists in both Yoga with Signs and Yoga without 
Signs.  
 
For Yoga with Signs, the main object of practice is the meditation on the deity’s body and the 
repetition of the mantra. The main emphasis of Yoga with Sig ns is the class of appearance.  
 For Yoga without Signs, there are the practices of emptiness and deity yoga but the emphasis is on the class of empty, i.e., the meditation on emptiness.  
 
According to this author (Ngawang Palden), the non -dual yoga of profu ndity and clarity is the 
Yoga without Signs. The emphasis here is on the meditation on emptiness. But the question to 
think about is this: Is only Yoga without Signs the non -dual yoga of profundity and clarity? Can 
there be a non -dual yoga of profundity an d clarity that is the Yoga with Signs?  
 
 
Lesson  50          Page 342 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Question: What is the meaning of the word “signs” in this context?  
Answer : The word ‘sign’ is ‘ chema’ in Tibetan. It is translated sometimes as symbol but it has 
the connotation of an appearing object. Perhaps, we ca n relate ‘sign’ here to mean dualistic 
elaborations, i.e. it relates to the class of appearance. When we talk about emptiness, it is like 
nothing is appearing so to speak.   
 Question: What is the meaning of taking the essence?  
I guess, in this context, ta king the essence means actualising the two yogas of   Yoga with Signs 
and Yoga without Signs.   Here, in this context, the purpose of entering the mandala is to be able to receive the initiations so that one can practice these two yogas and cultivate and a ctualise the path:  
• After entering the mandala, one receives the water initiation that authorises one to practise the Yoga without Signs.  
• Then by receiving the crown initiation, one is authorised to practise the Yoga with Signs.  
 The path is summarised into these two yogas: the Yoga with Signs and Yoga without Signs. Of these two, the real essence is the Yoga without Signs because that yoga serves as the direct antidote for severing the root of samsara i.e., it is the direct antidote to the afflictions.  
 Question: Can taking the essence be related to receiving the blessings of the lama?  
Answer:  One can explain taking the essence in so many other different ways. For example 
when we talk about what is the meaning of life, what is its essence and how one can take the essence of this rebirth, then practising the path that is shared with the person o f small capacity 
is one way of taking the essence.  
 
Practising the path that is shared with the person of middling capacity is another way of taking 
the essence.  
 Practising the path that is shared with the person of great capacity is yet another way of taking 
the essence.  
 Here, in this context of entering the mandala, there are many reasons why we are doing so. When you look at the ultimate essence, the whole point of entering the mandala is to be able to receive the initiation. What does one do with that initiation? So that one can actualise the path.  
  Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme  
Transcribed by Phuah Soon Ek and Vivien Ng  
Edited by Cecilia Tsong  
Checked by Yap Siew Kee  
 
 
CLARIFICATION BY GESHE  CHONYI OF DISCUSSION QUESTIONS 3 & 4 OF 19TH JULY 200 9  
 
 
Lesson  50          Page 343 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
Question 3: Who is the ‘I’ that meditates on the deity?  
 
The practice of the sadhana is called self -generation. If there is no I, then what generates into 
the deity?  
 Geshe -la: ‘I’ and the merely labelled ‘I’ are exactly the same or not?  ‘I’ must exis t.  
 
Whatever exists is necessarily merely labelled. If it is not merely labelled, it definitely doesn’t 
exist.  
 Geshe -la: Whatever exists, and then it is merely labelled.  
 In relation with the 'I' that is generated as the deity, is it correct to think tha t I am Vajradhara?  
 Geshe -la: Self generation is all about that. I am Vajradhara. Is it correct to do that? Is the 
thought of oneself as Vajradhara during meditation right or wrong? That is why I am asking, who is that 'I'?   When you hold on to divine pri de of yourself as deity of Vajradhara, is that mind a wrong 
consciousness? I am not Vajradhara but I believe myself to be Vajradhara and generate divine pride. Is that correct? You are not Vajradhara but you think yourself as Vajradhara. Is that a 
wrong co nsciousness?  
 
Of the seven types of consciousnesses that we studied in the presentation of the mind, the last 
consciousness is wrong consciousness. A wrong consciousness is something that has to be abandoned and is not to be adopted. We are not necessarily  saying this is not like that.  
 Geshe -la: This is a question for you to think about. We do a lot of such things. We visualise the 
whole sky filled with flowers but there are no flowers in the sky. I am not going to say whether it is right or wrong. But why  do we do these things?   
 Question 4: The objects of abandonment of the generation stage are ordinary appearance and ordinary conception. What are they?  
 Ordinary concept as you have presented it is grasping at phenomena as ultimately truly 
existent, it is  the mind conceiving the ordinary as truly existent.  Is it all right to say that? If that 
is the case, is grasping at the deity’s body as being truly existent an object of abandonment of 
Tantra?  
 Do you agree with that? Is it more than that or not? Is it  not that but something else?  
There are only two answers, either it is not that or it is something more than that. When we say, “There is something more than that,” that means that is also correct but there is something else.  
 
• There is the grasping at sentient beings as being truly existent.  
• There is also the grasping at sentient beings as being ordinary.  
 
Lesson  50          Page 344 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
Is the appearance of sentient beings as truly existent an object of abandonment of Tantra or not?    When you say ordinary appearance is the appearance of sentient beings as being truly existent 
and ordinary concept is the grasping at sentient beings to be truly existent and you posit them to be the objects of abandonment of Tantra, then the question arises, “Can those two be 
abandoned by the Sutric path? ” If they can be abandoned on the Sutric path, they are not the 
uncommon objects of abandonment of Tantra. Obviously, ordinary appearance and ordinary 
grasping must be something else.  
 If one takes ordinary appearance to mean a sentient being (who is ordi nary) appearing as a 
sentient being and takes ordinary concept to mean apprehending a sentient being just to be a sentient being, is that okay or not?  
Because when you qualify these two with true existence, they are no longer the uncommon objects of abandonment of Tantra.   Here in Highest Yoga Tantra, the purpose of the generation and completion stages are to stop ordinary appearance as ordinary, i.e., to stop sentient beings appearing as sentient beings and ordinary concept, i.e., to stop the idea that s entient beings are sentient beings.  
 
The ways to do this are:  
• through having the stable clear appearance of oneself as the deity. That stops ordinary 
appearance and  
• one develops the divine pride (identifying oneself as the deity) which stops the idea of sentient beings as sentient beings.  
 In the generation and completion stages of Highest Yoga Tantra, it is about seeing oneself and identifying oneself as the deity and seeing the environment and identifying the environment as being the inestimable mansion of the deity. Why? The purpose is to  stop this appearance of the 
ordinary as ordinary and to stop the concept of ordinary as ordinary.  
 Here when we say the objects of abandonment of the generation stage is ordinary appearance and grasping, in this context, “abandonment” takes on the connot ation of developing a certain 
mind to stop those two from arising. But you are not eliminating them. Therefore, the meaning of abandonment on the generation stage is stopping the appearance and the concept from arising.  
 Is there a need to qualify ordinar y appearance and ordinary grasping with true existence or not? 
It is not necessary because sentient beings appearing as sentient beings is enough. That is 
ordinary appearance. Whether it is truly existent or not, it is still a sentient being.  
 
Do you unde rstand why there is no need to qualify these two (ordinary appearance and 
ordinary grasping) with true existence? You need to understand why because when you affix those two with true existence, then that no longer makes them uncommon objects of abandonmen t of Tantra. That can be achieved with the Sutric path. That being the case, there 
 
Lesson  50          Page 345 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
is really no need to qualify ordinary appearance and ordinary grasping with true existence.  
 
We can simply say:  
• ordinary appearance is sentient beings appearing as sentient  beings and  
• ordinary concept is apprehending sentient beings to be sentient beings  
 Geshe -la: The ordinary environment is also included there. You must know the problem when 
you put in true existence. The consequence is already explained -  Sutra can abandon that, no 
need for Tantra.  
 Then what happens after you have achieved enlightenment? Does ordinary appearance and ordinary grasping exist?  
 Geshe -la: What do you think?  
 Does the Buddha see suffering sentient beings? When we say we abandonordinary a ppearance 
and ordinary concept, we are abandoning that mind to which the appearance appears. When we say abandoning the ordinary (appearance), this doesn’t mean to get rid of it as in if I don't want you to appear, I get rid of you! That is not the meaning  of abandon.  
 When you achieve enlightenment, you will necessarily have abandoned ordinary appearance and grasping. So does the Buddha see things as ordinary? Does the Buddha  believe them to be ordinary?  
 
I don't know. This is just doubt. Whether Buddha see ordinary things or not?  These are the objects of abandonment and the Buddha will have necessarily abandoned them. Then the 
question is, “If the Buddha has abandoned them, does he have ordinary appearance and 
grasping?”  That is the difficult point.  
    Interpreter: Ven. Tenzin Gyurme

Lesson  50          Page 348 
 
This transcript is restricted to those who have received a Highest Yoga Tantra empowerment  
Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
 
1 When the wisdom that directly perceives emptiness becomes the direct antidote to the innate afflictions, the 
bodhisattva achieves the uninterrupted path of the path of meditation. “B” in the chart refers to the innate 
afflictive obstructions which are aba ndoned from the first to the seventh grounds.  
There are seven uninterrupted paths of the path of meditation. Since there are seven uninterrupted paths of 
the path of meditation, then there are seven levels of the innate afflictive obstructions that are to be abandoned. In fact, there are nine levels of the innate afflictive obstructions to be abandoned on the path of meditation: - 
1. three big innate afflictive obstructions  
2. three middling innate afflictive obstructions  
3. three small innate afflictive obstructions  
The three big innate afflictive obstructions and the three middling innate afflictive obstructions are abandoned 
sequentially. However, the three small innate afflictive obstructions are abandoned simultaneously. Therefore, 
this makes up seven levels of i nnate afflictive obstructions. As the uninterrupted paths of the path of meditation 
are the direct antidotes to the innate afflictive obstructions, there are seven uninterrupted paths of the path of 
meditation to abandon all seven levels of the innate affl ictive obstructions.  
When a particular uninterrupted path of the path of meditation has abandoned its innate afflictive 
obstructions, the bodhisattva achieves the respective path of release of the path of meditation . Therefore, there 
are also seven paths of release of the path of meditation.  
After achieving a specific path of release, the bodhisattva will arise from meditative equipoise and enter into 
subsequent attainment (post -meditative equipoise). He then enters again into meditative equipoise to abandon 
the next level of innate afflictive obstructions. (Extract from Pg. 4, Lesson 2 of Basic Program class on  Mahayana Paths and Grounds ) 
 
2 After the seventh uninterrupted path of the path of meditation, the bodhisattva enters the path of release of 
path of meditation at the eighth ground having completely abandoned the innate afflictive obstructions. The 
bodhisattva then becomes a foe destro yer (the eighth milestone in the chart) which means that he has completely 
destroyed all the foes, i.e. the innate afflictive obstructions.  
At this point, the bodhisattva begins to overcome the knowledge obstructions which are abandoned by the 
uninterrupt ed paths of the eighth, ninth and tenth grounds which are the direct antidotes to the knowledge 
obstructions.  
There are three levels of knowledge obstructions - 
1. the big knowledge obstructions  
2. the middling knowledge obstructions  
3. the small knowledge obstructi ons 
The big knowledge obstructions are abandoned by the uninterrupted paths of the eighth ground. The middling 
knowledge obstructions are abandoned by the uninterrupted path of the ninth ground. The small knowledge obstructions are abandoned by the uninter rupted path of the tenth ground. The uninterrupted path of the last 
moment of the continuum of the sentient being is the direct antidote to the small knowledge obstructions and 
after they have been abandoned, the bodhisattva becomes a buddha. (Extract from  Pg. 5, Lesson 2 of Basic 
Program class on Mahayana Paths and Grounds ) 
 
3 Definition of an enjoyment body : It is a final form body possessing the five certainties:  
1. Certainty of abode: it abides only in Akanista  
2. Certainty of body: it is adorned with comple te manifest signs and exemplifications (or the major marks and 
minor signs)  
3. Certainty of retinue: it is surrounded only by bodhisattva superiors  
4. Certainty of doctrine: it teaches only the Mahayana Dharma  
5. Certainty of time: it remains as long as samsara is not empty (Extract from Pg. 6, Lesson 9, Module 11 of Basic 
Program, Ornament for Clear Realisations )

Eight Dissolutions  Internal signs

Amitabha Buddhist Centre                    Grounds and Paths of Secret Mantra 
FPMT Basic Program       Transcript of the Oral Commentary by Geshe Chonyi  
 
1. Earth element dissolves into the water element  Appearance of mirage  
2. Water element dissolves into the fire element  Appearance of smoke  
3. Fire element dissolves into the wind element  Appearance of fireflies or sparks within 
smoke  
4.Wind element dissolves into consciousness  Appearance of a sputtering butter -lamp 
going  out 
5. 80 conceptions dissolve into the white appearance  At first, burning butter lamp, then clear 
vacuity filled with white light  
6. White appearance dissolves into the red increase  Clear vacuity filled with red light  
7. Red increase dissolves into the black near - attainment  At first, vacuity filled with thick darkness; 
then, as if swooning unconsciously  
8. Black near -attainment dissolves into the clear light  Very clear vacuity free of the white, red and 
black appearance; the mind of the clear light 
of death  
Extract from ‘Part 6: An overview of Tantric Paths and Grounds’ by Geshe Tashi Tsering
